cards.name: Taking the Stage: 50 Years Ago at the Mayer
cards.blurb: Check out Taking the Stage at SCU's Mayer Theatre, a look back at 50 years of performances. Free admission makes it an easy morning add.
cards.why: It's free, it's interesting, and it's a good way to spend a rainy-ish morning indoors.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Walk over to the State of Illusion Exhibition while you're already on the SCU campus. Free contemporary art with some genuinely cool perceptual tricks.
cards.why: You're already there and it's free, so why not.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Quick stop at SaiGon BreadFast in Mountain View for a banh mi or Vietnamese coffee before your next block. Fast, cheap, and worth it.
cards.why: Perfect quick bite to bridge the afternoon without a full sit-down meal.

cards.name: Voyagers Book Club Grades 3 and 4
cards.blurb: Voyagers Book Club will feature a book just for currently enrolled 3rd and 4th graders. Please come to the Children's Reference Desk at Cupertino Library to sign up and get a book. Registration is…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Homework Help for Grades K-6
cards.blurb: Head to the Cupertino library for Homework Help for Grades K-6. Wait, this one's not for you two. Skip this and keep reading.
cards.why: Actually flagged as a mismatch for adults without kids — dropping this.

cards.name: OMESCAPE | Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1135 E Arques Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at OMESCAPE in Sunnyvale and work through one of their escape rooms together. They have multiple themes and the puzzles are genuinely tricky.
cards.why: A rainy Friday afternoon is exactly when you want to be locked in a room solving puzzles.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 10660 S De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at Yiassoo in Cupertino with Greek food and a relaxed dinner vibe. Try the mezze plates and take your time.
cards.why: A chill dinner spot to end a full Friday on a good note.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown, pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo and grab a coffee while wandering Jackson St through one of only three surviving Japantowns in the country.
cards.why: It's a genuinely cool way to ease into the day without rushing.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library for Fists of Fury, a free educational event that's worth a couple hours of your morning.
cards.why: It's free, it's today, and it's a solid way to spend a rainy-ish morning indoors.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge on Lincoln Ave, pick out a piece and paint it at your own pace while the rain does whatever it wants outside.
cards.why: It's the kind of chill, hands-on afternoon activity that's way more fun than it sounds.

cards.name: TrioSpa - Massage, Facials & Waxing / Trio Wellness Mgmt
cards.address: 1585 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage or facial at TrioSpa on The Alameda so you're totally relaxed before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: You've got a late night ahead, so banking some rest now is a smart move.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row, browse the boutiques, and grab dinner at Straits or Sino before heading out for the night.
cards.why: It's the right vibe for a Friday evening and dinner here sets up the jazz show perfectly.

cards.name: Huu Bac Quintet — San Jose Jazz
cards.address: 310 South First St, San Jose
cards.blurb: The Huu Bac Quintet blends Vietnamese, Chinese and Andean traditions with contemporary jazz, creating a refined sound world where heritage meets innovation. Led by Montreal-based composer and multi…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 681 Blossom Hill Rd #102, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Philz with a pour-to-order coffee and settle in at a table to ease into the day.
cards.why: A rainy morning calls for a proper coffee stop, not a drive-through.

cards.name: Development Review Committee
cards.blurb: Sit in on the Development Review Committee meeting and see what projects are shaping up in Los Gatos.
cards.why: Free, local, and genuinely interesting if you care about what gets built in town.

cards.name: Antoine’s Cookie Shop Los Gatos
cards.address: 35 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Antoine's and grab a cookie or two — they rotate flavors and everything is made fresh.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you walk past once and then can't stop thinking about.

cards.name: Los Gatos Library
cards.address: 100 Villa Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the Los Gatos Library for a couple of hours, a great way to spend a gray afternoon with good books and zero obligation.
cards.why: The building itself is worth the visit, and rainy days are what libraries were made for.

cards.name: Heritage Pub and Chop House
cards.address: 15 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into Heritage Pub and Chop House for a proper lunch, order something hearty and grab a pint while you're at it.
cards.why: Good food, good beer, and you're already on Santa Cruz Ave.

cards.name: CineLux Los Gatos Theatre
cards.address: 43 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Attend the Arts and Culture Commission Meeting at CineLux Los Gatos Theatre and hear what's coming to the local arts scene.
cards.why: It's a chance to actually have a say in what happens culturally in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Arts and Culture Commission Meeting
cards.blurb: Head to Arts and Culture Commission Meeting and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: West Valley Housing Resource Fair
cards.blurb: Head over to the West Valley Housing Resource Fair to see what's happening with local housing and connect with community resources.
cards.why: Free, relevant, and a good way to stay plugged into what's shifting in the area.

cards.name: Gamers Heaven Bay Area - Cafe
cards.address: 1171 Homestead Rd #190, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this cozy gaming cafe in Santa Clara — grab a coffee and settle into a game or two before the day really kicks off.
cards.why: It's a chill, indoor spot that's perfect for a rainy-ish Friday morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the Triton's galleries and the sculpture garden out back. The contemporary exhibits rotate often and the outdoor space is worth a slow loop even on a cloudy day.
cards.why: Free art, free admission, and the sculpture garden is genuinely gorgeous in the morning light.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Head over to catch the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU. It's a free show worth taking your time with — these kinds of immersive art installations are rare and the details reward a slow look.
cards.why: It's free and it's right in Santa Clara, so it fits the afternoon perfectly.

cards.name: Mio Vicino
cards.address: 1290 Benton St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Mio Vicino on Benton St. It's an Italian neighborhood spot with solid pasta and a relaxed vibe. Order something hearty and take your time.
cards.why: You need a proper sit-down lunch before the evening, and this place delivers.

cards.name: Daydream Holistic
cards.address: 2118 El Camino Real Studio 23, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Daydream Holistic for a massage or wellness treatment before the evening. It's a small studio on El Camino Real with a genuinely calming vibe — book ahead if you can.
cards.why: A pre-evening wind-down session makes the whole night feel better.

cards.name: 5PM Student Mass
cards.blurb: Sunday 5PM Mass at Mission Santa Clara
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Hapa's Brewing Company
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #90, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Hapa's Brewing Company for dinner and drinks. The food is solid and the beer list gives you plenty to work through on a Friday night.
cards.why: It's a laid-back, adults-first kind of place that's exactly right for ending the week.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 344 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at ZombieRunner Coffee on California Ave — grab a coffee and settle in for a bit before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good coffee and a chill vibe is exactly how you want to ease into a rainy Friday morning.

cards.name: Highlights Tour | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into the Highlights Tour at the Anderson Collection on the Stanford campus and get a guided look at some seriously impressive American modern and contemporary art.
cards.why: It's free, it's indoors, and it's a genuinely great way to spend a drizzly morning.

cards.name: Evvia Estiatorio
cards.address: 420 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Evvia Estiatorio for lunch — it's a Greek spot on Emerson with a warm, taverna-style feel. Go for the grilled meats or whatever the kitchen is running today.
cards.why: It's a step up from your average lunch spot and absolutely worth it on a slower Friday.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Computer History Museum for an hour before you have to head out. Even a partial visit to the Revolution exhibit is worth it.
cards.why: The history in this place is genuinely wild and an hour barely scratches the surface.

cards.name: 2026 Fellow Awards Ceremony
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Stick around for the 2026 Fellow Awards Ceremony at the Computer History Museum — it's a chance to hear from people doing interesting work in computing.
cards.why: You're already there and it's a cool way to see who's shaping the next chapter of tech history.

cards.name: Page Turners Book Club for Grades 4-5
cards.blurb: Welcome to the Page Turners Book Club for Grades 4-5 ! This month we are reading The City of Ember by Jeanne DuPrau . We will meet on Wednesday, April 15th, 2026, from 4 pm – 5 pm at the Los Altos…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Uncommon Threads
cards.address: 164 Main St #105, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Uncommon Threads on Main Street in Los Altos for some browsing before dinner. It's a fun clothing boutique worth a look.
cards.why: It's a great local shop and you're heading back to Los Altos anyway.

cards.name: Cosmic Coffee Company
cards.address: 1920 Lafayette St STE G, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a coffee at this well-loved local spot before the day gets going.
cards.why: A 5-star coffee shop is exactly how you want to kick off a rainy Friday.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Walk through the Patient No More Exhibition at King Library — free art on a rainy morning is a no-brainer.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and genuinely interesting when the weather isn't cooperating.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art, including the sculpture garden out back if the rain holds off.
cards.why: Free contemporary art and a legit sculpture garden that most locals still haven't discovered.

cards.name: Henry's World Famous Hi-Life
cards.address: 301 Sharks Wy, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Henry's World Famous Hi-Life for dinner. Order the tri-tip or whatever the bartender recommends — this place has been doing it right for decades.
cards.why: A San Jose classic that earns its name, solid spot to fuel up before a late night out.

cards.name: The Emo Night Tour
cards.address: 400 S 1st St
cards.blurb: The Emo Night Tour is coming to San Jose on April 17th!*21+.....doors/show start time 8:00pm*The greatest tribute night to the music that was never just a phase (including this event, which has been…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Dakao Sandwiches II
cards.address: 72 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a banh mi from Dakao Sandwiches II — quick, cheap, and absolutely hits the spot for breakfast on the go.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a Friday is with something delicious and low-key.

cards.name: The Hot Spot Studios Milpitas / Bay Area
cards.address: 30 S Abbott Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by The Hot Spot Studios and check out what's on — it's a creative, intimate studio space that's worth a walk-through before your midday plans pick up.
cards.why: Perfect way to fill a morning hour with something a little different.

cards.name: Lil' Athletes for Ages 18 Months - 4 Years
cards.blurb: Stories, stretches, and sports-themed activities for tots on the move! Parents/caregivers must stay and participate with their children. This program is for children between 18 months and 4 years…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: International Film Festival
cards.blurb: Head to the International Film Festival and catch a screening or two — free admission, so just show up and see what's playing.
cards.why: A free film festival on a rainy Friday afternoon is basically a gift.

cards.name: Naans & Curries
cards.address: 102 Great Mall Dr Suite 102, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Naans & Curries and go deep on the menu — get a curry, some naan, and take your time with it.
cards.why: One of the best spots in Milpitas and you're already in the area.

cards.name: TD Beauty Spa
cards.address: 56 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at TD Beauty Spa and let the afternoon slow down a bit with a massage or treatment before they close up.
cards.why: Friday afternoon and a spa visit just make sense together.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Head to EDscapade Games and book an escape room with your crew — they have solid themed rooms and the whole thing is a blast.
cards.why: Great way to shake off the spa vibes and get into something competitive and fun.

cards.name: Saucy Asian - Milpitas
cards.address: Food Court, 447 Great Mall Dr Space 10, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at Saucy Asian in the Great Mall food court — get the wings or whatever's saucy and share a bunch of stuff.
cards.why: Low-key, affordable, and consistently good for a casual Friday dinner.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the creek trail. It's shaded, easy, and a genuinely peaceful way to ease into the day before the rain rolls in.
cards.why: Best window to get outside is right now before the afternoon clouds up.

cards.name: Oasis Thai Spa
cards.address: 2203 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Oasis Thai Spa in Campbell and decompress for a couple hours. A rainy Friday afternoon is basically made for this.
cards.why: There's no better use of a slow, drizzly Friday afternoon.

cards.name: Olalá Colombian ice cream
cards.address: 475 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stop by Olalá for some Colombian ice cream before the evening kicks off. It's a fun, low-key treat and they're open until 9.
cards.why: A little sweet stop hits different after a spa afternoon.

cards.name: lelé kitchen
cards.address: 14180 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at lelé kitchen in Los Gatos. Try whatever's on the menu tonight and settle into the warm vibe. It's the kind of place that actually feels like a Friday night.
cards.why: It earns its reputation as one of the better dinner spots in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Civic Improvement Commission Special Meeting -Thursday, April 23, 2026
cards.blurb: Event date: April 23, 2026 Event Time: 06:00 PM - 07:00 PM Location: Campbell, CA 95008
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Walk over to King Library and take in the Patient No More Exhibition, a thought-provoking art show worth a slow stroll through.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and exactly the kind of thing to do when rain is threatening.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara for the contemporary galleries and the sculpture garden if it stays dry enough to step outside.
cards.why: Free world-class art and a genuinely peaceful afternoon vibe that most people completely sleep on.

cards.name: Dulcinea Panaderia Oaxaquena
cards.address: 795 S Second St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Dulcinea Panaderia Oaxaquena and grab a couple of pastries and something savory to hold you over before dinner.
cards.why: Oaxacan baked goods are not something you pass up, and the price makes it a no-brainer mid-afternoon stop.

cards.name: CineLux Almaden Café & Lounge
cards.address: 2306 Almaden Rd, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Catch a matinee at CineLux Almaden Café and Lounge, where you can actually order food and drinks at your seat.
cards.why: A rainy afternoon with 60% chance of rain is basically a built-in excuse to spend two hours in a good theater.

cards.name: City Council Regular Meeting
cards.blurb: Event date: April 21, 2026 Event Time: 07:00 PM - 11:59 PM Location: 70 North First Street Campbell, CA 95008
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 101 Forest Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Philz with one of their custom-blended pour-overs — ask for a Mint Mojito iced or a Tesora and take it slow before the day picks up.
cards.why: A solid coffee stop to ease into the day before things get busy.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander the free galleries at Cantor Arts Center and spend time outside with the Rodin sculpture garden. It's one of the better free museum experiences in the Bay Area, indoors and out.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a Friday morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Pop into this Gallery Conversation at the Anderson Collection — today it's focused on Jeremy Frey's woven works, and the guided format makes it actually interesting even if you don't know the artist.
cards.why: It's free, it's quick, and it's the kind of thing you'd never plan but always enjoy.

cards.name: Manresa Bread
cards.address: 271 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Grab something to go at Manresa Bread in Los Altos — their pastries and sandwiches are serious, and the breads are some of the best in the South Bay.
cards.why: Quick enough to grab before your afternoon commitment and genuinely worth stopping for.

cards.name: Palo Alto Earth Day Festival
cards.blurb: Save the date to join us for a day of fun celebrating sustainability and learning about what you can do now to help save the planet. Learn about available resources for Palo Alto residents to make an…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Avy Spa Massage
cards.address: 3783 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Avy Spa on El Camino and decompress after a full day on your feet. They're open late so no rush getting there.
cards.why: Perfect way to wind down after hours of walking around.

cards.name: Chef Chu's
cards.address: 1067 N San Antonio Rd, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Chef Chu's, a Los Altos institution for Chinese-American classics. Order family-style if you can and try the honey walnut prawns.
cards.why: This place has been around forever and still delivers every time.

cards.name: Barefoot Coffee
cards.address: 1819 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a proper coffee and something to eat at this beloved Campbell cafe before the day gets rolling.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything better and this place is worth the trip.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through actual Egyptian mummies, ancient artifacts, and hieroglyphics in a museum that somehow exists in San Jose — give yourself the full two hours because there's more to see than you'd expect.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the most surprising places in the South Bay and a rainy morning is perfect for an indoor museum.

cards.name: All Natural Day Spa
cards.address: 1042 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at All Natural Day Spa and actually decompress for a couple hours before the evening picks back up.
cards.why: A Friday afternoon spa slot is the right move before a late jazz night.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House for a proper dinner — the sukiyaki format means dinner is an event in itself, so take your time and enjoy the meal before heading out.
cards.why: You want a solid dinner before a 7 PM jazz set and this one delivers.

cards.name: SJZ Break Room Jazz Jam Ft. Martín Perna
cards.address: 310 South First St, San Jose
cards.blurb: San Jose Jazz presents free live music programming in conjunction with South First Friday. Following sets by our SJZ U19s and the Michael Webster Quartet, our all-ages jazz jam features special gue…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 118 Paseo de San Antonio, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a hand-crafted pour at Philz — their blended coffee is unlike anything you'd get at a chain, so take your time, grab a seat, and ease into the morning.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a big night is with a proper morning coffee ritual.

cards.name: Student Art Showcase at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through King Library and check out the Student Art Showcase. It's a rotating mix of student work across mediums, free to walk through, and worth more than a quick glance.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a solid way to spend a rainy morning indoors.

cards.name: Shahi Darbar Indian Cuisine
cards.address: 2155 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Shahi Darbar on El Camino Real. Order the curries, get the naan, and don't rush. This is real-deal Indian food worth slowing down for.
cards.why: You're already in Santa Clara and this place is consistently outstanding.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Head over to Santa Clara for the State of Illusion Exhibition. It's a visually striking show designed to mess with your perception of space and reality. Go slow and let it do its thing.
cards.why: Free art that actually makes you think is a rare combo and this one delivers.

cards.name: Massage Envy
cards.address: 1316 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Massage Envy to decompress before the evening. Even a 60-minute session will have you feeling completely reset before the show tonight.
cards.why: Rain in the afternoon, a massage on the table. Hard to argue with that.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row for a pre-show dinner. Grab a table at Straits or Sino, order something substantial, and take your time on the patio if the rain holds off.
cards.why: Good food and a relaxed vibe is exactly what you want before a big concert night.

cards.name: The Weeknd Night
cards.address: 400 S 1st St
cards.blurb: This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 681 Blossom Hill Rd #102, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Philz with a custom-blended pour-over and take your time with it — this is a proper sit-down coffee spot, not a drive-through.
cards.why: A rainy Friday morning is exactly when you want a slow, good cup of coffee.

cards.name: ShirSpa
cards.address: 15868 Cherry Blossom Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at ShirSpa and actually decompress. Perfect for a morning when the weather nudges you indoors anyway.
cards.why: A 5-star spa in Los Gatos on a drizzly day is a no-brainer.

cards.name: Ritual at Manresa
cards.address: 320 Village Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Ritual at Manresa is a high-end lunch worth lingering over — order whatever the kitchen is pushing today and let the meal breathe.
cards.why: This is a splurge spot, and a rainy Friday afternoon is exactly the right time for it.

cards.name: Art Beat Studio
cards.address: 68 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Art Beat Studio in Campbell and see what's on the walls today — it's a creative, low-key way to spend an afternoon hour.
cards.why: Good art and bad weather go together surprisingly well.

cards.name: Planning Commission Meeting
cards.blurb: Grab dinner before the evening kicks off at Stackers Pizzeria in Saratoga. The pizza is legit and the vibe is casual enough for a quick pre-event meal.
cards.why: You'll want something solid in you before a two-hour community workshop.

cards.name: Butter Cheeni
cards.address: 4360 Bloomfield Dr, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Actually, fuel up at Stackers Pizzeria before heading out for the evening.
cards.why: Great pizza without the fuss.

cards.name: Stackers Pizzeria
cards.address: 18572 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a quick dinner at Stackers Pizzeria in Saratoga before the workshop — grab a slice or a whole pie and settle in.
cards.why: Solid, satisfying, and done in time to get to the workshop comfortably.

cards.name: Wildfire Preparedness Workshop
cards.address: Redwood Estates, Los Gatos
cards.blurb: Santa Clara County Fire Department community safety program. Registration required.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: The Patient No More Exhibition is free and open this morning at King Library. Walk through it before the baseball crowd picks up and you'll have the place practically to yourself.
cards.why: Free art on a rainy Friday morning is a perfect opener before the game.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Ontario Tower Buzzers
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: San Jose Giants home game vs. Ontario Tower Buzzers at Excite Ballpark.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Fox Tale Fermentation Project
cards.address: 30 E Santa Clara St #120, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Post-game, head to Fox Tale Fermentation Project for a pour of something interesting. They do natural wines and fermented drinks in a relaxed, no-fuss setting on Santa Clara St.
cards.why: Best afternoon drink spot in downtown San Jose, full stop.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum has actual mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and an entire recreated tomb you can walk through. It's weird and wonderful in the best possible way.
cards.why: You'll walk in skeptical and walk out genuinely impressed every time.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Santana Row is the move for dinner. Stroll the boulevard, pick a patio at Straits, Sino, or Zinnia, and settle in for a proper sit-down dinner with a drink.
cards.why: It's the kind of Friday night spot that earns its reputation.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Start the morning at King Library with the Patient No More Exhibition — it's already open and worth a slow walk-through before the day gets busy.
cards.why: Free art on a rainy Friday morning is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into the Triton Museum of Art and wander the sculpture garden and contemporary galleries before your meeting — it's a genuinely great free museum that most people sleep on.
cards.why: Free world-class art and the outdoor sculpture garden is still worth a stroll even with the cloud cover.

cards.name: Tax Task Force Monthly Meeting
cards.blurb: Meeting of members to address policy and programmatic work of SVLGs tax efforts. Please contact Dan Kostenbauder at dkostenbauder@svlg.org for more information.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library

cards.blurb: Swing by Mediterranean Wraps on California Ave for a quick, satisfying bite before your midday commitment. The wraps are fresh, filling, and won't weigh you down.
cards.why: Good food, easy timing, sets you up well for the rest of the morning.

cards.name: Los Altos Library
cards.address: 13 S San Antonio Rd, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Sprouting Stories is the locked item here. The Los Altos Library is a great little spot to browse the stacks or grab a seat while you're in the neighborhood.
cards.why: You're already here, so make the most of it.

cards.name: Sprouting Stories
cards.blurb: Hi friends! Join us for our fun Sprouting Stories, a special storytime filled with stories, songs, and fingerplays that spark giggles, wiggles, and wonder! Perfect for children 0-5 and families. This…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio
cards.address: 632 Fremont Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Halo Pottery in Los Altos and get your hands in some clay. It's a working studio and a genuinely fun way to spend a drizzly afternoon.
cards.why: Rainy days and pottery studios were made for each other.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum has floor after floor of wild tech history. Even if you're not a gear head, the stories here are genuinely fascinating.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Books Inc. on Castro Street is a great independent bookstore for browsing after the museum. Pick up something for the weekend.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with mochi from Okayama Kobo and a bowl of udon at Gombei, then wander the Japanese grocery on Jackson St at your own pace.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and it's a genuinely great morning walk.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the sculpture garden and work through the contemporary galleries inside. It's free and the collection is legitimately world-class for a regional museum.
cards.why: The Triton is the most underrated art stop in the South Bay and it fits perfectly before your afternoon event.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Take your time with the Spring Egg-stravaganza and then swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition while you're in the arts headspace.
cards.why: Both are free and it's a smart way to double up on arts content while you're already out.

cards.name: Spring Egg-stravaganza

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball

cards.why: Perfect Friday evening vibe and a great way to decompress between the afternoon and dinner.

cards.name: 1-on-1 Tech Help

cards.why: Free, practical, and genuinely useful if you've got a tech question that's been sitting on the back burner.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose

cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown — grab mochi at Okayama Kobo, get a coffee, and browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St before the crowds show up.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through the Patient No More Exhibition at King Library — it's free and worth a slow walk through.
cards.why: Free art on a rainy-ish morning is a no-brainer.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA

cards.name: Tranquility Day Spa (Capitol Ave)

cards.name: Arbor Day Celebration & Ceremonial Tree Planting
cards.blurb: Head to Arbor Day Celebration & Ceremonial Tree Planting and see what's going on.

cards.name: Float Station

cards.name: TaoTaoTea

cards.name: Sascc Spring Concert
cards.blurb: Hit the French Market Carnival — browse the vendors, pick up something you don't need, and enjoy the outdoor market vibe while it's still dry.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge

cards.name: 四姐 Special Noodle

cards.name: Visionary Arts
cards.address: 1072 S De Anza Blvd a106, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Visionary Arts to browse the gallery. It's an eclectic, thought-provoking space that makes for a great afternoon wander before your later commitment.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Join the community at Paint-A-Rock, a fun low-key event where you paint rocks to hide around Saratoga for others to find. No skills required, just bring good vibes.

cards.name: Paint-A-Rock

cards.name: Afro Gala at Student Union
cards.blurb: The Afro Gala at SJSU's Student Union is tonight. Expect performances, culture, and a room full of people who actually want to be there.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely fun way to spend a Saturday evening.

cards.name: Clandestine Brewing
cards.address: 980 S 1st St ste b, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at Clandestine Brewing on S 1st St. Order a pint or two and decompress from the day.
cards.why: Great local brewery, open until 10, and exactly what you want after a full Saturday.

cards.name: A.M. Craft
cards.address: 481 E San Carlos St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at A.M. Craft with a solid coffee and whatever's coming out of the kitchen this morning. Good spot to ease in before the day picks up.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday morning calls for a great cup of coffee in a place worth sitting in.

cards.name: Community Yoga and Heartfulness Meditation
cards.address: 1780 Hillsdale Ave San Jose
cards.blurb: Drop into the free Community Yoga and Heartfulness Meditation at the Hillsdale library branch. Casual and low-key, great way to stretch out and reset before the afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, it's indoors, and it's the perfect way to use a grey morning productively.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum is genuinely one of the most surprising things in San Jose. Real mummies, ancient artifacts, authentic hieroglyphics. Walk the planetarium grounds and take your time inside.
cards.why: Wet weather is exactly when a museum like this shines.

cards.name: South Bay Sports Training & Batting Cages
cards.address: 2127 S 10th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Warm up at South Bay Sports Training and Batting Cages before the game tonight. Get in some swings, shake off the afternoon fog, and get into baseball mode.
cards.why: You're going to a baseball game tonight. This is the obvious pre-game move.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: San Jose Giants home game vs. Visalia Rawhide at Excite Ballpark.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Barry Galbreath-Flores Senior Saxophone at Music Building
cards.blurb: Head straight from the game to the SJSU Music Building for Barry Galbreath-Flores performing senior saxophone recital. Free, live, and a genuinely cool way to close out the night.
cards.why: It's free live music on a Saturday night and the timing works out perfectly after the game.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Bakenstein with a fresh pastry and coffee — it's the kind of neighborhood bakery that makes a rainy Saturday feel intentional.
cards.why: Perfect low-key way to ease into the day before things pick up.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum and get up close with real mummies, sarcophagi, and ancient artifacts — genuinely one of the most surprising museums in the South Bay.
cards.why: A rainy morning is exactly the right time to disappear into an indoor museum this good.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU's Mayer today — an arts showcase that gives you something to actually think about and talk over afterward.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and genuinely interesting on a grey afternoon.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Urban Plates, where you can mix and match fresh, made-from-scratch plates — think wood-roasted proteins, seasonal sides, and solid salads.
cards.why: Clean, filling, and fast enough that you won't lose half your afternoon sitting around.

cards.name: ShirSpa
cards.address: 15868 Cherry Blossom Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at ShirSpa with a massage or facial before tonight's show — let someone else take care of you for an hour.
cards.why: You've got time to kill before 7:30 and this is a much better use of it than sitting in a parking lot.

cards.name: Mint Leaf Cuisine
cards.address: 14420 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at Mint Leaf Cuisine in Saratoga for some refined Indian food before the show — the sauces are rich and the naan is the real move.
cards.why: It's the right kind of dinner for a night out at a live music event.

cards.name: Stroll Down Penny Lane - Celebrating the Life & Music of Sir Paul McCartney
cards.blurb: Event date: April 25, 2026 Event Time: 07:30 PM - 11:59 PM Location: 1 W. Campbell Ave. Campbell, CA 95008 Description: Stroll Down Penny Lane is a live performance with beautiful accompanying films…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Moonwake Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 1412 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a proper coffee at Moonwake — grab a latte and something to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: A solid local roaster beats a chain any day, especially on a grey rainy morning.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club at Cupertino Library for a half hour of casual conversation practice — it's low-key, free, and a surprisingly fun way to meet people.
cards.why: It's free, it's in Cupertino, and it fits perfectly before your 11 AM class.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Main Street Cupertino for lunch. The Saturday farmers market might still be winding down, and there are plenty of solid spots to grab a meal in the outdoor plaza.
cards.why: You're already in Cupertino, so this is the natural lunch move.

cards.name: How to Draw Fantasy Characters
cards.blurb: Are you fascinated by dragons, wizards, and magical realms? Do you love the imaginative world of Dungeons & Dragons and want to create your own legendary characters? Join author and comic book artist…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Drop-in Tech Help
cards.blurb: Sit in on How to Draw Fantasy Characters and actually try it — no prior drawing experience needed, just show up and have fun with it.
cards.why: It's a rainy Saturday and drawing fantasy creatures is a great way to spend it.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden if the rain lets up, then take your time through the contemporary galleries inside.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a rainy afternoon is basically the perfect plan.

cards.name: Sunflower Spa
cards.address: 14501 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Sunflower Spa in Saratoga and decompress. A massage is basically mandatory after a full day out in the rain.
cards.why: Saratoga is a great place to wind down, and this spa consistently delivers.

cards.name: Persian Kabobs
cards.address: 7335 Bollinger Rd Suite D, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day with dinner at Persian Kabobs in Cupertino. Get the kabob combo and some fresh-baked bread and call it a night.
cards.why: Rich, hearty food hits different after a cold rainy day.

cards.name: Mil's Diner
cards.address: 36 S Abbott Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Mil's Diner with a solid breakfast before the day gets going. Classic diner food, good coffee, cozy spot.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday morning calls for a proper sit-down breakfast, not a sad granola bar.

cards.name: Gentle Flow Yoga
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Gentle Flow Yoga class at the library and shake off the morning stiffness. Low-key, accessible, and exactly the right pace for a slow rainy day.
cards.why: It's free and indoors, which is basically the perfect combo when it's 57 degrees and drizzling.

cards.name: International Film Festival
cards.blurb: Catch a screening at the International Film Festival happening today in Milpitas. Free admission, great way to kill a couple hours before the afternoon.
cards.why: Rain outside, films inside — pretty much a no-brainer.

cards.name: Dungeons & Dragons: Spring Mini Campaign
cards.blurb: We're hosting a winter mini campaign! Our DM will guide you through the process from character creation and basic rules. Make your own character and get lost in the world of Dungeons and Dragons.…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Sinoy's Kitchen
cards.address: 283 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Sinoy's Kitchen in Milpitas for dinner after your session. Try whatever the locals are ordering and settle in.
cards.why: You'll have earned a real meal by this point in the day.

cards.name: IBU Taproom & Bottle Shop
cards.address: 1422 Dempsey Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at IBU Taproom with a coffee or a cold one if you're feeling bold — it's a chill spot to ease into the day without the rush.
cards.why: Rainy Saturday mornings were made for a laid-back taproom vibe.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Qi Gong class at Milpitas Library and let the slow, meditative movements reset your body for the afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, indoors, and genuinely relaxing on a grey day like this.

cards.name: Dakao Sandwiches II
cards.address: 72 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a Vietnamese sandwich at Dakao Sandwiches II — solid, fast, and easy on the wallet before your afternoon activities.
cards.why: Hard to beat a great banh mi for under $15 on a rainy day.

cards.name: Clear Lake Spa – Massage in Milpitas
cards.address: 1766 Clear Lake Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book yourself a massage at Clear Lake Spa and spend a couple hours actually unwinding before the evening picks up.
cards.why: A spa afternoon on a cold, rainy day is basically perfect timing.

cards.name: Cajun Crack'n Milpitas
cards.address: 275 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Cajun Crack'n Milpitas for a messy, fun seafood dinner — get the seasoned crab or shrimp and dig in with your hands.
cards.why: You want something satisfying and a little indulgent going into a book discussion night.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Milpitas Adult Book Discussion Group for their conversation on Raising Hare by Chloe Dalton, a quiet memoir about an unexpected connection with a wild hare.
cards.why: It's a genuinely interesting read and a great way to end a Saturday with good conversation.

cards.name: Milpitas Adult Book Discussion Group - Raising Hare: A Memoir by Chloe Dalton
cards.blurb: In April, the Milpitas Library Adult Book Group will be reading Raising Hare: A Memoir by Chloe Dalton. You are invited to join in the lively discussion. April 22nd at 11:00 AM In Our Program Room…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Saigon Spa
cards.address: 2055 Grant Rd Ste 100, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Start the rainy morning with a proper spa treatment at Saigon Spa. A massage or body treatment is the perfect way to ease into a drizzly Saturday.
cards.why: Cold and rainy outside means this is genuinely the best possible use of a Saturday morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum, where you can walk through decades of tech history in a way that's genuinely fascinating even if you're not a hardware person. The exhibits are dense and worth taking your time with.
cards.why: Rainy day, indoor museum, legitimately wild stuff on display — this is a no-brainer.

cards.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Catch the Steve Jobs in Exile exhibit while you're already at the Computer History Museum. It covers the NeXT years and the period most people overlook.
cards.why: You're already there, and this one is genuinely worth the extra time.

cards.name: Bevri - Palo Alto
cards.address: 335 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Bevri in Palo Alto for Georgian food. Think khinkali dumplings, rich stews, and wine from the Caucasus. Order more than you think you need.
cards.why: It's a solid lunch spot with a menu that feels different from the usual South Bay options.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Check out 1,000 Ways to Hold, a new performance work by Erika Chong Shuch at Stanford. It's free, and this kind of contemporary performance art is worth experiencing with fresh eyes and no expectations.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a good way to fill the afternoon before your evening event.

cards.name: La Bodeguita del Medio
cards.address: 463 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at La Bodeguita del Medio on California Ave. Cuban food, mojitos, and a lively vibe. Get the ropa vieja and order a drink while you settle in.
cards.why: Great dinner stop before heading out to your evening event.

cards.name: Wednesday Travel Nights: Grand Canyon Adventure
cards.blurb: Text from presenter: John Gayler will share both still-photo and live-action video highlights of a 2-week outfitter-led river trip through the Grand Canyon, including planning and logistics. But it’s…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Dolce Spazio Gelato
cards.address: 221 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a bowl of something warm at Rio Adobe Southwest Cafe. Their breakfast burritos and Southwestern-style eats are a solid way to kick off a rainy Saturday.
cards.why: Cheap, cozy, and exactly what you want when it's gray and drizzly out.

cards.name: ShirSpa
cards.address: 15868 Cherry Blossom Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Book a morning treatment at ShirSpa and let the rainy day work in your favor. A massage or facial feels even better when you have nowhere else to be.
cards.why: A wet Saturday morning is basically made for a spa visit.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge and paint your own pottery. Pick a piece off the shelf, sit down with some paint, and make something worth keeping.
cards.why: It's creative, low-key, and perfect for a cold rainy afternoon.

cards.name: Trattoria 360
cards.address: 360 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Trattoria 360 on East Campbell Ave. Order the pasta, settle into the atmosphere, and take your time.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the better Italian spots in the South Bay and well worth a long lunch.

cards.name: Sascc & County Assessor: Property Assessment Workshop
cards.blurb: Stop into the SASCC and County Assessor Property Assessment Workshop in Saratoga. Free, informative, and useful if you own property or just want to understand how assessments work in Santa Clara County.
cards.why: It's free and practical, and it puts you in Saratoga right before your evening program.
cards.venue: West Valley College, Fox 120 Lecture Hall

cards.name: Knit-Alongs at Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Settle in for Travel Program: China at the Saratoga Community Room. These community travel film and lecture nights are a great way to spend an evening without going far.
cards.why: A relaxed, interesting way to end the night without fighting rain or traffic.

cards.name: Travel Program: China
cards.blurb: Presenter Harlan Crowder will take you on a photographic journey through China and will feature three main locations: Wuzhen, the ancient and charming water town on the Yangtze River known as the…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Pav Bhaji and More Milpitas
cards.address: 529 S Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with something warm and filling at Pav Bhaji and More. The pav bhaji is the move — buttery bread rolls, spiced mashed veggies, and a chai to go with it.
cards.why: A cozy, affordable breakfast that sets the tone before the stitch-in.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) are hosting a friendly needle works stitch-in. Come join us from 10 am to 2 pm in the conference room, there are always extra seats! The South Bay Needlepointers…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Naans & Curries
cards.address: 102 Great Mall Dr Suite 102, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Naans & Curries in Great Mall. Get the naan fresh from the tandoor and pair it with a curry that hits. The place is consistently good and the portions don't mess around.
cards.why: Solid Indian food right when you need it, and it fits the rainy-day mood perfectly.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Drop into the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise Class at Milpitas Library. Slow, intentional movement and breathing. It's the kind of thing that sounds low-key but leaves you feeling genuinely reset.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday afternoon is basically made for this.

cards.name: Zen Haven Spa
cards.address: 1224 S Abel St #1224, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day at Zen Haven Spa in Milpitas. Book a session and let the rest of the afternoon just melt off. Good call after a full day of being out.
cards.why: You earned it, and they're open late so there's no rush.

cards.name: Apni Royal Kitchen
cards.address: 579 S Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Apni Royal Kitchen on S Main St. The food is home-style and packed with flavor. Order generously and take your time with it.
cards.why: One of the best-kept dinner spots in Milpitas and a great way to close out the day.

cards.name: Gentle Flow Yoga
cards.blurb: Start the morning with Gentle Flow Yoga. Low-key, free, and a solid way to shake off the early chill before the day gets going.
cards.why: Free yoga on a rainy Saturday morning is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Zen Haven Spa
cards.address: 1224 S Abel St #1224, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Treat yourselves to some spa time at Zen Haven. Great way to lean into the grey weather instead of fighting it.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday is basically spa weather by definition.

cards.name: International Film Festival
cards.blurb: Catch some short films at the International Film Festival in Milpitas. Free admission, local and international picks, and a perfect indoor hang for a wet afternoon.
cards.why: Free film festival on a rainy day is a no-brainer.

cards.name: IBU Taproom & Bottle Shop
cards.address: 1422 Dempsey Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to IBU Taproom for a late lunch. Grab a craft pour and something to eat while you decompress from the film screenings.
cards.why: Solid local taproom with good food and a relaxed vibe that fits a rainy afternoon perfectly.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Kill some time before the evening with an escape room at EDscapade Games. Book a room and see if you two can puzzle your way out before the hour's up.
cards.why: Way more fun than sitting around waiting for the evening, and it's a great couples activity.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Swing by the Milpitas Children's Chess Club at 6:30. Even without kids, it's worth checking out as a spectator or volunteer presence if that's your thing.
cards.why: It's on the list, so you're going.

cards.name: Milpitas Children's Chess Club
cards.blurb: Come play chess and make new friends in a fun and relaxed environment! This program is for children from grades K-8. Registration is not required but space is limited. This program is not a class.…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Angkor Chef
cards.address: 86 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Angkor Chef's neighbor for a warm-up, actually — kick things off with a morning soak and table massage at Bamboo Healthcare Spa to ease into the rainy day.
cards.why: A drizzly Saturday morning is the perfect excuse to start slow and get loose before the rest of the day.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Pick up a fresh mochi or pastry at Okayama Kobo and grab coffee while walking the blocks around Jackson St — one of only three surviving Japantowns in the country and a genuinely great morning neighborhood to wander.
cards.why: Rainy morning, covered storefronts, good coffee and sweets — this is exactly how you want to start a gray Saturday.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library for Fists of Fury, a free exhibition exploring Asian American martial arts culture through film, photography, and artifacts.
cards.why: It's free, indoors, and genuinely fascinating — a great way to spend a rainy late morning.

cards.name: Angkor Chef
cards.address: 86 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Book a table massage or foot treatment at Bamboo Healthcare Spa and let the afternoon rain become the best possible background noise for an hour of actual rest.
cards.why: A 5-star spa on a cold, rainy afternoon is hard to argue with.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University exhibition includes original artwork (serigraphs, called "silkscreen posters,") by the…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 543 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a warm coffee and something to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: Cold and rainy mornings call for a good cup of coffee first.

cards.name: Gentle Flow Yoga
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Gentle Flow Yoga session and ease into the day with some slow, intentional movement.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday morning is basically made for yoga.

cards.name: International Film Festival
cards.blurb: Catch some of the International Film Festival screenings and see what's showing on the big day.
cards.why: Free film festival on a rainy afternoon is a perfect excuse to stay in and watch something great.

cards.name: Baby Bouncers Lapsit for Prewalkers
cards.blurb: Join us on Tuesday mornings for Baby Bouncers Lapsit (for non-walking babies) up to 12 months old. This is a baby and caregivers program with an early literacy focus on traditional nursery rhymes,…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Little Learners STEAM for Ages 3-5
cards.blurb: Baby Bouncers Lapsit is a fun, interactive session designed for prewalkers and their adults.
cards.why: It's on the schedule, so make it count.

cards.name: Kovai Cafe - Milpitas
cards.address: 181 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Kovai Cafe for some South Indian flavors and a proper sit-down lunch after a busy morning.
cards.why: The place has a cult following for good reason.

cards.name: Retro Rewind
cards.address: 1343 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Retro Rewind is the kind of place you walk into for an hour and walk out two hours later, packed with vintage games, nostalgia, and good energy.
cards.why: It's the perfect rainy afternoon activity for adults who want to have fun without overthinking it.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Book an escape room at EDscapade Games and spend the evening solving puzzles and working together under pressure.
cards.why: Escape rooms and Saturday nights just make sense together.

cards.name: Bayon Temple
cards.address: 2425 S Bascom Ave suite a, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Bayon Temple with a warm bowl of congee or noodle soup — exactly the kind of cozy, low-key breakfast you want on a rainy Saturday.
cards.why: Cold and wet outside calls for something hot and comforting to kick things off.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through King Library to catch the Patient No More Exhibition — a thoughtful art show worth a slow walk-through before the day gets busier.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and a solid way to ease into the arts on a drizzly morning.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum is genuinely one of the most surprising places in San Jose. Real mummies, real sarcophagi, hieroglyphics everywhere — give yourself a full two hours to actually absorb it.
cards.why: Rain is basically a gift when you have a museum this good to disappear into.

cards.name: Cafe Dolce Sicilia
cards.address: 1165 Lincoln Ave #110, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Cafe Dolce Sicilia on Lincoln Ave is the move for a laid-back lunch. Grab a panino, a cannoli, and a proper espresso while you decompress from the museum.
cards.why: Intimate, Italian, and the kind of spot that makes a rainy afternoon actually feel great.

cards.name: Little Wine House
cards.address: 350 W Julian St Bldg 4, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Little Wine House on W Julian is the perfect decompression spot. Settle in, let the staff guide you through a glass or two, and let the afternoon wind down properly.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday evening and a wine bar is basically a perfect combination.

cards.name: Macla Presents: (Un)Documents By Jesús I. Valles
cards.address: 510 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113
cards.blurb: Tickets on Sale Now! Join us for a powerful evening of theater as Jesús I. Valles performs their solo performance of (Un)Documents, an evocative journey across borders, languages, and identities.…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at the Triton Museum of Art. Walk the sculpture garden, check out the contemporary galleries, and take your time with whatever's on view — it's a genuinely great art space and the free admission means zero pressure to rush.
cards.why: Rainy morning and a world-class free art museum is a no-brainer combo.

cards.name: GameTime Hobby Shop
cards.address: 2100 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into GameTime Hobby Shop and browse the board games, tabletop RPGs, and hobby supplies. Great spot to flip through something you haven't seen before or pick up a game for tonight.
cards.why: It's a rainy Saturday and this is exactly the kind of low-key browse that makes a gray morning feel fun.

cards.name: Birre by Pour Decisions
cards.address: 320 Sharks Wy, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Birre by Pour Decisions for lunch. It's a beer-forward spot with a solid food menu sitting right on the water near SAP Center. Order a pint and settle in.
cards.why: Good food, cold beer, and an indoor setting makes this the perfect rainy afternoon lunch.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Santana Row to walk the boulevard, pop into a few shops, and get a table at Straits or Sino before the evening gets busy. Great spot for a pre-dinner drink at the bar while you figure out the rest of the night.
cards.why: The covered outdoor walkways and indoor restaurants make it a solid rainy evening spot.

cards.name: Ethan Chung and Gabriel Santos Junior Saxophone Recitals at Music Building
cards.blurb: Catch the Ethan Chung and Gabriel Santos Junior Saxophone Recitals at the SJSU Music Building. Free classical performance in an intimate setting — shows up in your memory way longer than you'd expect.
cards.why: Free live music on a rainy Saturday night is a pretty perfect way to end the day.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Artists: Yvonne Escalante and Lauren O'Connor-Korb
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning here before the crowds show up. Walk through the full history of computing, from room-sized machines to the devices in your pocket. The exhibits are genuinely wild and the staff actually knows their stuff.
cards.why: Rainy morning calls for something indoors and interesting, and this place absolutely delivers.

cards.name: Diamond training & development
cards.address: 121 E El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Covour Coffee for a mid-morning cup before the day really kicks off. It's a good spot to sit down, warm up, and regroup.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday deserves a solid coffee stop between activities.

cards.name: Covour Coffee
cards.address: 2706 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Good coffee and a relaxed vibe make this a solid mid-morning stop. Grab something to drink and settle in for a bit before heading to the museum.
cards.why: It's a cozy indoor spot that fits perfectly on a cool, drizzly morning.

cards.name: Algorithms of Love
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head back into the Computer History Museum for this exhibition exploring how algorithms shape the way we connect, date, and relate to each other. It's thought-provoking without being preachy.
cards.why: It pairs perfectly with the morning museum visit and gives you something to chew on over lunch.

cards.name: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.address: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head to Castro Street for lunch. The strip has solid options for pho, bánh mì, and a handful of other cuisines. Pick whatever sounds good and grab a table out of the rain.
cards.why: Castro Street is the best lunch stretch in Mountain View and it's packed with options.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the day at FireWorks Ceramics. Browse the studio, check out what's available, and see if a drop-in session works for you. It's a low-key, creative way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: A ceramics studio is the perfect wind-down after a full day of arts and ideas.

cards.name: Apple at 50
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: This event is sold out. Unfortunately, we will not be able to admit walk-ins. Watch the livestream here. On April 1, 1976, Apple Computer was founded with a radical idea: that powerful computing…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Cafe Pro Bono
cards.address: 2437 Birch St, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning here with coffee and something to eat before diving into the day.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday morning calls for a cozy neighborhood cafe, and this one on Birch St delivers.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander the galleries, spend some time with the Rodin sculptures in the courtyard, and take your time with the permanent collection — it's world-class and free.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and genuinely impressive — perfect way to spend a drizzly morning on campus.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Pop into this gallery conversation about Jeremy Frey's woven work — it's a short walk from Cantor and a genuinely different kind of art experience.
cards.why: You're already on campus and this is the kind of intimate art talk you don't stumble into every day.

cards.name: La Bodeguita del Medio
cards.address: 463 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for lunch at this Cuban spot on California Ave. Get the mojito and whatever's coming off the kitchen that day.
cards.why: La Bodeguita is exactly the kind of lunch spot a rainy afternoon was made for.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Computer History Museum digging into exhibits on everything from early mainframes to the birth of the internet. It's a lot more gripping than it sounds.
cards.why: Two hours flies by here, and it's the ideal rainy afternoon activity.

cards.name: Paris, City of Innovation: 19th-century Universal Expositions as seen through the Roxane Debuisson Collection of Paris History
cards.address: 557 Escondido Mall, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: This exhibit, based primarily on materials in the Roxane Debuisson Collection on Paris History at Stanford University Libraries, highlights the role of Paris as a fertile environment for…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Kenz Coffee Bar, a cozy spot on University Ave perfect for a warm drink and something to eat before heading to campus.
cards.why: Good coffee is non-negotiable on a grey morning like this.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours wandering the Cantor Arts Center — the permanent collection is genuinely world-class and the indoor galleries are ideal when it's drizzly out.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and rain-proof.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch this new performance work by Erika Chong Shuch at Stanford — it's the kind of unexpected, physically expressive piece that sticks with you.
cards.why: Free and genuinely interesting contemporary performance on campus today.

cards.name: Friends of the Mountain View Library
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the Friends of the Mountain View Library book sale for a relaxed afternoon of digging through shelves at very forgiving prices.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key afternoon errand that turns into an hour of happy browsing.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into the Gallery Conversations session for Jeremy Frey's Woven exhibition and hear someone actually talk through the work with you.
cards.why: A guided conversation turns a good gallery visit into a great one.

cards.name: Kween's
cards.address: 292 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Kween's on Castro Street for a solid late lunch or early dinner — the vibe is casual and the food is worth it.
cards.why: Castro St is the right call for dinner before the game.

cards.name: Stanford Cardinal Softball vs. Florida State Seminoles Softball
cards.address: 161 Churchill Mall
cards.blurb: Head to the Stanford softball field and catch the Cardinal take on Florida State — college softball is fast, loud, and genuinely fun to watch in person.
cards.why: A night game with a big matchup is a great way to wrap up a Saturday.

cards.name: Public Tour | Auguste Rodin
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: This exhibition celebrates Auguste Rodin’s relentless pursuit to convey complex emotions, diverse psychological states, and pure sensuality through the nude. A century after his death, Auguste Rodin…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Covour Coffee
cards.address: 2706 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Covour Coffee with a warm drink and something to eat before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Good coffee on a rainy morning is non-negotiable.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Swing by the Anderson Collection for the Gallery Conversations around Jeremy Frey's stunning woven work. It's a focused, intimate way to actually learn about what you're looking at.
cards.why: Free art talk on a drizzly Saturday morning is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk over to Cantor Arts Center and spend some time with the Rodin sculptures and rotating galleries. The outdoor sculpture garden is partially covered, so the rain won't ruin it.
cards.why: It's free, world-class, and right there on campus.

cards.name: Ethel's Fancy
cards.address: 550 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Ethel's Fancy for lunch. The menu is seasonal and creative, so just order what sounds interesting and trust it.
cards.why: It's one of the best lunch spots in Palo Alto and worth every minute of the wait.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Color Me Mine on University Ave and paint something. Pick a piece, grab some glazes, and just mess around. No experience needed and it's genuinely fun with a drink in hand if they have it.
cards.why: Rainy Saturday afternoon activity that actually gives you something to take home.

cards.name: Bevri - Palo Alto
cards.address: 335 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Bevri on University Ave for Georgian food. Get the khachapuri, full stop.

cards.name: Branner Library Monthly Book & Map Exhibit - The Atlantic

cards.name: Co Ba Dessert, Tea & Coffee
cards.address: 3730 N First St Ste. 110, San Jose, CA 95134, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with something warm at Co Ba — grab a Vietnamese coffee or a milk tea and a light bite to kick things off.

cards.name: Gentle Flow Yoga

cards.why: Free yoga on a rainy Saturday is kind of perfect.

cards.name: Naans & Curries
cards.address: 102 Great Mall Dr Suite 102, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA

cards.name: Retro Rewind
cards.address: 1343 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA

cards.name: Knitting for Kids at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Little Learners STEAM for Ages 3-5 at Milpitas Library — even if you're not bringing a kid, it's a solid excuse to be in the library building.

cards.name: Little Learners STEAM for Ages 3-5

cards.name: Milpitas Library
cards.address: 160 N Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: You're already at Milpitas Library, so wander the stacks, grab a magazine, or just sit somewhere quiet for a bit.
cards.why: Libraries on rainy days are genuinely underrated.

cards.name: Ushiya Grill
cards.address: 1792 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA

cards.why: Warm food after a full rainy day is exactly what you want.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Walk through the State of Illusion Exhibition and take your time with it — this is the kind of show where you keep finding new things the longer you look.

cards.name: The Choclo
cards.address: 82 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA

cards.why: You'll be glad you stopped here.

cards.name: Restful SPA
cards.address: 991 Saratoga Ave #150, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at Restful SPA getting a massage or treatment — perfect way to slow the day down before the evening picks back up.
cards.why: A rainy Saturday afternoon was made for this.

cards.name: Santana Row

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library

cards.name: Computer History Museum

cards.name: Softball - San Jose State vs. San Diego State at San Jose, Calif.
cards.blurb: Catch the San Jose State softball game against San Diego State. Free admission, outdoor bleacher energy, and a good excuse to be outside even if it's a little grey.
