cards.name: Sea Salt Therapy
cards.address: 3199 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Sea Salt Therapy in Campbell. Float tanks and salt rooms are exactly what you want after a full day of walking around in the cold.
cards.why: Nothing resets you faster on a gray, damp day.

cards.name: Jake’s of Saratoga
cards.address: 12175 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd Suite"E, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night at Jake's of Saratoga. Settle into a booth, order something hearty off the dinner menu, and call it a proper Saturday.
cards.why: Great neighborhood spot with solid food and no pretension.

cards.name: SMT Bedrich Smetana
cards.address: 15400 Montalvo Rd, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Head to SMT Bedrich Smetana and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Huge Paperback Book Sale
cards.blurb: Dig through the Huge Paperback Book Sale at the Saratoga library — bring a tote bag because you will leave with more than you planned.
cards.why: It's free, it's indoors, and a rainy Saturday is the perfect excuse to stock up on reads.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge in Los Gatos and paint a piece of pottery — pick something you'll actually want to display and take your time with it.
cards.why: Hands-on, creative, and fully indoors, which is exactly right for a 90% rain day.

cards.name: Restful SPA
cards.address: 991 Saratoga Ave #150, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a table at Restful SPA on Saratoga Ave and book a massage — a solid mid-day reset that feels way more intentional than just waiting out the rain.
cards.why: A rainy afternoon and a massage is a combination that basically plans itself.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the day at Dio Deka in Los Gatos for upscale Greek cuisine. Order the mezze, split a bottle of something interesting, and settle in.
cards.why: It's a proper splurge dinner and the kind of place that makes a slow rainy Saturday feel like a real occasion.

cards.name: XYC Massage
cards.address: 19501 Stevens Creek Blvd #103, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to XYC Massage on Stevens Creek for reflexology or a follow-up treatment if you want to double down on the wellness afternoon.
cards.why: Two wellness stops on a rainy day is not excessive, it's smart.

cards.name: Brahma Kumaris Meditation Center, Silicon Valley
cards.address: 540 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into the Brahma Kumaris Meditation Center for a free guided meditation session. Good way to reset your mind before a busy afternoon.
cards.why: Rainy mornings and meditation were made for each other.

cards.name: Milpitas Adult Book Discussion Group - Raising Hare: A Memoir by Chloe Dalton
cards.blurb: Join the Milpitas Adult Book Discussion Group at the library as they dig into 'Raising Hare: A Memoir' by Chloe Dalton. No prep required, just show up and talk books.
cards.why: It's a free, low-key way to spend a rainy morning with other readers.

cards.name: Saucy Asian - Milpitas
cards.address: Food Court, 447 Great Mall Dr Space 10, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Saucy Asian in the Great Mall food court. Try whatever's looking good on the menu.
cards.why: You're going to want something satisfying before the afternoon fills up.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the free Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library. Great for loosening up and clearing your head.
cards.why: It's free, it's indoors, and it pairs perfectly with a rainy afternoon.

cards.name: Clear Lake Spa – Massage in Milpitas
cards.address: 1766 Clear Lake Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage or treatment at Clear Lake Spa in Milpitas. A couple hours here on a cold, rainy afternoon is genuinely the move.
cards.why: Nothing better to do with a grey Saturday than get a solid spa session in.

cards.name: XFYD Chess Class for Grades 6-8
cards.blurb: Designed for middle school students, this class focuses on more advanced chess strategies and tactics. Participants will strengthen critical thinking and teamwork while enjoying a challenging and…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar
cards.address: 445 Hershner Dr, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Kenz Coffee Bar with a solid cup and something to eat before the rain picks up.
cards.why: A cozy morning coffee stop beats sitting at home watching the clouds roll in.

cards.name: Beyond Text Bookstore
cards.address: 318 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through Beyond Text Bookstore and actually browse the shelves without feeling rushed.
cards.why: A rainy-ish Sunday morning is exactly when a great indie bookstore hits different.

cards.name: Ainsley House
cards.address: 300 Grant St, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Tour the Ainsley House, a beautifully preserved craftsman home with some genuinely interesting local history inside.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you keep meaning to check out and never do, so today's the day.

cards.name: Mint Leaf Cuisine
cards.address: 14420 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Mint Leaf Cuisine on Big Basin Way, a solid Thai spot worth sitting down for.
cards.why: The food is genuinely good and it fits perfectly between the museum and the afternoon ahead.

cards.name: Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit
cards.blurb: Walk through the Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit and see what local student artists have been working on.
cards.why: Free art on a gray Sunday afternoon is always a good call.

cards.name: Uncorked
cards.address: 14500 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Uncorked right on Big Basin Way for a glass of wine to wind down the afternoon.
cards.why: It's steps from the exhibit and the perfect way to decompress before dinner.

cards.name: Asa Restaurant | Los Gatos
cards.address: 57 Saratoga-Los Gatos Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Asa Restaurant in Los Gatos with a proper sit-down dinner, the kind of meal that makes a Sunday feel complete.
cards.why: It's a mid-to-upscale spot that actually delivers, and Sunday nights there are low-key great.

cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: Taste
cards.address: 423 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Taste on University Ave — the menu is focused and seasonal, so just order whatever sounds good and sit with it.
cards.why: They close at 3, so Sunday lunch here feels like a little treat you caught just in time.

cards.name: Bay Area Quiz Club
cards.address: 3700 Middlefield Rd Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Head to Bay Area Quiz Club for some low-stakes trivia fun with a crowd of locals who take it just seriously enough to be entertaining.
cards.why: Sunday afternoon trivia is genuinely a great use of the post-lunch hour.

cards.name: Illustrator Workshop: Bo Lu
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Catch the Illustrator Workshop with Bo Lu at Linden Tree Books in Los Altos — a free, hands-on session worth checking out even if you're not a regular illustrator.
cards.why: Free creative workshop on a rainy Sunday afternoon is exactly the right vibe.

cards.name: Bevri - Palo Alto
cards.address: 335 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with dinner at Bevri, a Georgian wine bar on University Ave. Go for the khinkali dumplings and order something from their natural wine list.
cards.why: Warm, cozy, and interesting enough to make the evening feel like it actually went somewhere.

cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Uptown
cards.address: 15662 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this Los Gatos cafe with a proper breakfast and good coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid local spot that closes at 2 PM, so morning is the move.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the galleries and take your time in the sculpture garden — it's free, uncrowded, and genuinely impressive for a local museum.
cards.why: Free contemporary art with a sculpture garden is a perfect way to spend a slow Sunday morning.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out this optical illusion and perception-based exhibition — the kind of show that's actually fun to walk through, not just stare at.
cards.why: It's free, it's in Santa Clara, and it pairs perfectly with the Triton visit for an art-heavy afternoon.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Pick a vendor or three and graze your way through lunch at San Pedro Square — tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee, whatever sounds good.
cards.why: Best all-in-one food stop in the area and a great spot to settle in and decompress for a bit.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a machine at Slingshot and play pinball for a couple hours — they've got a great selection and it's genuinely fun for adults.
cards.why: Beats sitting around and it's one of the better ways to spend a rainy Sunday evening.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Wander the California Ave stalls, grab a coffee and something warm to eat, and browse the produce and vendors before the Sunday crowd really fills in.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Sunday morning in Palo Alto.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Do the full loop up to the Dish and back — it's a solid climb with sweeping views of the Bay and the peninsula when the clouds cooperate.
cards.why: At 55°F this is actually perfect hiking weather, cool and comfortable the whole way up.

cards.name: Woodland Adult Book Discussion
cards.blurb: Drop into the Woodland Adult Book Discussion in Los Altos, a free community conversation that's low-key and genuinely good if you like talking books with a sharp crowd.
cards.why: A rainy afternoon and a book discussion go together naturally.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum checking out the exhibits on mainframes, early personal computers, and the full weird arc of Silicon Valley history.
cards.why: Indoor, endlessly interesting, and a great call when the weather turns gray.

cards.name: Serenity Wellness Spa
cards.address: 200 California Ave Suite 190, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Serenity Wellness Spa on California Ave and decompress properly after a full day on your feet.
cards.why: You've been moving since 9 AM, this is the right way to finish the day.

cards.name: Giorgio's Italian Grill and Pizzeria
cards.address: 939 W El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Giorgio's for a proper sit-down Italian dinner with pizza and pasta done well in a relaxed setting.
cards.why: Comfort food on a cold, rainy Sunday night is a hard thing to argue with.

cards.name: Daily Donuts & Sandwiches
cards.address: 708 Fair Oaks Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and a sandwich at Daily Donuts. It's a low-key spot perfect for a quick, satisfying breakfast before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good food, great price, and an easy way to start the day right.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Drop into the Monday Morning Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos. Free, no experience needed, just show up and decompress for an hour.
cards.why: A free midweek reset that most people don't even know exists.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk the Mountain View Farmers Market and browse the produce, baked goods, and local vendors. Cool morning air makes it a nice stroll without the afternoon crowds.
cards.why: Monday morning at the farmers market is genuinely chill and not packed.

cards.name: Stevenson Elementary School
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Check out what's happening at Stevenson Elementary today. Community events like this are easy to drop into and give you a feel for what's going on locally.
cards.why: It's free and it's today, so worth a look.

cards.name: UMe | Tea & Snacks
cards.address: 220 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to UMe on Castro Street for tea and snacks. Order a few things, grab a table, and take your time.
cards.why: Castro Street is the right place to land after a mellow morning.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a walk around Shoreline Park and Lake. The overcast sky actually keeps it comfortable, and the water views are great even on a cloudy day.
cards.why: Fresh air and open space are exactly what the afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto. Book a session and let someone actually work out whatever tension you've built up.
cards.why: A Monday massage is an underrated move and this place delivers.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Walk California Ave and browse the farmers market. Pick up fresh produce, sample whatever looks good, and take your time wandering the stalls.
cards.why: It's a classic Palo Alto Monday morning and the market is worth the stroll.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Drop into the Monday Morning Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos. It's free, low-key, and a genuinely good reset for the week ahead.
cards.why: Starting the week with some mental clarity beats scrolling your phone.

cards.name: City Hall Cafe
cards.address: 250 Hamilton Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to City Hall Cafe for lunch. It's a beloved local spot with a short menu done really well. Order whatever the special is and grab a seat.
cards.why: This place has a serious following for good reason, and it closes at 3 so don't sleep on it.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Walk the Rodin sculpture garden outside, then head in to explore the rotating galleries at your own pace.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful campus is hard to beat on a mild Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Forest Spa Boutique
cards.address: 530 Ramona St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a treatment at Forest Spa Boutique on Ramona St. Book ahead if you can, or pop in and see what's available.
cards.why: After walking Stanford, a little pampering feels totally earned.

cards.name: Books to Blooms: A Garden Lover’s Party
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Finish the evening at Books to Blooms, a garden lover's party at 170 State St in Los Altos. It's free, it's tonight, and it's a fun low-pressure way to end the day.
cards.why: A free evening event with a good vibe is a solid way to close out a Monday.

cards.name: Roy's Station Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 197 Jackson St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a coffee and settle into the vibe at Roy's Station, a converted gas station with serious character. Order whatever's brewing and take your time.
cards.why: It's a cool space with good coffee, perfect for easing into a Monday.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Walk through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU. It's free, thought-provoking, and the kind of show that gives you something to talk about over lunch.
cards.why: Free art on a cool Monday afternoon is a solid move.

cards.name: J. Lohr Vineyards & Wines
cards.address: 1000 Lenzen Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Do a proper tasting flight at J. Lohr. The tasting room is relaxed and the staff actually know their stuff. No rush, no pretension.
cards.why: A 4.9-rated winery in San Jose that most locals sleep on is worth your Monday.

cards.name: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: 1649 Naglee Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden. Even in mid-April the blooms are coming in, and the paths are quiet on a weekday afternoon.
cards.why: It's one of those spots that actually makes San Jose feel beautiful.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch. Mix and match from the vendors inside, grab a taco or banh mi, and snag a patio seat if the weather holds up.
cards.why: A dozen food options under one roof means everyone wins.

cards.name: Action 剧本杀
cards.address: 920 Saratoga Ave # 212, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night at Action mystery room. It's an immersive script-killing experience where your group plays out a storyline together. Weird, fun, and nothing like a standard escape room.
cards.why: A Monday night needs something different, and this delivers.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio — the lower loop is beautiful and easy, or push up to the farm if you want more of a workout.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this perfect hiking weather before it warms up.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Monday morning meditation and mindfulness session in Los Altos to reset and decompress.
cards.why: Free, local, and a genuinely nice way to ease into the week.

cards.name: Kali Greek Kitchen
cards.address: 451 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Kali Greek Kitchen on California Ave — the menu is fresh, the vibe is relaxed, and the patio is worth sitting at if the sun's out.
cards.why: Solid Greek food that doesn't feel like a chain, and the California Ave stretch makes for a nice post-lunch stroll.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: Town and Country Village, 855 El Camino Real #74, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Books Inc. in Palo Alto — pick up something you've been meaning to read or just wander the shelves for a bit.
cards.why: A great indie bookstore that doesn't feel rushed, perfect for a slow Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Serenity Wellness Spa
cards.address: 200 California Ave Suite 190, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Treat yourself to a spa session at Serenity Wellness Spa on California Ave in Palo Alto — you're already in the neighborhood.
cards.why: A genuinely excellent wellness spot and a Monday afternoon here feels indulgent in the best way.

cards.name: Sandy Wool Lake - Gazebo
cards.address: 901 Downing Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a slow walk around Sandy Wool Lake. The gazebo is a solid spot to sit, breathe some cool air, and ease into the day before things get busy.
cards.why: Cool crisp morning, no rain, and you've got the place mostly to yourselves.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop into the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In at Milpitas Library. Even if you're a total beginner, it's a relaxed come-and-go creative hangout where you can pick up something new.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a surprisingly fun way to spend a Monday morning.

cards.name: Cajun Crack'n Milpitas
cards.address: 275 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Cajun Crack'n for lunch. Get the seafood boil and dig in with your hands. It's messy, it's worth it.
cards.why: This is exactly the kind of lunch that makes a Monday feel like a day off.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Catch the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library. It's a gentle, grounding session that's perfect for the early afternoon lull.
cards.why: Free, calm, and a genuinely good way to reset after lunch.

cards.name: Zen Haven Spa
cards.address: 1224 S Abel St #1224, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Zen Haven Spa with a massage or body treatment. Let the afternoon tension go before dinner.
cards.why: You just escaped a room and solved puzzles. You've earned this.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out Fists of Fury at King Library, an exhibition exploring the history and cultural impact of martial arts in America — free, downtown, and genuinely interesting.
cards.why: Free admission and a topic that's way more compelling than it sounds on paper.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to Japantown and get a bowl of udon at Gombei, then pick up some mochi at Okayama Kobo and browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and lunch here is always worth it.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden. April is peak bloom season, so the colors and fragrance are at their best right now.
cards.why: It's one of the prettiest free spots in the whole South Bay and spring is the exact right time to go.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition in Santa Clara. It's a free arts show with enough visual trickery to keep you engaged for a solid hour.
cards.why: Free, visually interesting, and a great way to spend a cool afternoon without committing to anything heavy.

cards.name: Hungry Hound
cards.address: 1000 Lafayette St C, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Hungry Hound in Santa Clara for a quick bite before they close. It's a solid under-$15 spot worth trying.
cards.why: Good food, easy on the wallet, and a natural stopping point before the evening.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: End the day at Santana Row. Browse the boutiques, grab a seat at Sino or Straits for dinner, and enjoy the European boulevard vibe as the evening cools down.
cards.why: It's the best place in the South Bay to stretch a Monday evening into something that actually feels like a night out.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the paved creekside path at an easy pace, enjoy the shade from the trees, and take in how calm it is on a weekday morning.
cards.why: Cool, crisp morning air makes this the ideal time to be outside before the day warms up.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee Co.
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #10, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a well-crafted coffee and something to eat at this Lincoln Ave gem before it closes up for the afternoon.
cards.why: Closes at 3 PM, so catching it at lunch is the move.

cards.name: Student Art Showcase at King Library
cards.blurb: Browse the student art showcase at King Library and see what SJSU students have been working on this semester.
cards.why: Free, genuinely interesting, and a good way to spend an hour without committing to anything.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square for a mix-and-match late lunch from the vendors inside. Grab tacos, banh mi, or ramen and find a patio seat if the weather holds up.
cards.why: A dozen options under one roof means everyone gets exactly what they're in the mood for.

cards.name: XYC Massage
cards.address: 19501 Stevens Creek Blvd #103, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a massage and let the morning walk and city time fully melt away.
cards.why: Open until 10 PM, so there's zero rush to be anywhere else after this.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the creek path. It's shaded, flat, and a genuinely nice way to wake up before the day picks up.
cards.why: Cool morning air and an easy trail is a solid way to kick things off.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Swing through the Campbell Farmers Market and browse the vendors. Good spot to pick up snacks, fresh produce, or just wander with a coffee in hand.
cards.why: It's free, it's lively, and Monday mornings here are surprisingly low-key and pleasant.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge painting your own piece. Walk in, pick a ceramic, and get into it. No artistic talent required.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun afternoon activity that doesn't require any planning ahead.

cards.name: Aqui Campbell
cards.address: 201 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Aqui Campbell for lunch. Get the Cal-Mex classics and grab a table on the patio if the weather holds.
cards.why: It's a Campbell staple and the perfect landing spot after the farmers market.

cards.name: Arbor Day Celebration & Ceremonial Tree Planting
cards.blurb: Catch the Arbor Day Celebration and ceremonial tree planting in Saratoga. It's a free outdoor community event and a nice way to get some fresh air mid-afternoon.
cards.why: It's happening today and free outdoor events like this are always worth a look.

cards.name: Anchors Fish & Chips And Seafood Grill
cards.address: 14441 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Anchors Fish and Chips in Saratoga for an early dinner. Order the fish and chips and eat in. Straightforward, solid, satisfying.
cards.why: You're already in Saratoga and this place consistently delivers on the basics.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 50 University Ave d200, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night with a scoop at Salt and Straw in Los Gatos. The flavors rotate but they're always interesting. Worth the splurge.
cards.why: It's the best way to close out a Monday.

cards.name: Café San José
cards.address: 1583A Meridian Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and breakfast at Café San José, a cozy neighborhood spot perfect for easing into the day.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so get there early and enjoy it while it's fresh and quiet.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library at SJSU and check out the Royal Chicano Air Force poster collection. The work is vivid, political, and genuinely worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free art with real cultural weight, and it's a great way to spend a cool April afternoon indoors.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Wander the Campbell Farmers Market and browse the stalls — local produce, flowers, baked goods, and the kind of vendors worth chatting up.
cards.why: Monday morning means smaller crowds and a relaxed pace through the market.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Take a slow walk through the San Jose Municipal Rose Garden. April is when things really start blooming, so the timing is great.
cards.why: One of the best free spots in the South Bay and genuinely beautiful in spring.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and mix and match from the vendors: tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: It's the easiest, most satisfying lunch stop in downtown San Jose.

cards.name: Restful SPA
cards.address: 991 Saratoga Ave #150, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Restful SPA on Saratoga Ave with a massage or treatment. A solid way to transition out of the afternoon.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, this is exactly the right move.

cards.name: First Look: Quixote Nuevo
cards.blurb: Catch the First Look reading of Quixote Nuevo at Santa Clara University tonight. It's a free theater event and a genuinely fun way to end the evening.
cards.why: Free theater on a Monday night is a rare and easy yes.

cards.name: Keke's Breakfast Cafe
cards.address: 311 S Mathilda Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Keke's with a big breakfast spread — eggs, pancakes, the works. It's a proper sit-down breakfast spot that sets you up right for the day.
cards.why: Cool morning, nowhere to be — this is exactly the kind of slow breakfast Monday was made for.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Head over to the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU. It's a free art show worth poking around for an hour or so before lunch.
cards.why: Free art on a Monday is an easy yes, especially with no crowds to fight.

cards.name: Sunnyvale Farmers Market
cards.address: Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Walk Murphy Ave and browse the Sunnyvale Farmers Market stalls. Pick up some local produce, snack on samples, and just wander at whatever pace feels right.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and the morning crowd on a weekday is actually pretty chill.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Star Thai Massage on Murphy Ave. Book ahead if you can, but walk-ins are often available on weekday afternoons.
cards.why: After a full morning of walking and an escape room, your body will thank you.

cards.name: Toby’s Board Game Club 一游未尽桌游吧
cards.address: 10805 N Wolfe Rd #106, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Toby's Board Game Club. Grab a game off the shelf, order some drinks, and settle in for a couple hours of low-key fun.
cards.why: It's a genuinely great spot to end a Monday without it feeling like a waste of an evening.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 3051 Tasman Dr, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid cup at 1 Oz Coffee — good vibes, reasonable prices, and a great way to ease into the day before things open up.
cards.why: Every good Monday needs a proper coffee stop first.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Monday Morning Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos — a genuinely low-key way to reset your head for the week.
cards.why: Free, calming, and a nice counterweight to a busy Monday.

cards.name: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio
cards.address: 632 Fremont Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Halo Pottery in Los Altos and get your hands dirty — it's a laid-back studio session where you can try your hand at throwing clay or working on a piece.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun thing to do on a slow Monday morning before the place closes at 1.

cards.name: Sushi Boat
cards.address: 1026 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Sushi Boat in Sunnyvale for lunch — grab a seat and let the boats come to you, or order off the menu if you want something specific.
cards.why: Honestly one of the best sushi spots in the area and you'll want to eat here at least once.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out the State of Illusion Exhibition at Santa Clara University — walk through the installations and see what the artists are doing with perception and space.
cards.why: It's free, it's cool, and afternoons like this are made for wandering through art.

cards.name: Siam Pavilion Thai Massage
cards.address: 830 Stewart Dr STE 172, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Siam Pavilion Thai Massage in Sunnyvale and let someone work out whatever tension you've been carrying around.
cards.why: The perfect way to transition from afternoon to evening before dinner.

cards.name: Hatched
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real #161, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Hatched, a solid breakfast spot in Palo Alto — grab a proper sit-down meal before hitting the trail.
cards.why: Good food before a hike is always the right call.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Lace up and tackle The Dish Trail — the climb is real but the views over the Bay and foothills are worth every step, and the cool morning temps make it ideal hiking weather.
cards.why: 63°F and partly cloudy is basically perfect hiking weather.

cards.name: Living with OCD
cards.address: 866 Campus Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to the Living with OCD exhibition at Stanford and spend some time with it properly — it's the kind of thing that makes you think long after you leave.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a great way to slow down after the hike.

cards.name: Basquia Bakery
cards.address: 1775 E Bayshore Rd, East Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Basquia Bakery for a late lunch bite and something sweet — it's a genuine neighborhood gem worth the stop.
cards.why: You've earned a pastry after that hike.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Cantor Arts Center at Stanford — spend time with the Rodin sculptures outside, then wander through the galleries at whatever pace feels right.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a Monday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: W Spa
cards.address: 441 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day with a treatment at W Spa in downtown Palo Alto — book a massage or just use it as a proper decompression session.
cards.why: Best way to close out a day that started with a big hike.

cards.name: Antoine's Cookie Shop Palo Alto
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real #15, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: End the night with a cookie (or several) from Antoine's Cookie Shop before they close — the kind of dessert that makes you text someone about it.
cards.why: A light, fun finish to the day without committing to a full dinner out.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 101 Forest Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Philz with one of their hand-crafted pour-overs. Pick your roast, grab a table, and ease into the day.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a Monday.

cards.name: Elizabeth F. Gamble Garden
cards.address: 1431 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the formal gardens, rose beds, and wisteria-draped paths at Elizabeth F. Gamble Garden. It's quiet, beautifully kept, and free.
cards.why: Cool mornings are honestly perfect for a garden walk before the crowds show up.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Check out Erika Chong Shuch's immersive new installation at Stanford. It's a thoughtful, experiential work that rewards slowing down and actually sitting with it.
cards.why: Free, genuinely interesting, and not the kind of thing you see at every gallery.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into SWEET55 for Swiss chocolates and confections. Pick up a few truffles or a chocolate bar to snack on — the quality is serious.
cards.why: You deserve something good after a morning of walking and art.

cards.name: Woven Narratives: Textiles as Living Archives
cards.address: 488 Escondido Mall, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to the Woven Narratives exhibition at the Haas Arts Center to see textiles treated as living historical documents. The detail work is worth lingering over.
cards.why: It's a genuinely compelling way to spend an afternoon on campus.

cards.name: Watercourse Way Bath House Spa
cards.address: 165 Channing Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a private soaking tub session at Watercourse Way. Bring a book, decompress, and let the hot water do its thing.
cards.why: Nothing resets a Monday like a good soak.

cards.name: Naschmarkt - Palo Alto
cards.address: 2323 Birch St, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Naschmarkt for Austrian-inspired European cooking. Order the schnitzel if it's on the menu and lean into something a little different for a weeknight.
cards.why: It's a reliably great dinner spot that doesn't feel like every other Palo Alto restaurant.

cards.name: Café San José
cards.address: 1583A Meridian Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and breakfast at Café San José, a cozy neighborhood spot perfect for easing into the day.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio County Park & Open Space Preserve
cards.address: Cupertino, CA, USA
cards.blurb: Rancho San Antonio is the real deal for a midday hike — take the lower loop past the farm, or push up to Regnart Creek trail if you want to actually break a sweat.
cards.why: The clouds should clear by noon and the hills will look great after the cool morning.

cards.name: Rio Adobe Southwest Cafe
cards.address: 10525 S De Anza Blvd #100, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Rio Adobe is a solid post-hike lunch stop — get the tacos or a burrito bowl and eat outside if it warms up enough.
cards.why: Under $15 and genuinely good Southwest-style food, which is exactly what you want after a trail.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: The State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU is one of those shows that messes with your perception in the best way — give yourself time to actually sit with each piece instead of rushing through.
cards.why: It's free and a great way to spend the afternoon without fighting any weather.

cards.name: MeloMelo Coconut Dessert
cards.address: 19652 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: MeloMelo in Cupertino does coconut desserts done right — get a bowl with the classic toppings and take your time with it as a proper evening wind-down.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key ending to the day and the kind of spot you'll want to come back to.

cards.name: Cloud9 Coffee
cards.address: 1901 Embarcadero Rd #103, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Cloud9 Coffee with a great cup and a slow wake-up before the day kicks in.
cards.why: One of the best coffee spots in Palo Alto and it closes at 3, so morning is the move.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Lace up and do the full Dish loop for sweeping views of the Bay and the foothills. The climb is worth it and the trail is well-marked the whole way.
cards.why: Cool temps this morning make it perfect hiking weather before it gets busy.

cards.name: Living with OCD
cards.address: 866 Campus Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: This free exhibition at Stanford digs into the reality of living with OCD through personal stories, research, and immersive displays. Give yourself time to read everything.
cards.why: It's genuinely thought-provoking and free, a rare combo.

cards.name: Yummy Future Coffee, Matcha & Tea
cards.address: 170 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a matcha latte or a fun seasonal tea drink at Yummy Future on University Ave.
cards.why: Great way to rest your feet after a full morning and afternoon on campus.

cards.name: Laura Body Work Massage Therapy & WELLNESS
cards.address: 3239 El Camino Real #220, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Laura Body Work to close out the day right. After a hike and a few hours of walking around Stanford, your legs will thank you.
cards.why: Legit wellness spot that'll leave you feeling way better than when you walked in.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Start the morning with this optical illusion exhibition on the SCU campus. Walk through the installations, mess with your own perception a little, and take your time with whatever stops you cold.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and genuinely trippy first thing in the morning.

cards.name: Taking the Stage: 50 Years Ago at the Mayer
cards.blurb: Right after, check out this retrospective on the Mayer Theatre's first 50 years. It's a quick walk and a cool slice of local performing arts history you probably didn't know existed.
cards.why: It's free, it's right there, and it pairs perfectly with the first stop as a low-key morning arts double.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market and graze your way through lunch. Mix and match from tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever catches your eye, then grab a patio seat with your coffee.
cards.why: Best low-commitment lunch in downtown San Jose when you want options and no one at the table can agree.

cards.name: Gonzales-Peralta Adobe & Fallon House Historic Site
cards.address: 175 Sharks Wy, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into the Fallon House Historic Site and walk through one of San Jose's oldest surviving homes. The adobe and the Victorian house together tell a weird, layered story about early California that actually holds your attention.
cards.why: It's a genuinely underrated history stop that most locals have driven past a hundred times without going in.

cards.name: Body Rest Massage Wellness Center
cards.address: 621 E Campbell Ave #11e, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Body Rest Wellness Center and decompress for a bit. Good pick for an afternoon reset after a full morning of walking around.
cards.why: A Monday afternoon massage hits differently when you actually planned for it.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at The Midwife and the Baker, a beloved Mountain View spot known for fresh-baked goods and great coffee. Grab a pastry, settle in, and ease into the day.
cards.why: Best pastries in the area and they close at 2, so morning is the move.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Head out to Rancho San Antonio for a solid morning hike. The lower loop is easy and scenic, or push up to the farm overlook if you want a real workout.
cards.why: Mild temps and a low rain chance make this a great window to get outside.

cards.name: Evvia Estiatorio
cards.address: 420 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Evvia Estiatorio, a proper Greek kitchen in Palo Alto. Think wood-grilled meats, fresh salads, and warm pita. It's a sit-down lunch worth the tab.
cards.why: It's one of the best lunches in the South Bay and fits perfectly in your midday window.

cards.name: Palo Alto Art Center
cards.address: 1313 Newell Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to the Palo Alto Art Center for a couple of hours browsing the current exhibitions. Free admission, rotating shows, and a genuinely nice space to spend an afternoon.
cards.why: Great way to spend the early afternoon without spending a dime.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Arisa Thai Massage in Los Altos and let someone else do the work after the hike. Their therapeutic massage is the kind of thing you'll wish you'd done sooner.
cards.why: Post-hike, post-lunch, your body will absolutely thank you.

cards.name: Monday Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with the Monday Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos. A free, guided way to decompress after a full day out.
cards.why: Ending a Monday with something this grounding is hard to beat.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day on the trails at Alum Rock Park before the crowds show up. The foothills are peaceful in the morning and the cool air makes it perfect hiking weather.
cards.why: Cool temps and low rain chance make this a great morning outdoor stretch.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop into the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In at the library. Whether you're a stitcher or just curious, it's a chill creative hangout with friendly folks.
cards.why: It's happening today and it's free, so why not check it out.

cards.name: Ushiya Grill
cards.address: 1792 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Ushiya Grill for lunch. It's a solid spot for a satisfying midday meal before the afternoon kicks in.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so this is the window to catch it.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Qi Gong at Milpitas Library is a calm, grounding class that pairs movement with breath work. Great reset after lunch.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a nice way to spend a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: The Hot Spot Studios Milpitas / Bay Area
cards.address: 30 S Abbott Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Check out The Hot Spot Studios for a creative afternoon. It's an art and entertainment studio space worth exploring when you have time to wander.
cards.why: A 5-star local gem that most people sleep on.

cards.name: Somisomi
cards.address: 548 Barber Ln, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Somisomi for one of their taiyaki soft serve cones. The fish-shaped waffle cone stuffed with ice cream is the move.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun treat and the perfect afternoon pick-me-up.

cards.name: Roy's Station Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 197 Jackson St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Roy's Station, a neighborhood coffee shop with a laid-back vibe. Grab a coffee and something to eat before heading into the day.
cards.why: A solid local spot to ease into a Monday morning.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: This exhibition at King Library digs into Ricardo Favela's poster collection from the Royal Chicano Air Force. Walk through the prints, read the history, and take your time with it.
cards.why: Free, visually striking, and genuinely worth an hour of your morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Fists of Fury is also in King Library, so swing through while you're already there. It's a different lens on Asian American history through martial arts culture.
cards.why: You're already in the building, and it's a completely different subject matter worth seeing.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to San Pedro Square Market for lunch. Hit a few different vendors, grab a table on the patio, and take your pick from tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good.
cards.why: Best lunch spot in downtown for when you can't agree on just one thing.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along the Guadalupe River Park trail. It cuts right through downtown and feels a lot more peaceful than you'd expect for the middle of the city.
cards.why: Cool temps today make this a perfect afternoon walk without breaking a sweat.

cards.name: Tranquility Day Spa (Capitol Ave)
cards.address: 1671 N Capitol Ave, San Jose, CA 95132, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at Tranquility Day Spa and let the afternoon slow down. A massage or facial after a morning of walking around is a solid call.
cards.why: A Monday afternoon spa visit hits different when the city is still at work.

cards.name: Mio Vicino
cards.address: 1290 Benton St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Drive over to Mio Vicino in Santa Clara for dinner. It's a cozy Italian spot, good for pasta and a glass of wine to close out the day.
cards.why: Exactly the kind of low-key dinner a good Monday deserves.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the creek trail. It's shaded, flat, and easy — just a good way to get moving before the day picks up.
cards.why: Cool morning temps are perfect for this.

cards.name: Academic Coffee
cards.address: 499 S Second St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over and check out the Academic Coffee spot at 499 S Second — wait, this is Academic Coffee already. Let's get you to the King Library exhibition instead. Fists of Fury is a visual history of martial arts in America with some genuinely cool archival material.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and walkable from where you'll already be.

cards.name: Euro Market
cards.address: 980 El Camino Real #300, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Euro Market is a fun browse — Eastern European and imported goods you won't find at a regular grocery store. Good for picking up something interesting to take home.
cards.why: It's a genuinely different kind of afternoon stop.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square and build your own lunch from whichever vendors are calling to you — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: Best low-key lunch spot in downtown San Jose and you're already in the neighborhood.

cards.name: Svetlana’s Massage
cards.address: 15066 Los Gatos Almaden Rd Ste 120-A, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Svetlana's and actually decompress. A Monday afternoon slot is often the easiest time to get in.
cards.why: Five-star massage on a Monday is a very good use of your time.

cards.name: Easy Breezy Frozen Yogurt Los Gatos
cards.address: 307 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at Easy Breezy Frozen Yogurt on North Santa Cruz Ave. Load up your cup and sit outside if the evening stays mild.
cards.why: Simple, low-key ending to a full day.

cards.name: Venture Coffee
cards.address: 16845 Hicks Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Venture Coffee, one of those places that takes the craft seriously. Grab a well-pulled espresso or a pour-over and ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a great cup of coffee to kick things off right.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake for a late-morning walk around the lake. The trails are easy, the scenery is calm, and the cool morning air makes it genuinely pleasant to just stroll and decompress.
cards.why: A 61-degree morning with light cloud cover is honestly perfect for a walk.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge on N Santa Cruz Ave and paint a piece of pottery. You pick a ceramic piece, they give you the paints, and you go for it. No experience needed, zero pressure.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key way to spend a couple hours that doesn't feel like a typical lunch activity.

cards.name: Water Tower Kitchen
cards.address: 201 Orchard City Dr, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Water Tower Kitchen in Campbell for lunch. It's a solid sit-down spot with a good menu, solid drinks, and a comfortable vibe for a relaxed midday meal.
cards.why: You'll want a proper lunch after a creative morning.

cards.name: Narai Thai spa
cards.address: 15466 Los Gatos Blvd #209, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at Narai Thai spa on Los Gatos Blvd. A Thai massage or foot treatment in the early afternoon is a genuinely good use of a Monday when the rest of the world is grinding.
cards.why: Monday afternoon spa time hits different when you're actually doing it.

cards.name: High Tea & Book Tasting
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at the High Tea and Book Tasting at 110 E Main St in Los Gatos. You'll browse books, sip tea, and settle into a cozy, unhurried late afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, it's today only, and it's a lovely way to land after a full day.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 19439 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a cup at Philz — order a Tesora or Mint Mojito and grab a table by the window while the morning is still cool.
cards.why: A slow Monday morning coffee is the best kind.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the Japanese gardens at Hakone, duck into the bamboo grove, and take your time on the upper terraces where it gets genuinely quiet.
cards.why: Cool, overcast mornings are honestly the best time to be here.

cards.name: Mint Leaf Cuisine
cards.address: 14420 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Mint Leaf on Big Basin Way for lunch. It's an Indian spot with solid lunch service, so let them take care of you before the afternoon crowds.
cards.why: It's steps from Hakone and one of the better lunch spots in the village.

cards.name: La Sunflower
cards.address: 20688 4th St, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into La Sunflower for a treatment or just browse what they offer. It's a small wellness spot in Saratoga Village worth checking out on a slow Monday.
cards.why: A low-key Monday afternoon is the perfect time to actually do the self-care thing.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Los Gatos Creek Trail for an easy, flat walk along the water. The path is shaded and calm, great for clearing your head after lunch.
cards.why: Temps stay mild today and the creek trail never feels crowded on weekdays.

cards.name: The Bywater
cards.address: 532 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into The Bywater for an early dinner with some New Orleans-inspired food and solid cocktails. It opens at 3 but hits its stride around 5.
cards.why: Great food, relaxed vibe, and you beat the dinner rush going in this early.

cards.name: Oakmont Sandwiches & Coffee
cards.address: 19998 Homestead Rd Suite C, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with coffee and a sandwich at this Cupertino neighborhood spot on Homestead — grab a table and ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a locals-only kind of place and the perfect low-key way to kick off a Monday.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Fremont Older for some solid foothill hiking with sweeping views. The upper trails open up fast and most people have no idea this place exists.
cards.why: Cool temps and light cloud cover make this the ideal morning for a hike without breaking a sweat.

cards.name: Safar By Karimi
cards.address: 19930 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Clean up and head to Safar By Karimi for lunch — it's a small, well-loved Persian spot on Stevens Creek with flavors you won't forget.
cards.why: One of the most underrated lunch spots in Cupertino and it closes at 3, so catch it while you can.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing by the State of Illusion Exhibition at Santa Clara University — it's a free, thought-provoking show worth a slow walk-through.
cards.why: Free art on a Monday afternoon is always a good move, and this one is actually worth your time.

cards.name: Saigon Spa
cards.address: 2055 Grant Rd Ste 100, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a proper spa session at Saigon Spa in Los Altos. Book a massage and let someone else do the work for a change.
cards.why: After hiking and gallery-hopping, this is exactly the right way to end the day.

cards.name: GIWA
cards.address: 1494 Halford Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at GIWA in Santa Clara. Grab a coffee and something to eat and ease into the day at a solid neighborhood spot.
cards.why: Good food and a chill vibe make it a great way to kick things off.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out the State of Illusion Exhibition. Walk through and let the optical trickery mess with your head a little. Free admission, no rush.
cards.why: It's a genuinely cool free exhibition and the cooler morning temps make it perfect timing.

cards.name: Live Oak Park
cards.address: 641 Moreland Way, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Live Oak Park for a midday walk. It's a relaxed green space good for stretching your legs after a couple hours indoors.
cards.why: Mild weather like this is exactly what a park stroll is made for.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through Main Street Cupertino for lunch. Plenty of Asian restaurants and casual spots to pick from along the outdoor plaza.
cards.why: It's a great low-key afternoon lunch spot with enough variety that everyone finds something.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Head to King Library in San Jose for the Patient No More Exhibition. It's a thought-provoking free show worth taking your time with.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a good counterpoint to the lighter morning art.

cards.name: Elements Massage
cards.address: 308 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Elements Massage in Sunnyvale. Book a session and let the afternoon soreness disappear.
cards.why: A Monday evening massage is one of the best ways to reset for the week.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a hike through Rancho San Antonio. The lower meadow loop is an easy, gorgeous warmup, or push up toward PG&E Trail if you want to actually break a sweat.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the ideal window before it warms up.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to Stanford's Educational Farm and get your hands dirty. Weekday volunteer sessions are low-key and genuinely satisfying, whether you're weeding beds or helping with whatever the farm needs that morning.
cards.why: It's a surprisingly fun way to spend a Monday and you leave feeling like you actually did something good.

cards.name: Urfa Bistro
cards.address: 233 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Urfa Bistro on State Street in Los Altos. Turkish comfort food done right — think kebabs, mezze plates, and warm bread. Sit inside or snag a spot on the street-facing side.
cards.why: It's a solid local lunch spot that doesn't get old.

cards.name: Paris, City of Innovation: 19th-century Universal Expositions as seen through the Roxane Debuisson Collection of Paris History
cards.address: 557 Escondido Mall, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Check out the Paris Universal Expositions exhibit at Stanford's Cantor Arts Center. The Roxane Debuisson collection is a deep dive into 19th-century Paris through posters, objects, and ephemera that actually make history feel tactile.
cards.why: It's a genuinely interesting exhibit and the Cantor is a beautiful space to spend an afternoon.

cards.name: A & First Acupuncture and Massage
cards.address: 116 S Sunnyvale Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a session at A & First Acupuncture and Massage in Sunnyvale. Book ahead if you can, or check availability when you arrive.
cards.why: After hiking and volunteering, your body will thank you.

cards.name: Monday Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Cap the night with the Monday Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos. It's a calm, no-pressure way to decompress after a full day.
cards.why: A Monday meditation is kind of the perfect way to ease into the week with intention.

cards.name: Tylan cafe
cards.address: 2056 N Capitol Ave, San Jose, CA 95132, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Tylan Cafe with coffee and something to eat before the morning gets going.
cards.why: Every good day needs a solid first stop and this one delivers.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop into the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In at the library — bring a project or just show up and see what it's all about.
cards.why: It's a free, chill way to spend a Monday morning with people who actually know what they're doing.

cards.name: Knit & Crochet Circle at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Head into the Knit and Crochet Circle right after — grab a seat, pick up needles, and settle in for a relaxed afternoon at the library.
cards.why: Two back-to-back library events means you're not scrambling across town all day.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library for some slow, intentional movement and a mental reset.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and honestly kind of perfect for a Monday.

cards.name: IBU Taproom & Bottle Shop
cards.address: 1422 Dempsey Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by IBU Taproom and Bottle Shop for a cold pint and some afternoon downtime before the evening kicks in.
cards.why: Good beer, good vibe, and you've earned a drink after a full afternoon.

cards.name: Yogurtland Milpitas
cards.address: 752 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Yogurtland Milpitas for a customized froyo bowl to wrap up the evening on a sweet note.
cards.why: It's a satisfying low-key dessert stop that won't slow you down.

cards.name: EkuEku Bakery
cards.address: 4270 Albany Dr, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at EkuEku Bakery with fresh pastries and coffee to get the day going.
cards.why: Best way to start a Monday is a great bakery, not a drive-through.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Walk through the Patient No More Exhibition at King Library — it's a striking visual collection that's worth taking your time with.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely good use of a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: While you're already at King Library, catch the Fists of Fury exhibition on the same visit — two shows in one building.
cards.why: You're already there, and it's a completely different subject matter worth seeing.

cards.name: Suspiro
cards.address: 3060 Olsen Dr Suite 20, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Suspiro for a proper sit-down lunch. Try the local favorites and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's a solid mid-range spot that deserves more than a quick stop.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk through the San Jose Municipal Rose Garden while it's still in peak spring bloom. Stroll the paths, take some photos, and enjoy the quiet.
cards.why: April is exactly when this place is at its best.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing by the State of Illusion Exhibition in Santa Clara before it closes for the evening. It's a free show worth the detour.
cards.why: Free art exhibitions on a Monday afternoon don't come around often enough.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with an easy walk along the creek trail. It's shaded, flat, and peaceful — exactly what a Monday morning should feel like.
cards.why: Cool temps and a 60°F morning make this the perfect time to be outside before it gets busy.

cards.name: Pure Massage Therapy
cards.address: 116 E Campbell Ave Suite 7, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Drop in for a massage on Campbell Ave. Book a session and let someone work out whatever the weekend left behind.
cards.why: A weekday morning is the best time to actually get an appointment without the weekend rush.

cards.name: Aqui Campbell
cards.address: 201 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Aqui — order a burrito bowl or one of their Cal-Mex plates and sit on the patio if the sun comes out.
cards.why: It's a Campbell staple and the food is genuinely good for a weekday lunch.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library in San Jose. It digs into the history of martial arts in Asian American cinema and pop culture.
cards.why: It's free and the kind of exhibit you'll actually talk about afterward.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Walk the Patient No More exhibition while you're still at King Library. It covers disability rights history with photography and archival material worth taking your time with.
cards.why: You're already in the building and it's a genuinely compelling collection.

cards.name: Creekside Park
cards.address: 10455 Miller Ave, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a stroll through Creekside Park in Cupertino. Low key, green, and a good way to stretch your legs after a few hours of gallery time.
cards.why: A quick outdoor reset before dinner feels great on a mild evening like this.

cards.name: Heritage Pub and Chop House
cards.address: 15 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Heritage Pub and Chop House in Los Gatos for dinner. Order something off the chop house menu and settle in at the bar or a table on the strip.
cards.why: Los Gatos on a Monday night has a relaxed vibe and Heritage delivers a solid dinner without the weekend crowd.

cards.name: Park-It Market
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: The Park-It Market is happening on Main St today. Walk the stalls, browse whatever's out, and just enjoy the downtown Los Gatos vibe without an agenda.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and it's happening right now.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge on Santa Cruz Ave and paint a piece of pottery. You pick the piece, you pick the design, and they fire it for you to pick up later.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to spend an afternoon that doesn't feel like a touristy activity.

cards.name: The Happy Hound of Los Gatos
cards.address: 15899 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at The Happy Hound. Quick, good, and easy on the wallet. Order whatever's on the board and eat.
cards.why: No overthinking lunch on a Monday.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake County Park and walk the loop around the lake. The cloud cover keeps it cool and the trail is easy enough that you can actually have a conversation while walking.
cards.why: A cool, overcast afternoon is honestly ideal weather for a lakeside walk.

cards.name: Restore Hyper Wellness
cards.address: 15557 Union Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Restore Hyper Wellness in Los Gatos. Try compression therapy or red light if you haven't before. It's a solid reset after a full day on your feet.
cards.why: Your legs will thank you after the lake loop.

cards.name: Stackers Pizzeria
cards.address: 18572 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Stackers Pizzeria in Saratoga. Get a pizza, get comfortable, and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: It's genuinely great pizza and the right note to end the day on.

cards.name: Sweet Maple
cards.address: 20010 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Sweet Maple does breakfast and brunch right — settle in for a solid morning meal before the day gets going.
cards.why: A great rated spot that opens early and sets the tone for a good Monday.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the terraced Japanese gardens, find a quiet bench by the koi pond, and just breathe for a bit. It's genuinely peaceful.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect window to walk the grounds without breaking a sweat.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club at the Los Gatos library for a relaxed, community-driven chat session — no pressure, just good conversation.
cards.why: It's free, welcoming, and a genuinely fun way to meet locals on a slow Monday morning.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Petroglyph is a ceramics studio where you pick a piece and paint it yourself. It's surprisingly absorbing and you leave with something to show for it.
cards.why: Low-key, creative, and a fun way to spend a couple hours without feeling like you're doing 'touristy' stuff.

cards.name: Barnes & Noble
cards.address: 122 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Barnes and Noble on North Santa Cruz Ave. Grab a coffee from the in-store cafe and flip through whatever catches your eye.
cards.why: Los Gatos has a great walkable strip and this is a solid way to wind down the afternoon.

cards.name: Spa Rituals Wellness
cards.address: 340 Broadway Suite 7 ( 2nd floor, Saratoga Springs, NY 12866, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at Spa Rituals Wellness and let the afternoon melt away. A great spot to decompress mid-day when slots are easier to get.
cards.why: Ending the active part of the day with a spa visit is just a smart move.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 10660 S De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Yiassoo in Cupertino serves up Greek food worth sitting down for. Order the classics and take your time with it.
cards.why: It closes at 8 so you have plenty of time, and it's a satisfying way to finish off the day.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls at the Cupertino Farmers Market on Stevens Creek, pick up some fresh fruit, sample whatever looks good, and just ease into the morning at your own pace.
cards.why: It's a Monday morning and this is the most laid-back way to start the day.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Fremont Older Open Space for a solid mid-morning hike. The foothill views open up fast and the trails stay quiet on weekdays.
cards.why: You'll have the whole preserve basically to yourself on a Monday.

cards.name: Orange Square
cards.address: 20343 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Orange Square on Stevens Creek. Good food, solid vibe, and just the right stop after a morning hike.
cards.why: You earned a real sit-down lunch.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU. It's a free arts show worth a slow walk-through.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a nice way to spend a weekday afternoon.

cards.name: MeloMelo Coconut Dessert
cards.address: 19652 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into MeloMelo Coconut Dessert on Stevens Creek for an afternoon treat. Their coconut-based desserts are seriously good and the menu is unique.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you'll want to come back to after the first visit.

cards.name: Perfect healing massage center（836 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale 94087)
cards.address: 836 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Unwind at Perfect Healing Massage Center in Sunnyvale with a session to shake off the hiking legs from this morning.
cards.why: A massage at the end of an active day is never the wrong call.

cards.name: Cẩm Hưng Sandwich and Coffee
cards.address: 903 Reed Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a banh mi sandwich and Vietnamese coffee from this Sunnyvale gem — it's a proper breakfast, not just a snack.
cards.why: Solid way to kick off the day without overthinking it.

cards.name: Orchard Heritage Park (Apricot Orchard Barn)
cards.address: 560 E Remington Dr, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the orchard barn and get a feel for what Sunnyvale looked like before the tech campuses took over. It's a surprisingly interesting slice of local history.
cards.why: Low-key and genuinely worth an hour of your morning.

cards.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Dig into the Steve Jobs in Exile exhibition at the Computer History Museum — it covers the NeXT and Pixar years, which honestly might be the most interesting chapter of his whole story.
cards.why: This one's worth paying for if you have any interest in tech history.

cards.name: Orange Square
cards.address: 20343 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Orange Square is the spot for a proper late lunch. Grab a table and order something off their menu before the afternoon hits.
cards.why: Good food and a chill vibe to decompress after the museum.

cards.name: Gourmet Works
cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Gourmet Works on Main Street Los Altos. It's a culinary shop packed with quality cookware, gadgets, and pantry finds — the kind of place where you always end up buying something you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Way more fun than it sounds for anyone who likes food or cooking.

cards.name: Stellar Massage for Women Only
cards.address: 422 S Murphy Ave suite no 3, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Asian Wellness in Sunnyvale. Book ahead if you can, but walk-ins are often available on weekday afternoons.
cards.why: Best way to turn a good day into a great evening.

cards.name: Tong Sui Desserts & Drinks (Sunnyvale)
cards.address: 927 E Arques Ave #151, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night with dessert drinks at Tong Sui in Sunnyvale. Order the grass jelly or one of their warm tong sui bowls and just sit with it.
cards.why: Perfect low-key ending to a solid Monday.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Midwife and the Baker is a Mountain View morning institution — grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Hard to beat a bakery this good on a cool morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio — the loop out to the farm overlook is a solid two hours without being brutal.
cards.why: Cool temps make this the perfect window for a hike before it gets busy.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours volunteering at Stanford's Educational Farm, working in the fields alongside others on a weekday morning session.
cards.why: It's a genuinely satisfying way to spend time outside and give back.

cards.name: Katsu
cards.address: 859 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Katsu in Mountain View has an absurdly good reputation for a reason — try the local favorite and don't skip the sides.
cards.why: You'll want a real lunch after a morning of physical activity.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
