cards.blurb: Erika Chong Shuch's exhibition at Stanford is an immersive new work worth exploring slowly — give yourself time to sit with it.
cards.why: Free contemporary art at Stanford is always worth the stop.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Arisa Thai Massage in Los Altos and let someone sort out whatever the hike did to your legs.
cards.why: After a full morning outside, this is exactly what the afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Laasya Art Gallery
cards.address: 2225 E Bayshore Rd #100-7127, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing Laasya Art Gallery in Palo Alto, which carries eclectic work worth more than a quick glance.
cards.why: A relaxed gallery visit is a perfect low-key cap to an active day.

cards.name: Co Ba Dessert, Tea & Coffee
cards.address: 3730 N First St Ste. 110, San Jose, CA 95134, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and something sweet at Co Ba, a cozy Vietnamese cafe with tea, coffee, and desserts worth lingering over.
cards.why: It's a great low-key start before the day picks up.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop in on the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In and hang out with a creative crowd. You don't have to be an expert to join the vibe.
cards.why: A free, chill creative event that's actually happening today.

cards.name: Mil's Diner
cards.address: 36 S Abbott Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Mil's Diner for a solid sit-down lunch. Classic diner food done well, worth every bite.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so this is the perfect window to catch it.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library. It's gentle, grounding, and a nice afternoon reset.
cards.why: Free, no equipment needed, and a cool way to spend a quiet Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Clear Lake Spa – Massage in Milpitas
cards.address: 1766 Clear Lake Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Clear Lake Spa. Let the afternoon melt away before dinner.
cards.why: After a packed morning and afternoon, this is exactly the right move.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with the Patient No More Exhibition at King Library. Walk through the galleries and take your time with the artwork before the crowds show up.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely good way to start a Monday morning downtown.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market and build your own lunch from whatever looks good that day. Grab a seat on the patio if the sun's cooperating.
cards.why: A dozen vendors under one roof means everyone gets exactly what they want.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Stay in the library and check out the Royal Chicano Air Force poster collection. The work is vivid, political, and worth lingering over.
cards.why: You're already in the building, and this is a collection you won't see anywhere else.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Rose Garden. April is peak bloom season so the colors are ridiculous right now. Take your time looping the paths.
cards.why: Spring is the best possible time to be here and it's completely free.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Santana Row for a couple hours. Window shop the boutiques, grab a coffee if you need a pick-me-up, and scope out a dinner spot while you're at it.
cards.why: It's an easy, low-key afternoon stretch with plenty to look at and good people-watching.

cards.name: Game Kastle (Santa Clara)
cards.address: 1350 Coleman Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Game Kastle and browse the shelves. They carry board games, TCGs, and RPG supplies, so even if you're not buying, it's fun to dig through the inventory.
cards.why: A cool low-key stop that stretches the afternoon without requiring a reservation or a plan.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: End the night in Japantown. Get a bowl of udon at Gombei, pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo, and wander Jackson Street as the neighborhood settles into the evening.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a genuinely great dinner destination.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with an easy walk along the creek trail. The path is shaded, flat, and genuinely peaceful on a cool Monday morning.
cards.why: 60 degrees and mostly sunny is perfect trail weather before the day fills up.

cards.name: The Giving Pies
cards.address: 569 W Alma Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by for a late breakfast or pastry. The Giving Pies closes at 2 PM so hit it while it's still morning and the options are fresh.
cards.why: A local favorite with great food and a short window to catch it.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club at Los Gatos Library for some genuinely good conversation with a welcoming, mixed crowd.
cards.why: It's free, social, and a low-key way to spend a weekday morning with interesting people.

cards.name: Voyager Craft Coffee
cards.address: 111 W St John St Suite 100, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Stop in for a well-crafted coffee and decompress after the exhibition. Voyager does serious espresso in a clean, welcoming space.
cards.why: Best coffee stop in the area and a good spot to sit and recharge mid-afternoon.

cards.name: Bamboo Healthcare Spa Inc
cards.address: 871 Coleman Ave, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Book a late-afternoon treatment at Bamboo Healthcare Spa. It's a proper reset after a day of walking around.
cards.why: A perfect way to close out a Monday and actually feel good tonight.

cards.name: Orale Baja Mexican Restaurant
cards.address: 1750 S Winchester Blvd, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a table at Orale Baja and order something off the Mexican menu. It's casual, affordable, and hits the spot after a full day.
cards.why: Under $15 a person and solid food makes this an easy yes for dinner.

cards.name: Christy Donuts
cards.address: 1291 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a fresh donut and coffee at Christy Donuts, a local Milpitas staple worth the early stop.
cards.why: Best donuts in the area and they close at 4, so morning is the move.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop into the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In at Milpitas Library. Bring a project, meet some crafty regulars, or just watch and vibe.
cards.why: It's a chill, free creative hangout happening today.

cards.name: Kovai Cafe - Milpitas
cards.address: 181 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Kovai Cafe for lunch. Order something from their South Indian menu and take your time with it.
cards.why: Seriously good food and you deserve a proper sit-down meal mid-afternoon.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library. Gentle movement, breathing, and a solid reset for the afternoon.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a nice way to decompress on a cool Tuesday.

cards.name: THIÊN LONG SƠN - THE DRAGON MOUNTAIN
cards.address: 3215 Calaveras Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Take a walk up to Thiên Long Sơn, a striking hillside spot with views and a unique atmosphere worth exploring on foot.
cards.why: The cool weather is perfect for an outdoor wander before the evening.

cards.name: Clear Lake Spa – Massage in Milpitas
cards.address: 1766 Clear Lake Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Clear Lake Spa and let the afternoon tension go. It's a solid wellness stop with a great reputation.
cards.why: After a full day of moving around, this is exactly the right way to wind down.

cards.name: Tong Sui Desserts & Drinks (Milpitas)
cards.address: 265 W Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Tong Sui Desserts and Drinks with some warm or cold dessert drinks. Order a tong sui bowl and something boba-adjacent.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key ending to the day and the place is genuinely delicious.

cards.name: Café San José
cards.address: 1583A Meridian Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Café San José with coffee and a bite before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a cozy Willow Glen spot and the perfect low-key way to kick off a Tuesday.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out the Royal Chicano Air Force poster collection at King Library — it's a deep, colorful look at Chicano art and activism through Ricardo Favela's work.
cards.why: Free, genuinely interesting, and not the kind of thing you stumble into every day.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through Fists of Fury while you're still at King Library — it's a separate exhibition worth seeing back to back.
cards.why: You're already there, and this one stands on its own as a compelling show.

cards.name: Streetlight Records
cards.address: 980 S Bascom Ave, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Kill a solid hour flipping through vinyl and browsing the stacks at Streetlight Records on Bascom.
cards.why: One of the best record shops left in the Bay Area, period.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Santana Row. Walk the boulevard, grab a table for dinner at one of the spots along the strip, and let the evening wind down at a comfortable pace.
cards.why: It's the right vibe for a weeknight dinner with no agenda.

cards.name: T-Square Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 1102 Bird Ave. # 20, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at T-Square Coffee and Tea. Grab a coffee and something to eat, find a seat, and ease into the day at a pace that actually feels good.
cards.why: It's a great little spot to kick things off without rushing.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail while the morning is still crisp. The path runs along the creek under a solid canopy of trees, so even at 50 degrees it's pleasant once you get moving.
cards.why: Cool mornings and shaded trails are basically made for each other.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then spend time with the contemporary galleries inside. It's free and genuinely worth your time.
cards.why: This one flies under the radar and it really shouldn't.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 397 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by The Good Salad on Campbell Ave for lunch. Big, filling bowls with solid ingredients. Good for when you want something fresh after a morning out.
cards.why: You'll feel good about this one after a solid morning of walking and galleries.

cards.name: BE.STEAK.A
cards.address: 1887 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at BE.STEAK.A on Bascom Ave. This is a proper upscale dinner. Order the steak, take your time, and make an evening of it.
cards.why: After a full day, a serious sit-down dinner is exactly the right move.

cards.name: Blvd Coffee
cards.address: 15525 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a coffee at Blvd Coffee. Settle in, warm up, and ease into the morning before things get going.
cards.why: Good coffee and a relaxed vibe make it a solid way to kick off a cool morning.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Los Gatos Creek Trail for a morning walk along the creek. It's easy, shaded, and a genuinely nice stretch to clear your head before the day picks up.
cards.why: The weather today is perfect for a casual outdoor walk without working up a sweat.

cards.name: Body Rest Massage Wellness Center
cards.address: 621 E Campbell Ave #11e, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Body Rest Massage Wellness Center. A midday massage is a seriously underrated move on a slow Tuesday.
cards.why: Weekday appointments are easier to snag and you'll feel great for the rest of the afternoon.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 397 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at The Good Salad on Campbell Ave. Fresh, fast, and actually filling.
cards.why: It's one of the better lunch spots in Campbell and keeps things light after a massage.

cards.name: Drop-In Tech Help
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Swing by the Los Gatos Library for Drop-In Tech Help. Whether you've got a question about your phone, laptop, or anything else, the volunteers here actually know their stuff.
cards.why: Free and genuinely useful, especially if you've been putting off fixing something on your devices.

cards.name: High Tea & Book Tasting
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Head to the High Tea and Book Tasting at 110 E Main St. It's a fun, low-key afternoon event where you can browse books with tea in hand.
cards.why: It's a free event happening today and a pretty charming way to spend a Tuesday afternoon.

cards.name: CineLux Los Gatos Theatre
cards.address: 43 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Catch a film at CineLux Los Gatos Theatre to cap the night. It's a classic local movie house right in town.
cards.why: A movie is an easy, comfortable way to end a full day without having to think too hard.

cards.name: Puffin Donuts
cards.address: 1442 Pollard Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a stop at Puffin Donuts in Los Gatos. Grab a fresh donut and coffee and ease into the day at your own pace.
cards.why: It's a Tuesday morning and this place is worth the trip.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge in Los Gatos and paint a piece to take home. Pick something off the shelf, grab your glazes, and just make something — no experience needed.
cards.why: A fun, low-key creative stop that's genuinely enjoyable for adults.

cards.name: Mount Eden Vineyards
cards.address: 22020 Mt Eden Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Mount Eden Vineyards in Saratoga for a tasting. The views from up there are stunning and the wines are serious — this is the kind of winery visit you actually remember.
cards.why: 49 degrees in the morning means crisp, clear skies up on the mountain.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the trails around Vasona Lake in Los Gatos. The loop around the lake is easy and the water views are peaceful on a cool afternoon like this.
cards.why: The high of 65 is basically perfect for a lakeside walk.

cards.name: C Spa & Beauty
cards.address: 14567 Big Basin Wy c1, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at C Spa and Beauty on Big Basin Way in Saratoga and spend an hour actually unwinding before dinner.
cards.why: A Tuesday afternoon is the best time to get in without fighting a weekend crowd.

cards.name: Trattoria 360
cards.address: 360 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Trattoria 360 in Campbell. Order something off the pasta menu and take your time with a glass of wine — this place has the kind of relaxed vibe that works perfectly to close out the evening.
cards.why: The food is genuinely good and the atmosphere doesn't rush you out.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at SaiGon BreadFast and grab a bánh mì or one of their breakfast plates — solid food at a solid price to kick things off.
cards.why: Best way to start a Tuesday morning is with something actually good to eat.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club at Cupertino Library — it's a casual, welcoming group where you practice English through real back-and-forth conversation.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and genuinely interesting to meet people from all over the world.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space Preserve
cards.address: 22500 Cristo Rey Dr, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Rancho San Antonio and do a few miles on the trails — the meadow loop is an easy, beautiful walk with deer sightings on a good day.
cards.why: The 65°F high makes this perfect hiking weather, not too hot and not too cold.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara, spend time in the sculpture garden and wander the contemporary galleries at your own pace.
cards.why: It's free and genuinely impressive for a regional museum — a great afternoon slowdown after a big lunch.

cards.name: XYC Massage
cards.address: 19501 Stevens Creek Blvd #103, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at XYC Massage in Cupertino and let the day's hiking and walking get worked out of your legs.
cards.why: You hiked, you feasted — finishing the day with a massage is the right call.

cards.name: Mikiya Wagyu Shabu House | Santa Clara
cards.address: 3590 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Mikiya Wagyu Shabu House in Santa Clara with a hot pot spread worth lingering over — the wagyu is the move.
cards.why: A cool spring evening and a hot pot dinner is just a great combination.

cards.name: Dr.ink
cards.address: 19959 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Dr.ink, a beloved Cupertino coffee spot with solid drinks and a chill vibe to ease into the day.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything else better.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library. It's a free, low-key gathering where you can practice English conversation with a friendly group.
cards.why: Free and genuinely useful if you want to sharpen your conversational English in a relaxed setting.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then spend time with the contemporary exhibitions inside. It's a proper art museum that most people sleep on.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a cool Tuesday morning is a genuinely good way to spend a few hours.

cards.name: The Starving Musician - SANTA CLARA
cards.address: 3939 Stevens Creek Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Browse The Starving Musician in Santa Clara. Walls of guitars, amps, keyboards, and used gear. Even if you're not buying, it's a fun hour of picking things up and messing around.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you walk into for 20 minutes and leave an hour and a half later.

cards.name: Siam Pavilion Thai Massage
cards.address: 830 Stewart Dr STE 172, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Siam Pavilion Thai Massage in Sunnyvale. Thai massage, deep tissue, whatever sounds good. Let the day melt off.
cards.why: You'll feel completely reset walking out of here.

cards.name: Pour Decisions Craft Coffee | Craft Beer
cards.address: 1040 Grant Rd Suite #305, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a craft coffee at Pour Decisions. Good beans, relaxed vibe, and they do beer later in the day if you're feeling ambitious.
cards.why: Every good Tuesday needs a proper coffee start.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head over to Shoreline Park for a walk along the water. The bay views are wide open and the temperature should be comfortable by late morning.
cards.why: 65°F and sunny is exactly what this walk was made for.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the Mountain View Farmers Market and see what's good this week. Pick up something fresh, snack on whatever catches your eye, and take your time.
cards.why: It's a Tuesday morning and the crowds are minimal — best way to do a farmers market.

cards.name: His & Her Rocks Gallery
cards.address: 296 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the His & Her Rocks Gallery in Los Altos. Minerals, gems, and fossils displayed gallery-style. Even if you're not a collector, it's a genuinely cool space to walk through.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you stumble into and end up spending way more time than expected.

cards.name: Leleka Ukrainian Comfort Food (Sunnyvale)
cards.address: 1026 W Evelyn Ave Kitchen#114, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Leleka Ukrainian Comfort Food. Order something hearty and give yourself time to actually sit and enjoy it.
cards.why: Seriously one of the best-reviewed spots in the area and the food lives up to it.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Books Inc. on Castro Street and browse whatever section pulls you in. Good independent bookstore energy with a solid selection.
cards.why: A great way to wind down the afternoon before dinner.

cards.name: Monday Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Wrap the evening with Monday Meditation and Mindfulness in Los Altos. Free, low-key, and a genuinely nice way to close out a full day.
cards.why: After a packed day, this is exactly the kind of thing that actually feels good to do.

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 543 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a warm drink and something to eat at Peet's. Good coffee, easy vibe, no rush.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a Wednesday morning before the day picks up.

cards.name: Milpitas Library
cards.address: 160 N Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Milpitas Library to browse, decompress, or just wander the stacks for a bit before the afternoon event starts.
cards.why: A chill low-key stop that sets you up nicely for the 2 PM activity.

cards.name: Jun Bistro
cards.address: 290 Barber Ct, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Jun Bistro for lunch. It's a short window so don't dawdle — they close at 2 PM, and the food is worth showing up for.
cards.why: One of the better lunch spots in Milpitas and the timing works out perfectly.

cards.name: Knit & Crochet Circle at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Knit & Crochet Circle at Milpitas Library. Bring a project if you have one, or just show up and see what the group is working on.
cards.why: It's free, social, and weirdly satisfying even if you're just learning.

cards.name: TD Beauty Spa
cards.address: 56 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Get a facial, massage, or whatever sounds good at TD Beauty Spa. It's a solid spot to unwind for the back half of the day.
cards.why: After a busy morning, a little pampering hits different.

cards.name: MeloMelo Coconut Dessert
cards.address: 302 Barber Ct, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: End the night on a sweet note at MeloMelo Coconut Dessert. Order a bowl with grass jelly, coconut jelly, and whatever else catches your eye.
cards.why: It's a Milpitas staple and the perfect low-key way to wrap up the day.

cards.name: Cafe Dolce Sicilia
cards.address: 1165 Lincoln Ave #110, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Cafe Dolce Sicilia with espresso and a pastry — it's a cozy Italian-style cafe on Lincoln Ave that's worth settling into for a bit.
cards.why: Cool morning, warm cafe, good coffee. Hard to beat.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library — it's a deep dive into martial arts history and culture that's way more engaging than you'd expect from a library show.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and a genuinely cool way to spend a mid-morning.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to Japantown and grab a bowl of udon at Gombei, then pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo and browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and the lunch scene there is legit.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along Guadalupe River Park, a surprisingly quiet green corridor running right through downtown San Jose.
cards.why: It's a low-key afternoon stroll that clears your head before the evening kicks in.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara for free contemporary art and a sculpture garden that's genuinely impressive for a free admission spot.
cards.why: The South Bay's most underrated art museum and it won't cost you a thing.

cards.name: Downtown Farmers’ Market
cards.address: 101 Paseo De San Antonio, San Jose
cards.blurb: Wrap up at the Downtown Farmers' Market at Paseo De San Antonio — browse the stalls, pick up something local, and enjoy the evening air downtown.
cards.why: A relaxed way to end the day without needing to plan anything.

cards.name: Tico Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 1334 Dell Ave Suite G, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Tico Coffee Roasters with a solid cup and a quiet corner before the day picks up.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Wednesday morning in Campbell.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek path, enjoy the shade, and shake off the morning chill before the day warms up.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key outdoor stretch on a crisp April morning.

cards.name: Downtown Farmers’ Market
cards.address: 101 Paseo De San Antonio, San Jose
cards.blurb: Browse the stalls at the Downtown Farmers Market on Paseo De San Antonio, pick up fresh produce, snacks, or just wander and snack your way through lunch.
cards.why: Great timing to hit it when the market is in full swing and vendors are all set up.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out this exhibition recovering and celebrating Ricardo Favela's Royal Chicano Air Force poster collection at King Library on the SJSU campus.
cards.why: It's free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely rare look at Chicano art history.

cards.name: Bamboo Healthcare Spa Inc
cards.address: 871 Coleman Ave, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a spa treatment at Bamboo Healthcare Spa before dinner.
cards.why: A solid way to reset after a full day of walking and museums.

cards.name: Clandestine Brewing
cards.address: 980 S 1st St ste b, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a pint at Clandestine Brewing and settle in for the evening with good craft beer in a relaxed South First setting.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key Wednesday night spot that actually delivers.

cards.name: Heartbeat Cafe
cards.address: 14392 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Heartbeat Cafe, grab a coffee and something to eat before the day kicks off. Cozy spot, good food, worth the stop.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a cool April morning is with a solid breakfast.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the creek path. It's easy, shaded, and genuinely pretty this time of year. Great way to shake off the morning chill as it warms up.
cards.why: 63 degrees by afternoon means this morning slot is perfect for a trail walk before it gets busy.

cards.name: Danbi Korean Restaurant Los Gatos
cards.address: 9 Montebello Way, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Danbi for lunch. Korean food done well, and the lunch service wraps at 2 so you'll want to get there on time. Order whatever they're putting in front of you.
cards.why: The lunch window is short so this timing works perfectly.

cards.name: Villa Montalvo Gardens
cards.address: Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Villa Montalvo Gardens in Saratoga is a gorgeous afternoon wander through sculpted grounds and quiet garden paths. Just roam, breathe, and decompress.
cards.why: The gardens close at 5 and the afternoon light through the trees is worth it.

cards.name: Sunflower Spa
cards.address: 14501 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Sunflower Spa is right in Saratoga, so slide in for a treatment after the gardens. Book something in advance if you can. Good way to spend a Wednesday afternoon.
cards.why: You're already in Saratoga and a spa stop makes the afternoon feel intentional.

cards.name: Bai Tong
cards.address: 6011 14515 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Bai Tong is a solid Thai dinner in Saratoga on Big Basin Way. The rating speaks for itself, the price is reasonable, and dinner service runs until 8. Order the classics.
cards.why: You're finishing the day in Saratoga and this wraps it up well without having to go anywhere new.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Walk the bamboo groves and Japanese garden terraces at Hakone Estate. It's quiet on weekday mornings, so you'll actually get to breathe it in without crowds.
cards.why: This is one of the most genuinely peaceful spots in the South Bay and it's at its best on a cool morning like this.

cards.name: Barnes & Noble
cards.address: 122 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Barnes and Noble on Santa Cruz Ave. It's a well-stocked store in a walkable stretch of Los Gatos — pick up a book, flip through some magazines, or just wander.
cards.why: Los Gatos Barnes and Noble is one of the better ones in the area and a great low-key afternoon stop.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Los Gatos Creek Trail for a mellow stroll along the water. The creek path is shaded and flat, great for clearing your head between stops.
cards.why: A short walk after a garden visit feels natural, and the trail is easy and scenic.

cards.name: Body Rest Massage Wellness Center
cards.address: 621 E Campbell Ave #11e, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a massage at Body Rest Wellness Center. It's a legit massage spot in Campbell, no frills, just good work.
cards.why: A Wednesday afternoon massage is a completely valid life choice and you'll feel great for the rest of the day.

cards.name: Drinx
cards.address: 2415 S Winchester Blvd, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: End the day at Drinx in Campbell. It opens early and runs late, so you can grab a drink and settle in for a relaxed evening without rushing.
cards.why: A good neighborhood bar is the perfect way to close out a day that had a nice mix of everything.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Walk the Cupertino Farmers Market, pick up some fresh produce, snack on whatever looks good from the vendors, and ease into the morning without any rush.
cards.why: Cool morning air, free entry, and a chill way to kick off the day.

cards.name: Let’s Hike Saratoga: Saratoga To the Skyline Trail
cards.blurb: Hit the Saratoga to the Skyline Trail while it's organized and guided. The foothill views open up nicely as you climb and the 60s temperatures make it genuinely pleasant hiking weather.
cards.why: This hike is happening today and the weather is perfect for it.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 20750 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Post-hike lunch at Dish N Dash. Order the shawarma or a mezze spread and sit down properly after all that trail time.
cards.why: Middle Eastern comfort food after a morning hike is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art. Walk the sculpture garden, then head inside for the contemporary galleries. It's completely free and genuinely worth your time.
cards.why: Most people drive past this place their whole lives without going in.

cards.name: Shahi Darbar Indian Cuisine
cards.address: 2155 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Shahi Darbar. Order the lamb dishes or a solid curry, grab some naan, and take your time with it.
cards.why: One of the better Indian spots in Santa Clara and a satisfying way to close out a full day.

cards.name: Nahita Bakery
cards.address: 1334 S Mary Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Nahita Bakery with a pastry and something warm to drink. Good vibe for a cool April morning.
cards.why: A proper bakery breakfast beats a drive-through every time.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then take your time through the contemporary galleries inside. It's free and genuinely worth lingering in.
cards.why: This museum punches well above its weight and it's the kind of place you forget exists until you're standing in it.

cards.name: Sunnyvale Farmers Market
cards.address: Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Walk the Sunnyvale Farmers Market on Murphy Ave and pick up whatever looks good. Produce, flowers, snacks — just wander and see what catches your eye.
cards.why: It's a classic Wednesday morning and the market is already running.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum. Walk through the full timeline of computing, from punch cards to the internet. Even if tech isn't your thing, the scale of it is wild.
cards.why: It's the kind of museum where you lose track of time in the best way.

cards.name: Iraklis Restaurant
cards.address: 2165 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Iraklis in Santa Clara. It's Greek, affordable, and the food is the kind that makes you want to order more than you planned.
cards.why: Under $15 a person for a meal this good is a steal.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a session at Star Thai Massage on Murphy Ave. Book a Thai massage and let the afternoon soreness from all that walking melt off.
cards.why: A great way to shift gears before dinner.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk the lakeside trail, watch the birds do their thing on the water, and let the crisp morning air wake you up the rest of the way.
cards.why: Cool weather and an open sky — this is the best version of a Wednesday morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours inside the Computer History Museum — the exhibits go way deeper than you'd expect, and you'll keep saying 'wait, seriously?' the whole time.
cards.why: It's genuinely fascinating and a great use of a cool afternoon.

cards.name: Crumbl
cards.address: 660B San Antonio Rd, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Crumbl for a rotation of oversized, rotating-menu cookies — grab a couple to eat now and maybe one for later.
cards.why: You just walked a museum for two hours, you've earned a cookie.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Books Inc. on Castro Street, pick up something you've been meaning to read, and actually take your time flipping through things.
cards.why: A great independent bookstore is worth an afternoon wander any day of the week.

cards.name: Kween's
cards.address: 292 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Kween's for dinner, order something solid off the menu, and settle into a good meal to close out the evening.
cards.why: One of the better spots on the Mountain View restaurant scene and a satisfying way to end the day.

cards.name: Monday Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at this Monday Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos — a quiet, free way to decompress after a full day out.
cards.why: Free, calm, and a surprisingly good way to close out a packed Wednesday.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 3990 El Camino Real unit c, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at ZombieRunner with a solid cup of coffee. It's a runner's haven with a relaxed vibe — great spot to ease into the day before things get moving.
cards.why: Good coffee and a chill atmosphere beats a chain any morning.

cards.name: Meditation with Sara
cards.address: 1213 Newell Rd. Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Drop into Meditation with Sara at 1213 Newell Rd. Free, low-key, and a genuinely good reset — just show up and breathe for an hour.
cards.why: A free midweek meditation is a rare find and a great way to start the day grounded.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Head up to The Dish Trail for a proper Stanford foothills hike. The views from the top are sweeping and the loop keeps things interesting the whole way up.
cards.why: April weather in the foothills is as good as it gets.

cards.name: Sundance The Steakhouse
cards.address: 1921 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Post-hike lunch at Sundance The Steakhouse. It's a classic El Camino spot with a proper lunch menu worth sitting down for.
cards.why: You earned a real meal after that trail.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Walk the Rodin sculpture garden outside, then duck in to explore the galleries at your own pace.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful campus is hard to beat for a Wednesday afternoon.

cards.name: LaBelle Day Spas & Salons
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suites 95 & 33, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at LaBelle Day Spas and Salons. Book a quick treatment or just see what they have available for a walk-in refresh after a full day out.
cards.why: A little evening self-care is always a good call.

cards.name: Fremont Hills Country Club - Los Altos Hills
cards.address: 12889 Viscaino Pl, Los Altos Hills, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Cafe Nur in Los Altos. The food is fresh and full of flavor, the space is cozy, and it's a perfect way to close out the night.
cards.why: It's a satisfying dinner spot that never feels like a letdown.

cards.name: MeloMelo Coconut Dessert
cards.address: 302 Barber Ct, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at MeloMelo Coconut Dessert for something a little different — their coconut-based drinks and desserts are a legitimately good morning treat.
cards.why: It's open now and a fun way to kick off the day before things get busy.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop in on the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In, a relaxed community gathering where people bring their needlepoint projects and hang out. You don't have to be an expert — just show up, see what people are working on, and enjoy the chill vibe.
cards.why: It's free, it's low-key, and it's a genuinely nice way to spend a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Sandy Wool Lake - Gazebo
cards.address: 901 Downing Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Sandy Wool Lake Gazebo for a mid-morning walk around the lake before lunch. It's a peaceful spot, great for clearing your head and getting some fresh air while the temperature climbs toward that 71°F high.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a lakeside stroll before settling in for lunch.

cards.name: Sugar Mama Desserts
cards.address: Ranch 99 Shopping Center, 492 Barber Ln, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Sugar Mama Desserts in the Ranch 99 Shopping Center. The dessert-forward menu has something for every sweet tooth, and it makes for a fun, indulgent lunch stop.
cards.why: The dessert spread here is seriously worth the stop.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library. It's a gentle, flowing practice that mixes breathwork and movement — no experience needed.
cards.why: Free, calming, and a great way to reset in the middle of the afternoon.

cards.name: Knit & Crochet Circle at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Stick around the library for the Knit and Crochet Circle. Bring your own project or just hang and watch others work — it's a welcoming group and a genuinely relaxing couple of hours.
cards.why: Already at the library, and it's a nice low-effort afternoon activity.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 1976 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a custom Philz blend — tell them your vibe and they'll make it work. Good patio energy for a cool morning.
cards.why: Best way to ease into the day before things open up.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden first, then go inside for the contemporary galleries.
cards.why: It's free and genuinely good, the kind of place you forget exists until you're standing in it.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - The Pruneyard
cards.address: 1875 S Bascom Ave Suite #570, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head into LUNA Mexican Kitchen at the Pruneyard for lunch. Order a margarita and something off the brunch-to-lunch menu on the patio if the sun cooperates.
cards.why: It's a solid sit-down spot with a great vibe for a leisurely Thursday afternoon.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Smash City Rage Rooms and break some stuff. Pick your room, suit up, and go to town with a bat or crowbar.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to spend a Thursday afternoon with no kids around.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Wander the Campbell Farmers Market on Campbell Ave, pick up something fresh, and soak in the Thursday morning crowd before it gets busy.
cards.why: It's free, it's local, and the morning light makes it worth showing up for.

cards.name: Ban Sabai Thai Massage and Spa
cards.address: 108 Race St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Ban Sabai Thai Massage and Spa on Race St. Book a Thai massage and let the day fully melt off.
cards.why: After rage rooms, your body will actually want this.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Walk Campbell Ave, grab a coffee from one of the market vendors, and pick up some fresh produce or a pastry to snack on while you browse the stalls.
cards.why: It's the perfect low-key way to kick off a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Follow the creek path at an easy pace, enjoy the shade from the trees, and let the morning warm up around you before heading out for lunch.
cards.why: Cool mornings on this trail are genuinely lovely before it gets busy.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Pick a package, suit up, and smash some stuff. Plates, electronics, whatever they've got. Surprisingly therapeutic.
cards.why: Smash City is genuinely fun and a solid way to burn off some afternoon energy.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a table at San Pedro Square Market, mix and match from a dozen vendors, and eat on the patio while the evening cools down.
cards.why: It's the easiest, most satisfying dinner in downtown San Jose with zero commitment to a single cuisine.

cards.name: Lou's Coffee Shop
cards.address: 15691 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Lou's Coffee Shop. Grab a coffee and something to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a local Los Gatos staple and closes at 2 PM, so mornings are the move.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Take a relaxed walk along Los Gatos Creek Trail. The path is shaded and follows the creek, so it's a nice easy stretch to shake off the morning.
cards.why: 52 degrees warming up to 69 is genuinely perfect hiking weather.

cards.name: Svetlana’s Massage
cards.address: 15066 Los Gatos Almaden Rd Ste 120-A, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Book a midday massage at Svetlana's and let someone else do the work for an hour.
cards.why: A massage mid-day hits differently than a rushed evening appointment.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 50 University Ave d200, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Salt and Straw for lunch-adjacent ice cream on University Ave. Their flavors are genuinely unusual and worth the stop.
cards.why: Treating yourself to ice cream at noon on a Thursday is a valid life choice.

cards.name: Arbor Day Celebration & Ceremonial Tree Planting
cards.blurb: Head over to Saratoga for the Arbor Day Celebration and Ceremonial Tree Planting. It's a free community event and a good excuse to get outside again.
cards.why: It's free and it's today, so why not.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Gali Vineyards on West Main Street in Los Gatos. Grab a glass and settle into the evening vibe.
cards.why: A glass of wine after an afternoon outside is basically mandatory.

cards.name: Hapa's Brewing Company
cards.address: 114 S Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night at Hapa's Brewing Company on South Santa Cruz Ave. Order a pint and some food and let the night go as long as it wants.
cards.why: Good beer, good food, and zero reason to rush home.

cards.name: Tai Zhan
cards.address: 14572 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with coffee and breakfast at Tai Zhan, a local favorite tucked into Saratoga Village on Big Basin Way.
cards.why: Good coffee and a chill start beats rushing into the day.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the Japanese gardens at Hakone Estate, catch the bamboo grove, and take your time with the koi ponds and terraced hillside paths.
cards.why: Cool morning air and 51 degrees makes this a perfect walk before it warms up.

cards.name: Drop-in Tech Help
cards.blurb: Drop in for free tech help at the Saratoga library. Bring any device that's been bugging you.
cards.why: Free, no appointment needed, and actually useful.

cards.name: 四姐 Special Noodle
cards.address: 1530 S De Anza Blvd, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Head to 四姐 Special Noodle for a serious bowl of handmade noodles. Order whatever the table next to you is having.
cards.why: This place has a cult following for a reason and lunch here is worth it.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Kill time at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge in Los Gatos. Pick a piece, paint it, and they fire it for you to pick up later.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun adult activity and the Los Gatos location is a great spot to wind down the afternoon.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dio Deka, a Greek fine dining spot on Main Street in Los Gatos. Go for the lamb and order a cocktail to start.
cards.why: It's a splurge-worthy dinner and the vibe on a Thursday evening is exactly right.

cards.name: Stevens Canyon Trail
cards.address: 13492 Stevens Canyon Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Drop down from the hills and hit Stevens Canyon Trail for a quick morning hike. The canyon is shaded and the creek is running this time of year.
cards.why: Perfect conditions for a brisk hike before the day warms up.

cards.name: Drop-in Tech Help
cards.blurb: Stop by the Saratoga Library for free drop-in tech help if you have any device questions or software stuff that's been bugging you.
cards.why: Free, no appointment needed, and honestly a great use of 30 minutes.

cards.name: Stackers Pizzeria
cards.address: 18572 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Stackers Pizzeria on Prospect Road. Order a pie and eat in.
cards.why: Consistently one of the best pizza spots in the area and the lunch crowd hasn't hit full swing yet.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara for a couple of hours. Walk the sculpture garden outside, then go through the contemporary galleries inside. Admission is free.
cards.why: World-class rotating exhibits and a great sculpture garden, all free on a weekday afternoon.

cards.name: Gu eum Cake & Bakery
cards.address: 18566 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Walk next door to Gu Eum Cake and Bakery for something sweet after lunch. The Korean-style pastries and cakes are worth the stop.
cards.why: It's right there and you won't regret it.

cards.name: Arwa Yemeni Coffee
cards.address: 605 Tasman Dr, Sunnyvale, CA 94089, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a coffee at Arwa Yemeni Coffee — try their qishr or a traditional Yemeni brew to kick things off right.
cards.why: Nothing beats starting a cool morning with something warm and genuinely interesting.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on early computing, the internet, and AI are genuinely fascinating even if you've never touched a line of code.
cards.why: It's one of those places you keep meaning to go and always leave glad you did.

cards.name: Tennis Courts | Serra Park
cards.address: 1568 Hollenbeck Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Burn off lunch with some tennis at Serra Park. The courts are well-kept and the late afternoon light is perfect.
cards.why: The weather hits 70 by afternoon and these courts are a solid spot to enjoy it.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art and wander the galleries and the outdoor sculpture garden at your own pace.
cards.why: Free contemporary art with a sculpture garden on a crisp sunny day is hard to beat.

cards.name: Earthy Ruby Holistic and Acupuncture Center
cards.address: 20640 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a session at Earthy Ruby Holistic and Acupuncture Center in Cupertino. Whether it's acupuncture or a holistic treatment, it's a solid way to decompress after an active day.
cards.why: After tennis and a full day of exploring, your body will thank you.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls at the Mountain View Farmers Market, pick up fresh fruit, maybe a pastry, and ease into the morning without any agenda.
cards.why: Thursday morning, cool air, and a farmers market is the right way to start a day off.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Give yourself a solid two hours at the Computer History Museum. The Revolution exhibit alone covers room-sized computers through the smartphone era and the displays are genuinely wild, not dry.
cards.why: Even if you think you're not a tech person, this place pulls you in.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 736 N Mathilda Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dish N Dash for lunch. Get the shawarma or a mezze plate and sit down properly rather than grabbing something fast.
cards.why: It's some of the best Middle Eastern food in the South Bay and won't wreck your wallet.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club in Los Altos. It's a laid-back group conversation session, great for meeting people and just talking.
cards.why: Free, social, and a totally different vibe from anything else in the day.

cards.name: Artisan Wine & Spirits
cards.address: 2495 Old Middlefield Way, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Artisan Wine and Spirits on Old Middlefield for a proper browse. Grab a bottle of something interesting for the evening or just taste around if they're pouring.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good shop with a well-curated selection and a relaxed feel.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night at Next Level Board Game Cafe. Order food and drinks, pick a game from their library, and settle in for the evening.
cards.why: There is no better Thursday night move than a board game cafe with good company.

cards.name: Yummy Future Coffee, Matcha & Tea
cards.address: 170 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a matcha latte or a creative tea drink at this University Ave spot — good energy, good vibes before the day gets going.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a cool April morning.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hike the Dish loop up through the Stanford foothills — the views of the Bay stretch out wide from the top and the climb is just hard enough to feel earned.
cards.why: High of 70°F means it'll be perfect up there before it warms up.

cards.name: Tamarine Restaurant & Gallery
cards.address: 546 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Tamarine on University Ave. The Vietnamese food here is genuinely elevated, so go for the shaken beef or whatever the lunch special is and take your time.
cards.why: It's a legitimately great restaurant and lunch is the smart move for the price.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk through the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus and spend time in the Rodin Sculpture Garden outside — the bronze figures are massive and worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free world-class art and the campus makes for a great afternoon stroll.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Swing by the Stanford Educational Farm and volunteer for an hour or so — weeding, planting, whatever they've got going that afternoon.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to wrap up an afternoon outdoors.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dinah's Poolside Restaurant. Sit on the patio if it's warm enough, order something classic off the menu, and enjoy a proper sit-down meal to end the day.
cards.why: The poolside setting hits different on a clear spring evening.

cards.name: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: 1649 Naglee Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the garden paths and check out what's blooming. April is one of the best times to visit — the roses are starting to open up and the whole place smells incredible.
cards.why: It's free, it's beautiful, and the weather today is perfect for it.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Slingshot Pinball when it opens and get a few games in. Great machines, low-key vibe, and it gets more fun the longer you stay.
cards.why: A really good way to spend a Thursday evening without overthinking it.

cards.name: Voltaire Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 360 S Market St #80, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a coffee and something to eat at Voltaire — good beans, chill vibe, and a solid way to ease into the day.
cards.why: Perfect weather to sit outside with a coffee before the day kicks off.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Fists of Fury is a free exhibition at King Library exploring the history and culture of martial arts films — worth an hour or so of wandering through.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a great way to fill the morning before lunch.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market and mix and match lunch from a dozen vendors under one roof — tacos, ramen, banh mi, coffee, all in one spot.
cards.why: Easiest, most fun lunch in downtown San Jose on a nice day.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk it off along Guadalupe River Park before the opera — it's a quiet green stretch right through downtown, good for clearing your head before sitting still for a few hours.
cards.why: A quick walk in the fresh air is the perfect buffer before an afternoon show.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wander San Pedro Square Market and build lunch from whatever looks good — tacos, ramen, banh mi, coffee. Grab a patio seat if you can.
cards.why: 68 degrees and sunny is exactly when this place is at its best.

cards.name: La Traviata
cards.blurb: Our season concludes with Verdi’s tragic love story, La Traviata. The scandalous love affair between the courtesan Violetta Valery and the young gentleman Alfredo Germont threatens to tear the fabric…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Angkor Chef
cards.address: 86 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Angkor Chef for dinner before they close at 7 — Cambodian flavors, good portions, and a relaxed sit-down after the opera.
cards.why: It's an under-the-radar gem and a nice contrast to the grandeur of the afternoon.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with mochi from Okayama Kobo and a bowl of udon at Gombei — one of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a genuinely great way to ease into the day.
cards.why: Perfect weather to wander an outdoor neighborhood with good food and interesting shops.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Catch the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library, a deep dive into the history of martial arts in cinema and Asian American culture — free, walkable, and actually worth your time.
cards.why: It's free and genuinely interesting, and the King Library building itself is worth seeing.

cards.name: Island Taste Caribbean Grill
cards.address: 225 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Island Taste Caribbean Grill, a no-frills downtown spot with solid jerk chicken and Caribbean plates at prices that won't hurt.
cards.why: Quick, satisfying, and something you don't eat every day.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk through the Municipal Rose Garden while everything is in bloom. April is peak season, the paths are easy, and it's one of those spots that looks like it shouldn't be free.
cards.why: 70 degrees and rose season is basically the perfect combo for this place.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Pop into the State of Illusion Exhibition in Santa Clara for some thought-provoking visual art before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a good way to fill the gap before dinner without overthinking it.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Bakenstein for dinner. It opens at 5 and the ratings speak for themselves, so get there on the earlier side before the evening crowd builds.
cards.why: You need to eat before the show and this is a legitimately good stop.

cards.name: Purity Ring, mmj
cards.address: 400 S 1st St
cards.blurb: Place of My Own TourSoundcheck Q&A ExperienceIncludes:- One (1) GA Ticket- Access to Intimate Two (2) Song Acoustic Performance- Pre-show Q&A with the Members of Purity Ring- VIP Exclusive CD Sampler…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Satura Cakes
cards.address: 200 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Satura Cakes with a coffee and something from the pastry case. The cakes are beautiful but the morning bites are just as good.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into the day before things get going.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours wandering the Computer History Museum. The exhibits go way deeper than you'd expect and there's always something that makes you stop and go 'wait, really?'
cards.why: It's genuinely fascinating even if you've never touched a command line in your life.

cards.name: Cafe Pro Bono
cards.address: 2437 Birch St, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Cafe Pro Bono is a solid lunch spot with a relaxed vibe. Get there right at noon before it fills up.
cards.why: The window is short so it's worth planning around.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio while the weather is perfect. The lower loop is easy and scenic, or push up to the farm area for a longer stretch.
cards.why: 69 degrees with no rain is basically ideal hiking weather.

cards.name: Uncommon Threads
cards.address: 164 Main St #105, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Uncommon Threads on Main Street and browse the curated selection. It's the kind of shop where you go in for a look and leave with something you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: It's right in Los Altos and gets you warmed up for the evening on Main Street.

cards.name: Books to Blooms: A Garden Lover’s Party
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Friday, April 17 at 6pm at Turbow Farms (23263 Mora Glen Dr, Los Altos) Buy Tickets »
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Sweet Diplomacy® Gluten-Free
cards.address: 209 1st St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with something sweet from Sweet Diplomacy, a gluten-free bakery right in Los Altos. Grab a pastry and coffee to ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a low-key, delicious way to kick off a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Get a quick hike in at Rancho San Antonio before the day fills up. The lower loop is easy and scenic, or push up toward the farm if you want a little more.
cards.why: Perfect weather for it at 69°F, and it's one of those places that always delivers.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 170 State St Unit 121, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to The Good Salad for lunch. Build your own bowl with whatever's fresh, and eat on the patio if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: It's right in Los Altos and an easy, satisfying lunch after a busy morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Computer History Museum and spend a couple hours actually reading stuff. The exhibits on early computing and personal computers are genuinely wild, even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: It's the kind of museum that surprises you every time.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into SWEET55 for a Swiss chocolate pick-me-up. Grab a few truffles or a chocolate bar to snack on for the rest of the afternoon.
cards.why: Treating yourself mid-afternoon is always a good call.

cards.name: Bevri - Palo Alto
cards.address: 335 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Bevri in Palo Alto for Georgian food. Order the khachapuri and whatever wine they recommend, and take your time with it.
cards.why: Georgian food is underrated and Bevri does it really well.

cards.name: Book Launch with Randy Ribay
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Saturday, April 18 at 11am
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Cafe Nur
cards.address: 280 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Cafe Nur on Main Street in Los Altos. Grab a coffee and something to eat on the patio while the day is still fresh.
cards.why: Perfect 69-degree morning to sit outside with a good cup of coffee.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Head out on the trails at Rancho San Antonio. The lower meadow loop is easy and gorgeous, or push up toward PG&E trail if you want a real climb.
cards.why: A day this nice is basically wasted if you don't spend part of it outside.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Computer History Museum and spend a couple hours going deep on the permanent collection. The timeline of computing from punch cards to smartphones is genuinely wild to walk through.
cards.why: It's a fun, air-conditioned midday stop that's way more interesting than it sounds.

cards.name: Alegio Chocolaté / Claudio Corallo PALO ALTO
cards.address: 522 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Alegio Chocolaté on Bryant Street and try a few of the Claudio Corallo chocolates. These are single-origin bars made from one farm in São Tomé and there's nothing else quite like them.
cards.why: It's a genuinely special chocolate experience and a great way to kill an hour before the evening.

cards.name: Book Launch with David Levithan and Gabriel Duckels
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Thursday, April 23 at 6pm
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with mochi from Okayama Kobo and a slow walk through one of only three surviving Japantowns in the US.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into the day before your 10 AM stop.

cards.name: Shop at our Nikkei Treasures Sale!
cards.address: 535 North 5th Street, San Jose, CA, 95112
cards.blurb: During Nikkei Matsuri 2026, come and shop at our annual Nikkei Treasures rummage sale in our parking lot! Shop deals on Japanese art, antiquities, ceramics, houseware and more! Trying to test your…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: Japanese American Museum of San Jose

cards.name: La Original Paleteria y Neveria
cards.address: 273 Willow St, San Jose, CA 95120, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by La Original Paleteria y Neveria for a paleta or neveria treat — a sweet, easy lunch break that won't weigh you down.
cards.why: 68 degrees and sunny is exactly the weather this place was made for.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara, take in the contemporary galleries, and spend time in the sculpture garden outside.
cards.why: Free, genuinely good art, and the outdoor sculpture garden is a nice bonus on a mild day like this.

cards.name: The Orange Tabby
cards.address: 1344 Ridder Park Dr, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into The Orange Tabby for a bite before they close at 5 — grab a seat and see what's on the menu today.
cards.why: Highly regarded spot with a tight window, so catching it in the afternoon is the move.

cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a coffee and one of their donuts — the vegan options here are genuinely good, not a consolation prize.
cards.why: Perfect low-key start before a busy day downtown.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library — it covers the history of martial arts in pop culture and Asian American communities, and it's genuinely interesting to walk through.
cards.why: Free, engaging, and it's right in the heart of San Jose.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through San Pedro Square Market and graze from a few vendors before the main event — tacos, banh mi, coffee, you name it.
cards.why: Grab a quick bite without committing to a full sit-down meal before heading to Nikkei Matsuri.

cards.name: Free Admission on Nikkei Matsuri!
cards.address: 535 North 5th Street, San Jose, CA, 95112
cards.blurb: Enjoy free admission to the Japanese American Museum of San Jose when you go to Nikkei Matsuri, on April 26th, 2026! It’s the perfect opportunity to see our newest exhibit, House Meeting(s): Opening…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: Japanese American Museum of San Jose

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Walk over to Japantown and grab mochi at Okayama Kobo, browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St, and get a bowl of udon at Gombei if you're still hungry.
cards.why: It's one of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a natural continuation of the day's vibe.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Wind down with a stroll through the Rose Garden — spring is peak bloom season and it looks incredible right now.
cards.why: 70 degrees and sunny is exactly the right weather for this.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Santana Row — grab a table on the Zinnia patio or at Straits, browse the boutiques after dinner, and enjoy the evening energy on the boulevard.
cards.why: It's a great place to close out a full day without rushing anywhere.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on how we got from room-sized machines to smartphones in a few decades are genuinely wild to walk through.
cards.why: It holds up even if you've been before, and today's perfect for it.

cards.name: Naschmarkt - Palo Alto
cards.address: 2323 Birch St, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Naschmarkt in Palo Alto for a European-style lunch. The menu leans Austrian and seasonal, so order whatever sounds good and take your time with it.
cards.why: Lunch windows here are short so it's worth timing it right.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the trails along Shoreline Lake with the bay stretched out in front of you. No agenda needed, just wander.
cards.why: 70 degrees and calm is basically perfect for an afternoon outside.

cards.name: The Sense head + scalp spa - Mountain View
cards.address: 642 San Antonio Rd, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Book a head and scalp treatment at The Sense spa to decompress before the evening. It's the kind of thing you forget exists until you're already in the chair.
cards.why: Good way to reset before heading out for the night.

cards.name: Artisan Wine & Spirits
cards.address: 2495 Old Middlefield Way, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Artisan Wine and Spirits on Old Middlefield and grab something interesting to sip on — they have a solid selection worth poking around.
cards.why: A low-key stop that fits perfectly between the spa and the sound bath.

cards.name: April Full Immersion Sound Bath Saturday, April 25th | 6:30pm–8:00pm
cards.address: 324 Castro Street, Mountain View, CA, 94041
cards.blurb: April Full Immersion Sound Bath on April 25th - Designed as a seasonal reset, this 90-minute experience supports nervous system regulation. Through gong, crystal bowls, voice, and drum, you’ll be guid
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls at the Mountain View Farmers Market, grab some fresh fruit or a pastry, and ease into the day at a relaxed pace.
cards.why: Perfect weather to be outside this morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on early computing, the internet, and Silicon Valley's origins are genuinely fascinating, even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: It's one of the better museums in the Bay Area and it's right here in Mountain View.

cards.name: Sourdough & Co.
cards.address: 809 Cuesta Dr Suite A, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Sourdough & Co. and build your sandwich on freshly baked bread with whatever toppings catch your eye.
cards.why: Solid, affordable lunch that won't slow you down for the afternoon.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a walk or bike ride along the water at Shoreline Park. The bay views are open and wide, and the fresh air after lunch feels exactly right.
cards.why: 70 degrees and sunny is about as good as it gets for a lakeside afternoon.

cards.name: K Spa and Bodywork
cards.address: 151 2nd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage or bodywork session at K Spa and Bodywork and genuinely decompress before the evening.
cards.why: A weekday afternoon slot is the move — way easier to get in and way more relaxing.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Books Inc. on Castro Street, pick up something you've been meaning to read, and take your time.
cards.why: Great independent bookstore energy and a nice way to wind down before dinner.

cards.name: Introduction To Crystal Singing Bowls - Friday - 4/24 •
cards.address: 324 Castro Street, Mountain View, CA, 94041
cards.blurb: Experience the unique resonance of crystal singing bowls through a guided listening session. Learn how different tones interact with the body and explore the difference between frosted and gemstone-in
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history of martial arts cinema and its cultural impact. Free, unhurried, and genuinely interesting.
cards.why: It's a free exhibition and a great way to spend a slow morning before lunch.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Take a walk through Guadalupe River Park to stretch your legs and get some air before the afternoon. The path through downtown is quiet and green in a way that surprises most people.
cards.why: A 70-degree April day is basically made for this walk.

cards.name: Miguel Novelo: Inframundo Exhibition Opening
cards.address: 560 South First Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to Miguel Novelo: Inframundo Exhibition Opening and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Jandee Thai Massage
cards.address: 1699 N Capitol Ave #50, San Jose, CA 95132, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Jandee Thai Massage with a solid hour of bodywork. Book ahead if you can, but walk-ins are often welcome.
cards.why: After a full morning and afternoon out, this is the right move before dinner.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Santana Row for dinner. Walk the boulevard first, then grab a table at one of the restaurant patios. Straits and Sino are both solid options, and the outdoor seating is great on an evening like this.
cards.why: A warm Thursday night on Santana Row is a genuinely good time.

cards.name: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio
cards.address: 632 Fremont Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the morning getting hands-on at Halo Pottery in Los Altos. Drop in and throw some clay with Hila — it's a genuinely fun way to start the day with something tactile and creative.
cards.why: A pottery session on a perfect 69-degree morning beats staring at a screen.

cards.name: Cloud9 Coffee
cards.address: 1901 Embarcadero Rd #103, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Cloud9 Coffee for a late morning pick-me-up before heading into Palo Alto proper. Great ratings, great vibes, and a solid spot to settle into before the day gets going.
cards.why: Every good day has a coffee stop built in.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to the Anderson Collection at Stanford for the Spotlight Tours on Thursdays. A docent-led walk through world-class modern and contemporary art, totally free, in a beautiful space.
cards.why: It's Thursday, the tours run today, and the Anderson Collection is genuinely one of the best art stops in the Bay Area.

cards.name: South Adobe Creek Loop
cards.address: Adobe Creek Loop Trail, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch on the Adobe Creek Loop Trail. It's a flat, easy walk along the creek with good scenery and just enough movement to feel like you earned the evening ahead.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a post-lunch stroll and a nice way to reset before the evening.

cards.name: Petals & Prosecco
cards.blurb: Co-hosted by the Los Altos Mountain View Community Foundation and Los Altos History Museum, Petals &amp\; Prosecco is an inspiring gathering that celebrates community, connection, and the people and…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Clover Bakery & Cafe
cards.address: 4342 Moorpark Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Clover Bakery and grab a pastry and coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Perfect low-key start before the farmers market buzz kicks in.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Santana Row Farmers Market, grab some samples, pick up whatever looks good, and stroll the row while the morning crowd is still fresh.
cards.why: 68 degrees and a farmers market is basically a perfect Thursday morning.
