cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Weekly open-air farmers market featuring local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Urban Plates
cards.address: 3055 Olin Ave #1020, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Urban Plates for a proper sit-down lunch. Order a bowl or a plate and take your time.
cards.why: Solid food and a relaxed vibe make it a great midday reset.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Drive out to Alum Rock Park and wander the creek trails through the foothills. The afternoon light in there is genuinely beautiful and the crowds are thin on a weekday.
cards.why: You've got ideal weather and the park closes at 5, so now is the time.

cards.name: Oasis Thai Spa
cards.address: 2203 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Oasis Thai Spa and decompress after the walk. A good afternoon treat for adults.
cards.why: After a couple hours on the trails, your legs will thank you.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at San Pedro Square Market for dinner. Pick your way through the vendors, grab a drink at the bar, and find a patio seat to close out the evening.
cards.why: A dozen food options under one roof means everyone eats exactly what they want.

cards.name: Coffee & Water Lab
cards.address: 603 Saratoga Ave #40, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Coffee & Water Lab — grab a pour-over or latte and ease into the day before heading out.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone for everything that follows.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — it's free, thought-provoking, and a solid warm-up before the plant sale.
cards.why: Free contemporary art and you're already in Santa Clara, so it fits perfectly.

cards.name: Forge Spring Plant Sale
cards.blurb: Come get starts for your home gardens! Features organically grown seedlings you can pick up and plant directly in your garden.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art for the sculpture garden and current galleries — take your time, it's free and genuinely worth it.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated art spaces in the South Bay and it closes at 4 PM, so now is the right time.

cards.name: Central Park Pavilion BBQ Area
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Take a walk through Central Park and find a spot at the BBQ pavilion to relax — 70 degrees and shady trees make this one of those afternoons you don't want to rush.
cards.why: After a museum, getting outside in this weather is exactly right.

cards.name: Perspire Sauna Studio
cards.address: 3935 Rivermark Plaza, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Perspire Sauna Studio and sweat it out in an infrared sauna — it's a solid wind-down after a full morning and afternoon.
cards.why: A Thursday afternoon sauna session feels indulgent in the best way.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Bakenstein for dinner — this place is a local gem with serious food, so order generously and sit with it.
cards.why: It's in Santa Clara and the evening hours are when this spot really shines.

cards.name: Roy's Station Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 197 Jackson St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Roy's Station Coffee & Tea on Jackson St. Order something to drink, grab a seat, and ease into the morning before things get moving.
cards.why: Good coffee in a chill spot is the right way to kick off a Thursday.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing by King Library and check out the Fists of Fury exhibition. It's free, it's downtown, and it's a solid way to spend a morning before the game.
cards.why: Free and genuinely interesting — this one's easy to say yes to.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch. Grab tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good — a dozen vendors under one roof means everyone wins. Snag a patio seat if the weather holds.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown, and 70°F is perfect patio weather.

cards.name: Football - San Jose State vs. 2026 Spring Game at San Jose, Calif.
cards.blurb: Football - San Jose State vs. 2026 Spring Game https://sjsuspartans.com/boxscore/8431 View on site | Email this event
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk it off along Guadalupe River Park. The trail runs right through downtown and it's surprisingly green and calm once you're on it.
cards.why: A post-game walk in 70°F weather is exactly what you want before dinner.

cards.name: Island Taste Caribbean Grill
cards.address: 225 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at Island Taste Caribbean Grill on E Santa Clara St. Get a plate and take your time with it.
cards.why: Caribbean food after a day of football and walking is a solid ending move.

cards.name: Dumont Creamery & Café
cards.address: 28 N Almaden Ave Suite 40, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Dumont Creamery and grab a coffee and something to eat before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Good coffee and a chill spot to ease into the morning.

cards.name: Taking the Stage: 50 Years Ago at the Mayer
cards.blurb: Head over to SCU and check out the Taking the Stage exhibition at the Mayer before the tennis match starts — it's free and covers 50 years of performing arts history on campus.
cards.why: Free culture hit that fits perfectly before the match.

cards.name: Santa Clara University Men’s Tennis vs San Diego
cards.blurb: West Coast Conference (WCC) Dual Senior Day
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art and spend time in the sculpture garden. The contemporary exhibits rotate regularly so there's always something fresh to look at.
cards.why: Free, genuinely good art museum that most people overlook.

cards.name: Taplands
cards.address: 1171 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Taplands for a late afternoon beer. They have a solid rotating tap list and it's a relaxed spot to decompress after a full afternoon.
cards.why: Opens at 3 and it's exactly the kind of low-key spot this afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Milk Belly Bakery
cards.address: 30 E Santa Clara St #110, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Milk Belly Bakery with a pastry and coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key start and they close at 3, so morning is the move.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library, a deep dive into the history and culture of martial arts in film and pop culture.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and a great way to spend a weekday morning before the crowds pick up.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market and mix and match lunch from the vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a spot on the patio with a 70-degree afternoon.
cards.why: You can graze through multiple kitchens under one roof, which beats committing to one spot after a big morning.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along Guadalupe River Park, a surprisingly green and quiet stretch right through downtown San Jose.
cards.why: 68 degrees and sunny is exactly when this walk is at its best.

cards.name: Gameshop Downstairs
cards.address: 124 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Gameshop Downstairs for some tabletop games, board games, or whatever's being demoed on the floor today.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to spend an hour and a half without a plan.

cards.name: Independent Bookstore Day 2026
cards.address: 1378 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125
cards.blurb: Head to Independent Bookstore Day 2026 and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Elizabeth F. Gamble Garden
cards.address: 1431 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the free gardens at Elizabeth F. Gamble Garden — the wisteria and rose sections are gorgeous this time of year, and the grounds are peaceful on a Thursday morning.
cards.why: Perfect spring morning to be outside before the day gets going.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the Revolution exhibit alone is worth it, and the sheer scale of what they have on display will genuinely surprise you.
cards.why: It's one of the best museums in the Bay Area and way more interesting than it sounds.

cards.name: Kali Greek Kitchen
cards.address: 451 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Kali Greek Kitchen on California Ave for lunch. Get the bowls or pita plates and sit outside if the patio is open.
cards.why: Fresh, solid food and a great street for a midday break.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch the Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection on the Stanford campus. The docent-led format gives you a focused look at a handful of standout works rather than trying to see everything at once.
cards.why: Free, thoughtful, and a genuinely good way to spend an hour with great art.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: While you're on the Stanford campus, swing through Erika Chong Shuch's installation '1,000 Ways to Hold' — it's a free, immersive work worth a slow look before you head out.
cards.why: You're already there and it's a compelling piece to sit with for a bit.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the evening at Dinah's Poolside Restaurant in Palo Alto. Order something off the classic menu and enjoy the retro poolside vibe as a relaxed end to the day.
cards.why: It's a fun, laid-back spot that feels like a reward after a full day.

cards.name: Middle Grade Book Club: Space for Saffron with author Rie Neal
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Sunday, April 26 at 4pm
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Start the morning wandering the Japanese-style gardens and bamboo groves — it's a genuinely peaceful way to ease into the day before the crowds show up.
cards.why: Cool morning temps and 68°F weather make this the perfect time to be outside.

cards.name: Band Jam: Guitar & Ukulele
cards.blurb: Grab your guitar, grab your ukulele - let's jam! Join us every week, singing and strumming along with popular songs in a fun, easy-going environment. Meet people who are also working on their music…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Mint Leaf Cuisine
cards.address: 14420 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Mint Leaf Cuisine on Big Basin Way for lunch. Try the local favorites and enjoy the cozy atmosphere.
cards.why: It's right in Saratoga and the lunch hours line up perfectly.

cards.name: Historic Saratoga Village
cards.address: Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll through Historic Saratoga Village, pop into the boutique shops and galleries along the main stretch, and take your time soaking in the quiet village vibe.
cards.why: It's a genuinely pleasant afternoon stretch with good shops and no hustle.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge in Los Gatos and paint a piece of pottery — pick something off the shelf, grab your paints, and go at your own pace. No experience needed.
cards.why: It's a surprisingly fun and low-key way to spend a couple of hours with a drink in hand.

cards.name: ManyFriends Brewing Company
cards.address: 14482 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by ManyFriends Brewing Company on Big Basin Way and grab a pint or two. Settle into the taproom and wind down from the afternoon.
cards.why: A great local brewery with solid vibes for an early evening drink.

cards.name: Dos Burros
cards.address: 14560 Big Basin Wy Suite B, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Dos Burros in Saratoga. Order some tacos and enjoy dinner in a relaxed spot that locals clearly love.
cards.why: A satisfying and easy dinner to close out a full day in Saratoga.

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 2035 Camden Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid coffee and something to sip while you ease into the day — grab a seat and settle in before heading out.
cards.why: Every good Thursday needs a proper coffee first.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail at your own pace, enjoy the shade from the trees, and take in that perfect 68-degree morning air along the water.
cards.why: A day this nice deserves at least an hour outside.

cards.name: Aqui Campbell
cards.address: 201 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Aqui for their Cal-Mex lunch — the veggie burritos and enchiladas are solid, and the patio vibe on a day like this is hard to beat.
cards.why: Good food, good price, and you'll be satisfied without feeling sluggish for the afternoon.

cards.name: Sanity Thai Massage
cards.address: 20 S Santa Cruz Ave STE 105, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Sanity Thai and actually use the next couple of hours for yourself — this place is legitimately good and worth it on a weekday afternoon.
cards.why: Midday Thursday is the perfect time to sneak in a massage before anyone else needs you.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Head to Kids Knitting (Ages 7+) and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Historic Preservation Committee
cards.blurb: Swing by the Historic Preservation Committee meeting in Los Gatos and hear what's actually happening with the town's older buildings and neighborhoods.
cards.why: If you care about Los Gatos, this is the room where decisions actually get made.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Dio Deka on E Main St with a proper dinner, think Mediterranean-inspired plates and a great wine list in a space that feels a cut above.
cards.why: You've had a full day and dinner here is absolutely worth it.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU while you're already in Santa Clara. Perceptual art and optical illusions make for a fun, interactive morning stretch.
cards.why: Free, walkable from the Triton, and way more engaging than it sounds.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hike the Fremont Older Open Space before your afternoon event. The foothill trails are low-key and the views from the upper sections are genuinely spectacular.
cards.why: Perfect 69-degree weather for a quick afternoon hike without the crowds.

cards.name: LEGO Club
cards.blurb: Join us at the Cupertino Library 3:30-4:30 PM! Students in Kindergarten to 8th grade are welcome to play and create with LEGOs on the first Tuesday of each month. Space is limited.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at Santana Row. Browse the boutiques, then grab a table at Sino or Straits for dinner and a drink on the patio.
cards.why: The Thursday evening crowd is lively without being overwhelming, and the dining options are genuinely great.

cards.name: Gentle Flow Yoga
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Gentle Flow Yoga session and ease into the day with some movement.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a nice way to shake off the morning chill.

cards.name: Ed R. Levin County Park
cards.address: 3100 Calaveras Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Ed R. Levin County Park and walk the trails while the temperature climbs into that sweet 72-degree range.
cards.why: Fresh air and good views make for a perfect late-morning stretch.

cards.name: Naans & Curries
cards.address: 102 Great Mall Dr Suite 102, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Naans & Curries on Great Mall Drive. Order whatever the staff recommends and don't skip the naan.
cards.why: Consistently one of the best spots in Milpitas for a satisfying sit-down lunch.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Catch the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise class at Milpitas Library for some grounding movement after lunch.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely calming way to spend an early afternoon.

cards.name: Clear Lake Spa – Massage in Milpitas
cards.address: 1766 Clear Lake Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Clear Lake Spa and let the rest of the afternoon melt away.
cards.why: You did yoga and a hike already, so this is the logical next move.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown — grab mochi at Okayama Kobo and a bowl of udon at Gombei when they open, then wander Jackson St and browse the Japanese grocery.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a genuinely great way to ease into a Friday.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and an Egyptian temple tucked into a San Jose neighborhood. Wander the galleries and take your time in the planetarium courtyard.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the most surprising places in the South Bay and nobody expects it to be this good.

cards.name: Little Wine House
cards.address: 350 W Julian St Bldg 4, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Little Wine House opens at 4 PM and it's the right call for a pre-dinner drink. Settle in, try a glass, and let the evening start slowly.
cards.why: A perfect gear-shift between afternoon and dinner without rushing anywhere.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Free contemporary art and a sculpture garden you can walk through at your own pace. The Triton punches well above its weight for a free museum.
cards.why: Free, uncrowded, and the sculpture garden is perfect on a clear afternoon.

cards.name: Elyse Restaurant
cards.address: 96 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Elyse Restaurant opens at 5 PM so you can snag an early table before the Friday crowd fills it up. Order whatever looks good and take your time.
cards.why: It's a Friday night and you deserve a proper sit-down dinner.

cards.name: $9 Before 9 pm Happy Hour
cards.address: 48 South 1st Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to 48 South 1st Street for the $9 Before 9 PM Happy Hour. It's a great low-key cap to the night with a drink in hand.
cards.why: Nine bucks for a happy hour drink downtown on a Friday is a solid deal.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a walk along the creek trail. The morning air is cool and the path is shaded, so it's a genuinely pleasant way to shake off the early hour before the day heats up.
cards.why: 52 degrees and shaded trails is basically perfect walking weather.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. Real mummies, real artifacts, real hieroglyphics in San Jose. Walk the grounds and the planetarium garden while you're at it.
cards.why: It's one of those places locals haven't been to in years and always say they should go back.

cards.name: Euro Grill
cards.address: 980 El Camino Real #100, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Euro Grill in Santa Clara. It's fast, affordable, and the food punches well above the price point.
cards.why: Quick, delicious, and you won't feel weighed down for the afternoon.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art and spend time in the sculpture garden out back. The current exhibitions rotate often and admission is free, so there's no reason to rush through.
cards.why: Free contemporary art with outdoor space is a genuinely good Friday afternoon.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Smash City Rage Rooms and break some stuff. It's exactly what it sounds like and it's a blast.
cards.why: Friday afternoon stress has a very satisfying solution here.

cards.name: How Exploration, Uncertainty, and Risk Help Us Find Meaning with Author Alex Hutchinson
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Author Alex Hutchinson is talking about how exploration, uncertainty, and risk shape a meaningful life. Good conversation for a Thursday morning if you're into that kind of thing.
cards.why: Free talk, interesting subject, and it fits perfectly after the museum.

cards.name: Fish With You鱼你在一起Mountain View
cards.address: 156 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Fish With You on Castro and order one of their malatang or fish hot pot bowls. Bold flavors, solid lunch spot.
cards.why: Castro Street is the move for lunch and this place delivers.

cards.name: Electrify Your Home: A Community Workshop
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Drop into this free community workshop on electrifying your home. Practical, relevant, and you'll probably leave knowing something you didn't before.
cards.why: Free evening event and actually useful if you own or rent in the area.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Next Level Board Game Cafe has hundreds of games to pick from. Grab a drink, pull something off the shelf, and settle in for a couple hours.
cards.why: Best way to spend a Thursday afternoon with no agenda.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the water — it's shaded, flat, and genuinely pleasant on a cool morning like this.
cards.why: 55 degrees and sunny is just about perfect for a creekside walk.

cards.name: Knit-Alongs at Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Drop into the Knit-Along at Cupertino Library. Bring a project or just show up and see what it's about — these are low-key, social, and surprisingly fun even if you're a beginner.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely different way to spend a Friday afternoon.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the bamboo groves and Japanese garden terraces at Hakone. It's quiet on weekday mornings and the scenery is legitimately stunning.
cards.why: This place has a way of making you forget you're in the middle of Silicon Valley.

cards.name: Saratoga Meat & Fish
cards.address: 14320 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd #5, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Stop by Saratoga Meat and Fish to pick up something good for the weekend, or just browse — it's the kind of specialty shop that makes a quick errand feel like a treat.
cards.why: A cut above what you'd find at any grocery store and worth the visit.

cards.name: Uncorked
cards.address: 14500 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: End the day at Uncorked on Big Basin Way. Grab a glass on the patio and settle into the Friday evening pace of Saratoga Village.
cards.why: There's nowhere better to close out a Friday than with wine in Saratoga's most charming little stretch.

cards.name: Sue's Gallery Cafe
cards.address: 14665 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Sue's Gallery Cafe on Big Basin Way. Grab a coffee and something from the menu on the patio and ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a charming spot to kick off a Friday morning before the day gets going.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Fremont Older Open Space. Take the upper loop for the sweeping foothill views — it's genuinely beautiful and you'll probably have the trails mostly to yourself.
cards.why: 73°F high and morning cool makes this the perfect window for a hike.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then spend time with the contemporary galleries inside. It's free and genuinely world-class for a local institution.
cards.why: You'll be glad you went — most people sleep on this place.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Check out Jessica Monette's Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming in the Project Room at SCU. It's a contemplative, intimate exhibition worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free art, good vibes, and it's already in your orbit for the afternoon.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon at Picchetti Winery up on Montebello Road. Grab a glass of wine, sit outside, and take in the hillside views. It closes at 5 so get there with enough time to settle in.
cards.why: A gorgeous way to end the active part of the day before dinner.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House in San Jose. Go for the sukiyaki hot pot and let the evening slow down over a proper sit-down meal.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner that feels like an event on its own.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Start the morning at the de Saisset Museum at SCU with Jessica Monette's 'Root Me in the Soil' — intimate, earthy work in the Project Room that's worth sitting with for a few minutes.
cards.why: Free art on a crisp morning is a great way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Iraklis Restaurant
cards.address: 2165 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Iraklis for lunch — solid Greek food on El Camino, the kind of place where you order more than you planned to and don't regret it.
cards.why: Under $15 a head for food this good is a deal you take.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: While you're already there, walk through the State of Illusion exhibition and see how the two shows play off each other — very different vibes in the same building.
cards.why: Two shows, one stop, zero dollars.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art, including time in the sculpture garden out back where the afternoon light actually makes everything look better.
cards.why: It's consistently one of the best free art spaces in the South Bay.

cards.name: Knit-Alongs at Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Swing by the Knit-Along at Cupertino Library — bring your own project or just show up and see what people are working on, it's a low-key, no-pressure hang.
cards.why: If you knit or have been meaning to get back into it, this is a surprisingly fun Friday afternoon thing.

cards.name: Moods. Wine Bar and Bistro
cards.address: 111 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Moods Wine Bar and Bistro on Evelyn Ave. Order a glass of something good, share some small plates, and settle in.
cards.why: A 4.9 you don't need to dress up for is a rare thing, and Friday night here just feels right.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at SaiGon BreadFast — grab a bánh mì or a savory Vietnamese breakfast bite to kick things off right.
cards.why: It's open now and a solid way to start a Friday morning without overthinking it.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head into the Computer History Museum and lose yourself in the exhibits — the history of computing is genuinely wild and the displays are way more interesting than you'd expect.
cards.why: Two hours here goes fast, and it's the kind of place that makes you feel smarter without feeling like homework.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk the Mountain View Farmers Market, pick up some fresh produce, snack on whatever looks good, and browse the stalls without any rush.
cards.why: Free, outdoors, and the morning air at 53°F is perfect for a slow stroll through the market.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a walk or just find a spot to sit by the water at Shoreline Park. The bay views open up nicely in the afternoon and it's a great way to stretch out after lunch.
cards.why: 73°F in the afternoon with bay views is exactly what a Friday should feel like.

cards.name: Gourmet Works
cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Gourmet Works in Los Altos, a kitchen and specialty food shop that's fun to wander through even if you don't need anything.
cards.why: Great for picking up something interesting to cook or drink this weekend.

cards.name: Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels
cards.address: 477 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a bagel at Izzy's — grab a classic with lox and cream cheese and eat it at one of the outdoor tables on California Ave.
cards.why: Best bagels in Palo Alto, full stop.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Stanford Foothills trail up to the Dish for sweeping views of the Bay and the peninsula. The climb is worth it and the wide fire roads make for a great brisk walk.
cards.why: It's going to be a gorgeous 73°F afternoon and you want to be outside before lunch.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Clean up and head to Dinah's for a relaxed poolside lunch. Order something from the grill and sit outside — it's one of those spots that feels like a mini vacation.
cards.why: Poolside patio on a warm spring Friday is a very good idea.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits cover everything from early mainframes to the internet age and it genuinely moves fast even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: It's a seriously impressive collection and the afternoon indoor stretch is well timed.

cards.name: Thai Smile Bodywork & Massage
cards.address: 467 Hamilton Ave Suite 26, 2nd Floor, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Thai Smile Bodywork on Hamilton Ave. Post-hike, post-museum, this is the move.
cards.why: You'll feel completely reset going into the evening.

cards.name: Coffee & Water Lab
cards.address: 603 Saratoga Ave #40, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a solid cup at Coffee & Water Lab — order whatever they're pouring and settle in for a bit before the city wakes up.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone and this place does it right.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through real Egyptian mummies, ancient artifacts, and hieroglyphics at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. It's genuinely wild that this place exists in San Jose — give yourself the full two hours.
cards.why: It's one of those places you keep meaning to go and never do, so today's the day.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Smash City Rage Rooms and break some stuff. It's exactly what it sounds like and it's a blast.
cards.why: Friday afternoon energy is perfect for this kind of release.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Santana Row for dinner. Grab a table at Straits or Sino, then walk the boulevard after eating and see what's happening on the strip.
cards.why: It's a Friday night and Santana Row is the right place to be for it.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the trails at Rancho San Antonio. The lower loop is easy and gorgeous, but push up toward the farm if you want a real walk with views.
cards.why: 54 degrees at 9 AM is basically perfect hiking weather.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum. The Revolution exhibit alone is worth it, and the sheer scale of what they have on display will genuinely surprise you.
cards.why: You don't have to be a tech person to find this stuff fascinating.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 170 State St Unit 121, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at The Good Salad in Los Altos. Fresh, solid ingredients, and it's the kind of spot where you actually feel good after eating.
cards.why: It's a great midday reset after a busy morning.

cards.name: Gourmet Works
cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Gourmet Works on Main St in Los Altos. Browse the cooking gear, pick up something fun, or just wander through one of the better kitchen shops in the area.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun browse even if you're not planning to cook anything soon.

cards.name: K Spa and Bodywork
cards.address: 151 2nd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage or bodywork session at K Spa in Los Altos. It's a proper spa, so plan ahead if you can, but walk-ins sometimes work on a Friday afternoon.
cards.why: Best way to wind down before dinner.

cards.name: Tong Sui Desserts & Drinks (Palo Alto)
cards.address: 540 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night at Tong Sui Desserts in Palo Alto. Get the grass jelly or the mango drink, find a seat, and decompress after a full day.
cards.why: Light, sweet, and the perfect low-key way to end a Friday.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 3051 Tasman Dr, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at 1 Oz Coffee with a solid cup and something light to eat before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone for a good Saturday.

cards.name: The South Bay Needlepointers (SBN) Stitch-In
cards.blurb: Drop into the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In at the library. Even if you're brand new, it's a chill creative hang with people who actually know what they're doing.
cards.why: Free, social, and a genuinely fun way to try something different on a Saturday morning.

cards.name: Pav Bhaji and More Milpitas
cards.address: 529 S Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Pav Bhaji and More in Milpitas. The pav bhaji is the move, but poke around the menu because everything's affordable and solid.
cards.why: Hard to beat the value and flavor for a casual Saturday lunch.

cards.name: Retro Rewind
cards.address: 1343 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Retro Rewind in Milpitas. Browse the vintage finds, old-school gear, and whatever oddities they've got stocked up this week.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you walk out of with something you didn't know you needed.

cards.name: Cajun Crack'n Milpitas
cards.address: 275 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Cajun Crack'n for dinner. Get the seafood boil, pick your spice level, and dig in with your hands. It's messy and worth it.
cards.why: A Saturday night deserves a proper dinner, and this one delivers.

cards.name: Science, Technology, and Innovation Commission Regular Meeting
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at the Science, Technology, and Innovation Commission meeting. It's open to the public and a genuinely interesting window into what Milpitas is planning next.
cards.why: If you're curious about the city you're in, this is worth an hour of your evening.

cards.name: Clover Bakery & Cafe
cards.address: 4342 Moorpark Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Clover Bakery and grab a pastry and coffee before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key opener on a cool spring morning.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk through the rose garden while everything is still in bloom and the morning light is good.
cards.why: April is prime time for this place and it's one of the prettiest free spots in San Jose.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum, real mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and surprisingly deep exhibits for a San Jose afternoon.
cards.why: Nobody expects this to be as cool as it is, and it genuinely delivers every time.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU. It's the kind of show that messes with your perception in a good way.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a great way to spend the mid-afternoon.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge and paint a piece. No experience needed, just pick something off the shelf and go.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun thing to do together and something a little different for a Saturday night.

cards.name: Blossom & Bourbon Restaurant
cards.address: 632 Blossom Hill Rd, San Jose, CA 95123, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night at Blossom and Bourbon for dinner. Settle in, order something off the cocktail menu, and take your time.
cards.why: It's the right spot to wind down after a full day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the creek path. It's shaded, easy, and a genuinely pleasant way to ease into the day before the sun really heats up.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for a stroll before it warms up.

cards.name: kawaii diy studio - DIY Decoden Art Studio
cards.address: 19701 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into this DIY decoden art studio and make something fun. Pick your base, load it up with resin gems and charms, and walk out with a one-of-a-kind piece.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun and creative morning activity that most people have never tried.

cards.name: The Pastaria & Market
cards.address: 27 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to The Pastaria for lunch. Fresh pasta, a relaxed atmosphere, and a great spot to sit and actually enjoy the meal.
cards.why: Los Gatos has great lunch energy on a warm Saturday and this place delivers.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art. Walk the sculpture garden, then explore the contemporary galleries inside. Free admission and genuinely impressive work.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you feel good about discovering, and the sculpture garden is especially nice on a warm afternoon.

cards.name: Petals Creamery
cards.address: 5 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Petals Creamery for a scoop on your way back through Los Gatos. Rated insanely well for a reason, so don't skip it.
cards.why: Best ice cream in the area and a perfect afternoon treat after the museum.

cards.name: Massage of San Jose
cards.address: 938 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Massage of San Jose for a late afternoon unwind. Walk-in or call ahead to grab a slot.
cards.why: A Saturday evening massage hits different when the day has already been this good.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Santana Row. Stroll the boulevard, browse if anything catches your eye, and grab dinner at Sino or Straits with a patio table.
cards.why: Saturday night on Santana Row has great energy and plenty of good options for dinner.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: West Valley College parking lot, 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga, CA 95070
cards.blurb: Start the morning wandering the Saratoga Farmers Market at West Valley College. Pick up fresh produce, grab a pastry or breakfast bite from one of the vendors, and ease into the day at a relaxed pace.
cards.why: Cool morning air and a great excuse to snack your way through a Saturday market.

cards.name: Villa Montalvo Gardens
cards.address: Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the trails through Villa Montalvo Gardens and soak in the Mediterranean-style grounds, sculpture garden, and hillside paths before the afternoon crowds show up.
cards.why: It's one of those places that feels way more special than people expect.

cards.name: Book-Go-Round Used Bookstore
cards.address: 14410 Oak St, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Book-Go-Round on Oak Street in Saratoga and dig through the shelves for used books, vintage finds, and the occasional hidden gem.
cards.why: It's a genuinely great used bookstore and a fun browse after being outside.

cards.name: Loma Brewing Company
cards.address: 130 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Loma Brewing Company on Santa Cruz Ave for lunch. Grab a table, order a beer and whatever looks good on the menu, and settle in for a proper midday meal.
cards.why: Solid food, good beer, and a lively but laid-back vibe on a Saturday afternoon.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House in San Jose. Go all in on the sukiyaki hot pot experience and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's one of the most legit Japanese hot pot spots in the South Bay and worth the trip.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: West Valley College parking lot, 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga, CA 95070
cards.blurb: Start the morning wandering the Saratoga Farmers Market at West Valley College — grab something fresh to snack on, pick up local produce, and ease into the day at a relaxed pace.
cards.why: Cool morning temps and a local market is the perfect low-key Saturday kickoff.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Walk the terraced Japanese gardens at Hakone Estate, find a quiet bench by the koi pond, and just breathe for a bit. It genuinely feels like you've stepped out of the Bay Area entirely.
cards.why: The weather today is ideal for being outside, and this place earns its reputation.

cards.name: Sushi Heaven
cards.address: 12108 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Sushi Heaven for a solid sushi lunch — it's a small spot so keep it simple, order a few rolls, and enjoy the midday break.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hitting it right at noon means you're in and out fresh.

cards.name: Book-Go-Round Used Bookstore
cards.address: 14410 Oak St, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Book-Go-Round, one of those genuinely great used bookstores where you always leave with something you weren't expecting to find.
cards.why: A slow afternoon flip through used books is exactly the kind of thing weekends are for.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge in Los Gatos and paint a piece of pottery. It's hands-on, unhurried, and a surprisingly good time even if you've never done it before.
cards.why: A creative afternoon activity that doesn't feel like you're trying too hard.

cards.name: The Bywater
cards.address: 532 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at The Bywater on North Santa Cruz Ave. Sit at the bar or grab a table, order something from the Louisiana-inspired menu, and cap the day properly.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun dinner spot with good food and a lively Saturday evening vibe.

cards.name: Ceré Tea
cards.address: 18568 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Ceré Tea for a morning drink and a sit-down moment before the day gets going. Great spot to decompress and actually taste something good.
cards.why: A warm drink at a place like this beats a chain coffee stop any day.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Triton Museum of Art and spend some real time in the galleries — contemporary art, rotating exhibitions, and a sculpture garden out back worth walking through.
cards.why: Free, genuinely good art, and not the kind of place you'll fight crowds at.

cards.name: Ceré Tea
cards.address: 18568 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a tea and settle in for a bit at this Saratoga gem before the afternoon kicks off.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key morning stop that actually sets a good tone for the day.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: The State of Illusion Exhibition is worth the stop — expect immersive, perception-bending work that's more engaging than a standard gallery walk.
cards.why: Free and interesting, and it pairs well with the Triton visit if you're already in an arts headspace.

cards.name: Toby’s Board Game Club 一游未尽桌游吧
cards.address: 10805 N Wolfe Rd #106, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Toby's Board Game Club has a massive library of games and a great setup for adults who actually want to play. Grab a game from the shelf and go head to head for a few hours.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to spend a Saturday evening without just defaulting to a bar.

cards.name: Cap't Loui
cards.address: 19700 Vallco Pkwy #150, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Cap't Loui is a solid dinner finish in Cupertino. Order the seafood, split some dishes, and call it a night on a high note.
cards.why: Good food at a fair price point, and it's open late enough to make a proper dinner out of it.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours digging into the full sweep of computing history, from room-sized mainframes to the machines that changed everything. It's genuinely fascinating even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: The exhibits are wild in a way that sneaks up on you.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Book a Thai massage to decompress after the afternoon. A solid way to reset before dinner.
cards.why: Your legs will thank you after a full day of walking.

cards.name: GK Pastry
cards.address: 809 Cuesta Dr, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at GK Pastry with a coffee and something from the pastry case. It's a legit neighborhood bakery worth the visit.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Saturday morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum. Even if you're not a tech person, the exhibits on how we got from punch cards to pocket computers are genuinely wild.
cards.why: It's one of the best museums in the Bay Area and you can actually lose track of time in there.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the Mountain View Farmers Market and pick up whatever looks good. Vendors change with the season so there's always something worth grabbing.
cards.why: It's a perfect farmers market morning temperature-wise and you're already in the neighborhood.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head over to Shoreline Park for a walk along the water. The bay views open up fast once you get past the lake, and the afternoon light is great.
cards.why: High of 76°F makes this the perfect window to be outside.

cards.name: OMESCAPE | Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1135 E Arques Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book an escape room at OMESCAPE in Sunnyvale. They do themed rooms for groups and it's a solid hour of actually having to use your brain together.
cards.why: Great way to flip the energy from chill afternoon to something a little more competitive.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 3990 El Camino Real unit c, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee at ZombieRunner, a quirky running-culture cafe that pulls a solid shot and keeps things low-key on a Saturday morning.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun spot with good coffee and no pretense.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a midday massage at the Massage Therapy Center right on California Ave. They do solid therapeutic work and it's a genuinely relaxing way to spend 60-90 minutes.
cards.why: After walking the market, this is an easy and satisfying segue into the afternoon.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Stroll the California Ave Farmers Market, pick up whatever looks good, and just wander — this stretch on a Saturday morning is one of the better ways to spend an hour in Palo Alto.
cards.why: It's free, it's lively, and the produce and vendor variety are worth the walk.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Head up to The Dish Trail for a solid afternoon hike in the Stanford foothills. The views open up fast as you climb and the big dish itself is a great landmark to loop around.
cards.why: It's an iconic South Bay hike and the afternoon light up there is hard to beat.

cards.name: Topi Cakes
cards.address: 2305 El Camino Real B, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Topi Cakes for a late-morning treat turned lunch dessert situation. Their cakes and pastries are seriously good, and the space is cute without being over the top.
cards.why: You won't find a better cake stop in this part of Palo Alto.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Cool down with a walk through Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The Rodin sculpture garden outside is genuinely impressive, and the rotating indoor galleries are always worth a look.
cards.why: Free world-class art at a beautiful campus museum is a no-brainer end to an active afternoon.

cards.name: Roy's Station Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 197 Jackson St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Roy's Station, a converted gas station turned coffee shop. Grab an espresso drink and settle in — it's a genuinely cool spot to ease into a Saturday.
cards.why: Best way to kick off the day before the city wakes up.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art and take your time in both the galleries and the sculpture garden out back. The contemporary exhibits rotate often and the outdoor space is genuinely lovely on a day like this.
cards.why: Free world-class art with a sculpture garden you can actually walk around in.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Head over to the Cupertino Farmers Market and walk the stalls. Pick up some fresh produce, snack on whatever looks good, and enjoy the cool morning before it warms up.
cards.why: 52 degrees in the morning at an outdoor market is honestly perfect.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum has real mummies, real hieroglyphics, and a planetarium tucked into a gorgeous Egyptian Revival building in San Jose. It's one of those places that sounds weird until you're actually inside and completely hooked.
cards.why: You'll spend way longer in here than you planned and that's a good thing.

cards.name: Loto-Ja Ice Cream
cards.address: 74 S 24th St, San Jose, CA 95116, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Loto-Ja for some ice cream after the museum. Small shop, great flavors, and a solid afternoon treat before the next stop.
cards.why: It's about to hit 77 degrees and you've earned it.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the evening at Santana Row strolling the boulevard, browsing the boutiques, and grabbing dinner at one of the restaurant patios. Straits, Sino, or the Zinnia patio are all solid calls.
cards.why: The Row on a warm Saturday evening has a great vibe and plenty of options for dinner.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with a Thai massage at Star Thai in Sunnyvale. Book ahead if you can. A solid way to unwind after a full day on your feet.
cards.why: Nothing better than ending a packed day with an actual massage.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 101 Forest Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Philz on Forest Ave. Get a cup custom-blended to order and ease into the day on their patio as the weather warms up.
cards.why: A proper Saturday starts with a proper cup of coffee.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio while it's still cool. The Deer Hollow Farm loop is a solid two-hour route that's scenic without being brutal.
cards.why: 77 degrees by afternoon means morning is the perfect window to be outside.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 170 State St Unit 121, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by The Good Salad in Los Altos for lunch. Fresh build-your-own bowls that actually fill you up after a hike.
cards.why: It's the kind of lunch you feel good about after a morning on the trails.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum in Mountain View. The Revolution exhibit walks you through the full arc of computing history in a way that's genuinely wild even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: This place holds up every single time.

cards.name: Alegio Chocolaté / Claudio Corallo PALO ALTO
cards.address: 522 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Alegio Chocolaté in Palo Alto for some of the most serious single-origin chocolate you'll find anywhere. Taste a few pieces, pick up a bar to take home.
cards.why: It's a genuinely special shop and a fun afternoon detour.

cards.name: Aging with Grace: The Life of the Great Basin Bristlecone Pine A talk by Tim Aston at Los Altos Library
cards.blurb: Tonight at Los Altos Library, Tim Aston gives a talk on the ancient bristlecone pines of the Great Basin. These trees are thousands of years old and the story behind them is genuinely fascinating.
cards.why: Free, local, and the kind of talk you'll still be thinking about next week.

cards.name: Jelly Donut
cards.address: 2665 Cropley Ave, San Jose, CA 95132, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a fresh donut and coffee from Jelly Donut — grab a classic glazed or go for one of their specialty options while the day warms up.
cards.why: Hard to beat a 52-degree morning with a warm donut in hand.

cards.name: Ed R. Levin County Park
cards.address: 3100 Calaveras Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Ed R. Levin County Park and stretch your legs on the trails before the afternoon heat kicks in. The views from up on the hillside are worth the climb.
cards.why: The weather is perfect for a morning hike and you'll want to be outside on a day like this.

cards.name: Anton
cards.address: 1969 O'Toole Way, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Anton is a solid lunch stop with a tight, well-executed menu. Order whatever's fresh and grab a table before the Saturday crowd fills in.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so this is the sweet spot to catch it.

cards.name: Retro Rewind
cards.address: 1343 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Retro Rewind is loaded with vintage arcade games, pinball machines, and all the nostalgia you can handle. Pay to play and just go wild for a couple hours.
cards.why: This kind of place is a blast on a Saturday afternoon.

cards.name: Massage Envy
cards.address: 581 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Massage Envy. Book a session ahead of time and let the day fully melt off.
cards.why: After hiking and arcade games, your body will thank you.

cards.name: Los Gatos Spring Into Green Event
cards.address: Plaza Park, Los Gatos, CA
cards.blurb: The town celebrates its 10-year anniversary of Spring Into Green with over 20 interactive booths, music, entertainment, flower crowns, bike valet, farmers market, and State of the Town address by Mayo
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Danbi Korean Restaurant Los Gatos
cards.address: 9 Montebello Way, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Danbi for Korean lunch — think well-crafted bowls and solid banchan in a relaxed Los Gatos setting.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so this is your window, and it's worth it.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Walk into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge and paint a piece of pottery at your own pace — no experience needed, just pick a piece and go.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun afternoon activity that doesn't feel touristy.

cards.name: San Jose Japantown: From Bachelor Society to Family
cards.blurb: Check out the San Jose Japantown exhibit tracing the neighborhood's history from a bachelor society to thriving family community — it's a fascinating slice of local history.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and not something you'd stumble into on a regular weekend.

cards.name: Dolce Spazio Gelato
cards.address: 221 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Dolce Spazio for a scoop or two of gelato on N Santa Cruz Ave before dinner.
cards.why: You're already in Los Gatos and this place is genuinely great gelato.

cards.name: Bai Tong
cards.address: 6011 14515 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Bai Tong in Saratoga with Thai food that punches well above its price point — order the curries and something with basil.
cards.why: A solid dinner spot to close out a full day on a high note.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: April is peak bloom season here, so walk the rows and actually stop to smell the roses — seriously, thousands of them.
cards.why: Perfect spring morning for it before the day heats up.

cards.name: Aids: A People’s History of the Pandemic at King Library
cards.blurb: Walk through this exhibition at King Library and sit with the history it's documenting. It's heavier than a typical gallery visit but absolutely worth your attention.
cards.why: Free, thoughtful, and the kind of thing you'll actually think about later.

cards.name: Sjma's 2026 Gala + Auction After-Party
cards.address: San José Museum of Art, 110 South Market Street, San José, CA 95113
cards.blurb: Late-night celebration featuring live DJ sets, open bar, light bites, and dancing to support the Museum's biggest fundraiser of the year.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Let's Hike Event: Saratoga to the Skyline Trail
cards.address: 17301 Skyline Blvd, Saratoga, CA 95070
cards.blurb: Moderately challenging 5.1-mile guided hike with 1,026-foot elevation gain and panoramic views, taking approximately 2.5 hours. Registration and liability waiver required.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Swing through the Saratoga Farmers Market to grab a coffee, some fresh pastries, and maybe a bag of stone fruit to snack on later.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into the morning before hitting the trail.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the bamboo groves and koi ponds at Hakone Estate and Gardens — slow pace, beautiful detail, totally restorative after a morning hike.
cards.why: It genuinely feels like a different world and it's exactly the right vibe post-trail.

cards.name: Sunflower Spa
cards.address: 14501 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Get a treatment at Sunflower Spa on Big Basin Way to stretch out those post-hike legs and reset before the afternoon.
cards.why: Your muscles will thank you and it keeps the whole day in the same chill Saratoga zone.

cards.name: Spring Wine Walk
cards.blurb: Head to Los Gatos for the Spring Wine Walk and stroll through downtown sipping local pours at participating spots along the way.
cards.why: A free afternoon wine walk on a warm Saturday is basically perfect planning.

cards.name: Antoine’s Cookie Shop Los Gatos
cards.address: 35 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Antoine's Cookie Shop and grab a couple cookies before dinner — the selection is serious and the quality backs it up.
cards.why: You're already in Los Gatos and this place is genuinely worth the detour.

cards.name: Los Gatos Soda Works
cards.address: 21 College Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into Los Gatos Soda Works for dinner and drinks — good food, lively atmosphere, and a great way to close out a full day.
cards.why: It's the kind of spot that makes a Saturday feel complete.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the trails around Vasona Lake while the morning is still cool. Grab a bench by the water or loop the perimeter path at whatever pace feels right.
cards.why: 78 degrees later means now is the sweet spot for being outside.

cards.name: Sanity Thai Massage
cards.address: 20 S Santa Cruz Ave STE 105, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Sanity Thai Massage for a midday session. Book a traditional Thai massage or a foot treatment and actually decompress for an hour.
cards.why: You'll feel like a different person walking into the afternoon.

cards.name: One Fish Raw Bar
cards.address: 193 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head to One Fish Raw Bar for lunch. Order oysters, something off the raw bar, and a glass of wine. It's a proper sit-down situation.
cards.why: One of the best raw bars in the South Bay and lunch here never disappoints.

cards.name: Book-Go-Round Used Bookstore
cards.address: 14410 Oak St, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Book-Go-Round in Saratoga. It's the kind of used bookstore where you always leave with something you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Slow, no-pressure afternoon browsing at its best.

cards.name: Wildfire Preparedness Workshop
cards.address: Redwood Estates, Los Gatos
cards.blurb: Attend the Santa Clara County Fire Department Wildfire Preparedness Workshop in Los Gatos. Learn practical steps for protecting your home and neighborhood from wildfire risk.
cards.why: Living in the hills, this is genuinely useful information to have.

cards.name: Santa Clara County Fire Department Wildfire Workshop - Los Gatos
cards.address: Redwood Estates Pavilion, Los Gatos, CA
cards.blurb: Community workshop teaching wildfire preparedness using the Ready, Set, Go! model, with speakers and resource fair featuring CAL FIRE, American Red Cross, PG&E, and local law enforcement.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Ritual at Manresa
cards.address: 320 Village Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Ritual at Manresa for a dinner worth lingering over. This is a high-end experience, so settle in and let the kitchen do its thing.
cards.why: A Saturday night deserves a proper meal, and this is one of Los Gatos' best.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown with mochi from Okayama Kobo and a slow wander down Jackson St before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a morning before the sale.

cards.name: Shop at our Nikkei Treasures Sale!
cards.address: 535 North 5th Street, San Jose, CA, 95112
cards.blurb: During Nikkei Matsuri 2026, come and shop at our annual Nikkei Treasures rummage sale in our parking lot! Shop deals on Japanese art, antiquities, ceramics, houseware and more! Trying to test your…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum and see real mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and artifacts that have no business being this close to home.
