cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out Fists of Fury at King Library, an exhibition exploring the history of martial arts in Asian American cinema and culture.
cards.why: It's free, it's fascinating, and it fits perfectly after the museum.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk through the rose garden while everything is in bloom and the late afternoon light is doing its thing.
cards.why: April is peak bloom season and it's hard to beat a free, beautiful outdoor walk.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Santana Row for dinner on the boulevard. Grab a table at Straits, Sino, or the Zinnia patio and let the evening stretch out.
cards.why: Warm Saturday evening, good food, outdoor seating. That's the whole pitch.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at SaiGon BreadFast and grab a bánh mì or some Vietnamese breakfast bites to kick things off right.
cards.why: It's a great low-key way to ease into the morning without overthinking it.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the Computer History Museum's exhibits — the timeline of computing from punch cards to the iPhone is genuinely wild, and the Robot Hall of Fame never gets old.
cards.why: Two hours flies by in here, and it pairs perfectly with the sound bath energy you're building toward tonight.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk off breakfast along the bay trail at Shoreline Park, watch the kitesurfers, and let the 76-degree afternoon do its thing.
cards.why: The weather today is exactly what this place was made for.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 170 State St Unit 121, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Head to The Good Salad in Los Altos for a fresh, satisfying lunch. Load up a bowl and settle in.
cards.why: It's the kind of lunch that makes you feel good going into an afternoon without weighing you down.

cards.name: Kua Body
cards.address: 197 1st St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Kua Body and let someone work out the week's tension before tonight's sound bath. They do bodywork and massage-style treatments worth settling into.
cards.why: Going into a sound bath already relaxed is a completely different experience.

cards.name: RAJARANI Bakery and Restaurant
cards.address: 952 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Grab an early dinner at RAJARANI Bakery and Restaurant. South Indian food done well, and the portion sizes will hold you comfortably through the evening.
cards.why: You'll want real food before the sound bath, and this hits the spot without being too heavy.

cards.name: April Full Immersion Sound Bath Saturday, April 25th | 6:30pm–8:00pm
cards.address: 324 Castro Street, Mountain View, CA, 94041
cards.blurb: April Full Immersion Sound Bath on April 25th - Designed as a seasonal reset, this 90-minute experience supports nervous system regulation. Through gong, crystal bowls, voice, and drum, you’ll be guid
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Greenlee's Bakery
cards.address: 1081 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Greenlee's Bakery on The Alameda. Grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day before things get moving.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so go early while the good stuff is still on the shelf.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, real ancient artifacts, and an Egyptian garden courtyard you will not believe is in San Jose. Walk through the tomb replica and take your time in the galleries.
cards.why: This is genuinely one of the most surprising museums in the Bay Area and it never gets old.

cards.name: Voltaire Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 360 S Market St #80, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Voltaire Coffee Roasters for a proper afternoon coffee. Good espresso drinks and a chill vibe to decompress before the next stop.
cards.why: You need a breather before the evening and this is a solid spot to get one.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: The Municipal Rose Garden is at its absolute best in spring. Walk the paths, take some photos, and soak up the 80-degree afternoon before heading to your evening event.
cards.why: Perfect April weather for it and the roses should be blooming right now.

cards.name: Fiber Arts Circle at Almaden
cards.address: 6445 Camden Ave. San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to Almaden for Play Your Way, Sensory Spring. It's a hands-on, sensory-friendly event worth checking out even if you don't have kids tagging along.
cards.why: It's free and a fun, low-key way to wrap up the active part of your day.

cards.name: Play Your Way - Sensory Spring
cards.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
cards.blurb: Sensory-friendly evening event designed for children with autism and their families, featuring hands-on museum activities with limited attendance and reduced noise and bright lights.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
cards.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown. Pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo, grab a coffee, and wander Jackson St before the crowds show up.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and it's a genuinely good morning walk.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton punches way above its weight for a free museum. Walk the sculpture garden first, then head inside for the contemporary art exhibitions.
cards.why: Free, legitimately great art, and the sculpture garden is perfect on a warm morning like this.

cards.name: Nabi Cat Cafe
cards.address: 2255 The Alameda, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Nabi Cat Cafe for lunch and some cat time. Order a drink, find a spot, and let the cats come to you.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun adult afternoon stop and the vibe is hard to beat.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: The State of Illusion Exhibition is worth a proper slow look. Give yourself time to move through it without rushing.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and it wraps up well before you need to head to the evening lecture.

cards.name: Hapa’s Brewing Company
cards.address: 59 Barack Obama Blvd, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Quick stop at Hapa's Brewing for a pre-lecture beer. Grab a pint at the bar and decompress for 20 minutes.
cards.why: Great craft beer and it's a solid way to transition into the evening.

cards.name: SJSU Art Galleries Tuesday Night Lecture: Ken Rinaldo at ART 133: Art Lecture Hall
cards.blurb: The Evolution of Information is Life Ken Rinaldo is an internationally recognized artist known for interactive robotic and bio-art installations. His work explores hybrid ecologies between humans,…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Sweetdragon Baking Company
cards.address: 898 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Sweetdragon Baking Company. Grab a pastry and a coffee and ease into the day at one of San Jose's best-loved neighborhood bakeries.
cards.why: A 4.9 rating doesn't lie — this place is worth the stop.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library before the salon kicks off. It's a free deep-dive into the history of martial arts in Asian American cinema and culture.
cards.why: You're already heading to King Library at noon, so this is a perfect warm-up.

cards.name: Philharmonia Baroque's Artist Salon--Shunske Sato at Martin Luther King Junior Library
cards.blurb: Philharmonia Baroque’s Artist Salons are pre-concert talks led by performers, scholars, and guest artists. Offered a few weeks before each concert set, they give audiences historical and cultural…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along Guadalupe River Park and Gardens. It's a surprisingly green and calm stretch right through downtown, good for a relaxed stroll without any agenda.
cards.why: Perfect weather to be outside in the middle of the afternoon.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the late afternoon at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. Real mummies, real hieroglyphics, and genuinely fascinating exhibits in a building that looks like it was plucked out of ancient Egypt.
cards.why: It's one of the most unexpected places in all of San Jose and it earns every bit of its reputation.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House. This is a proper sit-down sukiyaki experience with top-tier wagyu beef cooked at the table. Slow down, order well, and make an evening of it.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner that turns a good Saturday into a great one.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on early computing, AI, and internet history are genuinely wild even if you're not a tech person. Give yourself time to wander.
cards.why: It sounds nerdy but everyone who goes ends up staying longer than planned.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 736 N Mathilda Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Grab an early dinner at Dish N Dash in Sunnyvale. Middle Eastern staples done right at a price that won't hurt. Get the shawarma and something with hummus.
cards.why: Solid, filling food that won't leave you sluggish for the evening.

cards.name: Book Launch with David Levithan and Gabriel Duckels
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Thursday, April 23 at 6pm
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: DeMartini Orchard
cards.address: 66 N San Antonio Rd, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into DeMartini Orchard and pick up seasonal fruit, local honey, or whatever looks good at the stand today. It's a great way to decompress after the hike.
cards.why: It's a Los Altos gem that most people drive right past.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum is genuinely wild in the best way. Walk through the Revolution exhibit, check out the Babbage Engine, and give yourself more time than you think you'll need.
cards.why: Two solid hours here and you'll leave with your mind rearranged a little.

cards.name: American Cancer Society Discovery Shop - Los Altos, CA
cards.address: 243 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: The American Cancer Society Discovery Shop on Main Street is a proper thrift find, well-curated and worth a slow browse after the book event.
cards.why: You're already in Los Altos, and this shop consistently has good stuff on the shelves.

cards.name: Middle Grade Book Club: Space for Saffron with author Rie Neal
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Sunday, April 26 at 4pm
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 650 Castro St #130, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid cup at 1 Oz Coffee on Castro St. Good spot to wake up and ease into the day.
cards.why: Perfect way to kick off a Saturday morning before things get busy.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the Computer History Museum. The exhibits on computing history are genuinely wild even if you're not a tech person. Give yourself the full two hours.
cards.why: It's one of those places that sounds nerdy but ends up being fascinating to basically everyone.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the Mountain View Farmers Market and pick up whatever looks good. Fresh produce, local vendors, easy Saturday vibes.
cards.why: It's free, it's fun, and Saturday morning at a farmers market just hits different.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along the water at Shoreline Park. The bay views are wide open and the weather today is ideal for a relaxed stroll.
cards.why: 76 degrees and sunny is exactly the kind of afternoon you want to spend outside.

cards.name: UMe | Tea & Snacks
cards.address: 220 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into UMe for tea and snacks on Castro St. Great spot to recharge with something light and browse the menu at your own pace.
cards.why: A relaxed afternoon tea break is a solid move before an evening event.

cards.name: South Bay Ceramics - Studio 1954
cards.address: 1954 Old Middlefield Way STE I, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Try a drop-in ceramics session at South Bay Ceramics Studio 1954. Get your hands in some clay and make something actually cool.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to spend an afternoon and you walk away with something to show for it.

cards.name: Introduction To Crystal Singing Bowls - Friday - 4/24 •
cards.address: 324 Castro Street, Mountain View, CA, 94041
cards.blurb: Experience the unique resonance of crystal singing bowls through a guided listening session. Learn how different tones interact with the body and explore the difference between frosted and gemstone-in
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: GK Pastry
cards.address: 809 Cuesta Dr, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at GK Pastry. Grab a coffee and a pastry and ease into the day without any rush.
cards.why: It's one of the best morning stops in Mountain View and sets the right mood for the day.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum. The Steve Jobs, Read Me, and Algorithms of Love exhibitions are all inside, so you can move through all three without doubling back.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, the history here is genuinely wild and the exhibitions are all running now.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a walk along the water at Shoreline Park. The 76°F high makes this the sweet spot of the day to be outside.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a stroll before sitting down to eat.

cards.name: Urban Curry And Biryani
cards.address: 856 W El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Urban Curry And Biryani for lunch. Get the biryani and settle in for a proper midday meal.
cards.why: It's a great excuse to try some of the best South Asian food Mountain View has to offer.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into FireWorks Ceramics and try your hand at a pottery session. It's low-key, creative, and surprisingly absorbing.
cards.why: It's a fun way to spend an afternoon that doesn't feel like a typical tourist activity.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Arisa Thai Massage before the evening picks up. Book ahead if you can.
cards.why: A good massage before a 7 PM event means you show up relaxed and ready.

cards.name: How To Choose Crystals With Confidence: Friday May 1st, 7pm-9pm
cards.address: 324 Castro Street, Mountain View, CA, 94041

cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Holy Cannoli! Catering
cards.address: 138 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with something sweet from Holy Cannoli! — grab a cannoli and a coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Perfect low-key start before the farmers market crowd picks up.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Weekly open-air farmers market featuring local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stay on Santana Row after the market and settle into a patio lunch at Straits, Sino, or Zinnia — take your time, the vibe here is made for lingering.
cards.why: You're already there and the afternoon is heating up nicely, so a shaded patio seat is exactly right.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a proper couple of hours inside the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum — actual mummies, ancient artifacts, and a surprisingly immersive replica tomb you can walk through.
cards.why: It never gets old and the $9–$12 ticket is absolutely worth it.

cards.name: Coffee & Water Lab
cards.address: 603 Saratoga Ave #40, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Coffee & Water Lab with a well-crafted pour and something light to eat before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone and this place does it right.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Walk the terraced Japanese gardens at Hakone Estate and decompress in one of the most beautifully kept outdoor spaces in the South Bay.
cards.why: The weather is perfect for wandering outside and this place genuinely feels like a different world.

cards.name: Band Jam: Guitar & Ukulele
cards.blurb: Grab your guitar, grab your ukulele - let's jam! Join us every week, singing and strumming along with popular songs in a fun, easy-going environment. Meet people who are also working on their music…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Swing through the Saratoga Farmers Market on Fruitvale Ave for fresh produce, local snacks, and a quick browse before your 10:30 anchor.
cards.why: It's right there on a perfect morning and farmers market Saturdays just feel good.

cards.name: Body Rest Massage Wellness Center
cards.address: 621 E Campbell Ave #11e, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Body Rest Massage Wellness Center in Campbell and let the afternoon be genuinely restful.
cards.why: A Saturday with a massage built in is a Saturday done correctly.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 20750 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dish N Dash in Cupertino for a solid Middle Eastern lunch. Order the wraps, mezze spreads, and something warm off the menu.
cards.why: You'll want a real sit-down meal after a full morning on your feet.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House in San Jose. Order the sukiyaki hot pot and go slow with it.
cards.why: It's a proper dinner experience worth stretching out on a Saturday night.

cards.name: Soquel Demonstration State Forest
cards.address: 29400 Highland Way, Los Gatos, CA 95033, USA
cards.blurb: Get into the redwoods early at Soquel Demonstration State Forest. The trails wind through second-growth forest and the morning light through the canopy is genuinely gorgeous.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a morning hike before the day heats up.

cards.name: Out of the Barrel
cards.address: 201 Saratoga-Los Gatos Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Out of the Barrel is a wine bar and bottle shop in Los Gatos. Grab a glass and a bite for lunch and settle into the laid-back vibe.
cards.why: Great spot to decompress after the hike with a solid pour and something to eat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge lets you paint your own pottery with no experience needed. Pick a piece, grab your paints, and spend a couple of hours making something you'll actually want to keep.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun afternoon activity that doesn't feel touristy at all.

cards.name: Drop-In Tech Help
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Swing by the Los Gatos Library for Drop-In Tech Help. Bring any device giving you grief and get hands-on help from the volunteers on staff.
cards.why: Free, useful, and you're already in Los Gatos, so why not knock it out.

cards.name: Online Author Talk : Jason Reynolds
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Head to Online Author Talk : Jason Reynolds and see what's going on.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: PintxoPote
cards.address: 424 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: PintxoPote on Santa Cruz Ave is a Basque pintxos bar. Order several small bites, grab a glass of txakoli, and graze your way through the evening menu.
cards.why: It opens at 5 and fits perfectly as a relaxed end to the day.

cards.name: Moonwake Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 1412 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Moonwake Coffee Roasters and get into a proper Saturday headspace with a well-pulled espresso drink and something to snack on.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone and this place actually takes it seriously.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander through the Japanese gardens at Hakone Estate, find a bench near the koi pond, and let the whole place slow you down for a couple hours.
cards.why: Perfect weather for walking around outside and this garden genuinely feels like a different world.

cards.name: Sushi Heaven
cards.address: 12108 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Sushi Heaven before the kitchen closes. It's a small lunch window so go straight there and order generously.
cards.why: Solid sushi at a reasonable price and the lunch hours are tight, so it rewards people who plan ahead.

cards.name: Beyond Text Bookstore
cards.address: 318 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Beyond Text Bookstore on North Santa Cruz Ave. It's the kind of shop where you go in for one thing and leave with four.
cards.why: A genuinely great independent bookstore is worth making time for.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Furry Reading Buddies is the anchor here. While you're at Saratoga Library, the farmers market at 14000 Fruitvale is steps away to pick up some produce or snacks after.
cards.why: Already in Saratoga and the market wraps up the afternoon perfectly.

cards.name: Furry Reading Buddies for Grades K-5
cards.blurb: Buddy up with a furry friend and read aloud from your favorite book! Practice your reading skills and build up your self-confidence reading to a patient and furry listener (fully trained therapy…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Sea Salt Therapy
cards.address: 3199 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Sea Salt Therapy with a salt room session. Walk-in availability varies so call ahead, but it's a genuinely relaxing way to end an active day.
cards.why: After walking around all day, your body will thank you.

cards.name: Square Pie Guys
cards.address: 501 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night at Square Pie Guys in Campbell. Get a Detroit-style square pie and order more than you think you need.
cards.why: It's one of the best pizza spots in the South Bay and a great way to close out a Saturday.

cards.name: Fancy Nancy Tea Party
cards.blurb: Ooh la la! You are invited to a Fancy Nancy tea party! Enjoy special treats along with a read-aloud of "Fancy Nancy, Bonjour Butterfly". Learn a few fancy French words, and create a beautiful craft…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: His & Her Rocks Gallery
cards.address: 296 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into His & Her Rocks Gallery on State Street and browse the mineral and gem collection — it's a genuinely surprising little place.
cards.why: Quick, fun, and totally low-key after a morning event.

cards.name: Timothy Adams Chocolates
cards.address: 539 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Timothy Adams Chocolates and pick through their handmade truffles and bonbons — treat yourself, grab a box to take home.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you'd kick yourself for skipping on a Saturday afternoon.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum walking through exhibits on everything from punch cards to the internet — way more engaging than it sounds.
cards.why: The history on display here is genuinely wild and the afternoon pacing is perfect for it.

cards.name: K Spa and Bodywork
cards.address: 151 2nd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a treatment at K Spa and Bodywork in Los Altos — book a massage or bodywork session and let the day fully decompress.
cards.why: A Saturday evening spa session is hard to argue with.

cards.name: Clover Bakery & Cafe
cards.address: 4342 Moorpark Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Clover Bakery and grab a pastry or breakfast bite with your coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a Saturday morning with something good in hand.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Fremont Older for a solid morning hike with sweeping foothill views. The upper trails open up nicely and the crowds stay thin.
cards.why: 66 degrees in the morning with an 80 degree high makes this the sweet spot to be outside.

cards.name: Meyhouse Sunnyvale
cards.address: 187 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Clean up and head to Meyhouse for a sit-down lunch. The kitchen runs through 2 PM so you have a comfortable window to relax and eat well.
cards.why: Good food after a morning hike hits different.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art and walk the sculpture garden out back. The contemporary galleries are genuinely worth your time and the whole thing is free.
cards.why: It's the most underrated art stop in the South Bay and you'll be out well before your afternoon commitment.

cards.name: LEGO Club
cards.blurb: Join us at the Cupertino Library 3:30-4:30 PM! Students in Kindergarten to 8th grade are welcome to play and create with LEGOs on the first Tuesday of each month. Space is limited.
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the day at Santana Row. Walk the boulevard, browse a few shops, then grab a table on one of the patios for dinner and drinks.
cards.why: 80-degree weather on a Saturday evening with a good patio is basically perfect.

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 543 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid coffee at Peet's — grab a latte and something to snack on before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone for a good Sunday.

cards.name: THIÊN LONG SƠN - THE DRAGON MOUNTAIN
cards.address: 3215 Calaveras Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Thiên Long Sơn for a walk around the grounds. It's a peaceful spot with some really striking scenery — great way to stretch your legs before lunch.
cards.why: The morning temps are perfect for being outside before it warms up.

cards.name: Ushiya Grill
cards.address: 1792 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Ushiya Grill for lunch and try the local favorites off their menu.
cards.why: It's one of those spots people keep coming back to for a reason.

cards.name: Retro Rewind
cards.address: 1343 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Retro Rewind is a spot packed with vintage games, retro arcade machines, and throwback vibes. Kill a couple hours flipping through nostalgia.
cards.why: It's a great wind-down after the escape room while still keeping the fun going.

cards.name: Tong Sui Desserts & Drinks (Milpitas)
cards.address: 265 W Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Tong Sui for a sweet drink or dessert to hold you over before the evening event. Their menu is full of Hong Kong-style drinks and desserts that are worth trying.
cards.why: Under $15 and genuinely delicious — easy yes.

cards.name: Science, Technology, and Innovation Commission Regular Meeting
cards.blurb: Wrap the day at the Science, Technology, and Innovation Commission meeting. It's a free public meeting where you can hear what's shaping Milpitas's tech future.
cards.why: Free, local, and actually interesting if you care about what's happening in the city.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls on Campbell Ave, pick up fresh produce, sample whatever looks good, and take your time — this market is worth the full loop.
cards.why: Sunday farmers markets are one of the best things about living here and this one is genuinely good.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, real artifacts, and a full Egyptian temple — explore the galleries and spend some time in the planetarium building and courtyard.
cards.why: It's one of the most legitimately surprising museums in the entire Bay Area.

cards.name: La Traviata
cards.blurb: Catch La Traviata this afternoon — get there a few minutes early, grab your seat, and let Verdi do the rest.
cards.why: Live opera on a Sunday afternoon is a genuinely great way to spend a few hours.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Walk the market stalls on Campbell Ave, grab a fresh pastry or breakfast bite from one of the vendors, and pick up some local produce while the morning is still crisp.
cards.why: Perfect morning temperature for wandering an outdoor market before the crowds build up.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the trail along Los Gatos Creek for a relaxed walk through the shade. The creek path is easy, quiet, and a nice way to stretch out before lunch.
cards.why: That 72-degree high is going to feel great on a shaded trail with creek sounds in the background.

cards.name: La Jaiba
cards.address: 10 E Hamilton Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: La Jaiba in Campbell is the move for lunch. Order big, sit back, and let the food do the talking.
cards.why: One of the best-rated spots in Campbell and a local staple worth the stop.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. Real mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and ancient artifacts tucked away in a San Jose neighborhood most people drive past without knowing.
cards.why: This place genuinely surprises people every time and it's an easy way to spend a Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the Santana Row boulevard, pop into a few boutiques, and find a patio seat as the afternoon cools down. Great people-watching and a nice vibe before dinner.
cards.why: The outdoor walkway hits different on a warm spring evening with a drink in hand.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market for dinner and graze across multiple vendors. Pick up tacos from one stall, something else from another, and grab a drink on the patio to close out the night.
cards.why: Lets you mix and match instead of committing to one menu, which is honestly the right call after a full day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the Los Gatos Creek Trail — lace up and walk the shaded path along the creek while it's still cool and quiet out.
cards.why: 56 degrees and peaceful is basically perfect trail weather.

cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Downtown
cards.address: 340 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Grab brunch at Los Gatos Cafe Downtown on Santa Cruz Ave. It's a local staple with solid all-day breakfast fare, and the patio is a great spot to ease into the day.
cards.why: You need food after that walk and this place delivers.

cards.name: Beyond Text Bookstore
cards.address: 318 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wander into Beyond Text Bookstore and browse the shelves. It's the kind of shop where you go in for one thing and leave with three.
cards.why: Great way to kill an hour without screens.

cards.name: Sanity Thai Massage
cards.address: 20 S Santa Cruz Ave STE 105, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Sanity Thai Massage. Let someone work out whatever the trail didn't loosen up.
cards.why: A Sunday afternoon massage is exactly what adults without plans should be doing.

cards.name: Arbor Day Celebration & Ceremonial Tree Planting
cards.blurb: Head over to Saratoga for the Arbor Day Celebration and Ceremonial Tree Planting. It's free, it's outside, and it's a genuinely nice thing to be part of.
cards.why: It's a free community moment on a pretty Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: La Sunflower
cards.address: 20688 4th St, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into La Sunflower in Saratoga Village for some wellness browsing or a treatment. It's a lovely little stop to decompress before dinner.
cards.why: You're already in Saratoga, and this place has serious good-vibes energy.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dio Deka in Los Gatos. This is a proper Greek restaurant with an elevated menu. Go for the lamb, share some mezze, and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's Sunday, you've earned a nice dinner, and Dio Deka is the real deal.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Walk the Saratoga Farmers Market and grab breakfast on the go — fresh pastries, fruit, local honey, all of it out in the cool morning air before the crowds show up.
cards.why: It's free, it's local, and 54°F mornings were made for wandering a farmers market with coffee in hand.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the Japanese gardens at Hakone Estate and actually slow down for an hour. The bamboo grove, koi ponds, and terraced hillside feel completely removed from everything.
cards.why: On a clear spring morning with mild temps this place is as good as it gets in the South Bay.

cards.name: Lou's Coffee Shop
cards.address: 15691 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Lou's Coffee Shop in Los Gatos is a classic diner lunch stop. Grab a booth, order something hearty, and take your time.
cards.why: Good diner food after a morning of walking is exactly what you want.

cards.name: CineLux Los Gatos Theatre
cards.address: 43 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Catch an afternoon matinee at CineLux Los Gatos Theatre on North Santa Cruz Ave. Old-school neighborhood theater, good vibe for a lazy Sunday afternoon.
cards.why: A movie in the middle of the day feels like a small luxury and this theater actually has character.

cards.name: Trattoria 360
cards.address: 360 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Trattoria 360 on Campbell Ave is the move for dinner. Italian comfort food, good wine list, solid atmosphere for a Sunday evening out.
cards.why: It's the kind of place that actually feels like a treat without being over the top about it.

cards.name: Saratoga Meat & Fish
cards.address: 14320 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd #5, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Saratoga Meat and Fish before they close at 5. Browse the case, pick up something good for the week, or just see what they've got.
cards.why: It's one of the best specialty butchers in the area and worth a quick stop before dinner.

cards.name: GK Pastry
cards.address: 809 Cuesta Dr, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at GK Pastry with fresh-baked pastries and a coffee — it's the kind of spot where you linger a little longer than planned.
cards.why: Hard to beat a 7 AM bakery on a crisp Sunday morning.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Fremont Older Open Space while the day is still cool. The upper loop gives you sweeping foothill views and it stays surprisingly uncrowded on weekends.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a morning hike before the day heats up.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by SaiGon BreadFast for a banh mi or a Vietnamese breakfast bowl. It's a great low-key lunch stop with solid flavors and nothing fussy about it.
cards.why: You've earned a proper meal after that hike.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head into the Triton Museum of Art for a couple hours. Walk the sculpture garden first, then browse the contemporary galleries inside. It's free and genuinely world-class for a South Bay museum.
cards.why: Free art on a Sunday afternoon is always the right call.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by SaiGon BreadFast for a banh mi or a Vietnamese breakfast bowl — solid flavors, nothing fussy.
cards.why: You've earned a proper meal after that hike.

cards.name: OMESCAPE | Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1135 E Arques Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at OMESCAPE in Sunnyvale for an escape room session. These rooms are designed for adults who actually want a challenge, not a walk-through.
cards.why: Great way to flip the energy mid-afternoon.

cards.name: Sushi Boat
cards.address: 1026 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for lunch and order a few rounds of whatever's floating past on the boat. The novelty never really gets old.
cards.why: One of the best-loved spots in Sunnyvale for a reason.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the trail at a leisurely pace and enjoy the 74-degree afternoon. It's flat and easy, so you can just cruise and decompress after lunch.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a casual afternoon walk along the creek.

cards.name: Middle Grade Book Club: Space for Saffron with author Rie Neal
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Head to Los Altos Library for this free author talk with Rie Neal. Even if you haven't read the book, author events like this are a fun, low-key way to spend an hour.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and something a little different for a Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: Puranpoli
cards.address: 3074b Scott Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Puranpoli for some solid Indian food to close out the night. Try the house specialties and take your time with it.
cards.why: A great low-key dinner spot to wind down the evening.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 650 Castro St #130, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a coffee and something to eat at 1 Oz Coffee on Castro St. Good spot to ease into the day before things get busy.
cards.why: Solid coffee and a chill vibe to kick things off right.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head into the Computer History Museum and spend a couple hours genuinely geeking out. The artifacts and exhibits are wild even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: The history here is actually fascinating and it's a great way to spend a sunny but not-quite-warm-enough-for-all-outdoor morning.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the Mountain View Farmers Market and see what's good. Pick up some fresh produce, snack on whatever looks interesting, and just enjoy the morning crowd.
cards.why: Perfect Sunday morning energy and it's right there on Castro.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk it off at Shoreline Park. It'll be close to the day's high of 74 by now, so stroll along the water, watch the windsurfers, or just sit and enjoy the bay.
cards.why: 74 degrees and sunny in the afternoon is pretty much ideal for this spot.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into FireWorks Ceramics and browse or try your hand at something creative. It's a fun way to spend an hour without any pressure.
cards.why: Good way to cap the afternoon with something hands-on and a little different.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind the day down with a massage at Star Thai Massage. Book ahead if you can and just let someone else do the work for once.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, this is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Goodthing coffee
cards.address: 532 Ramona St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a good coffee at Goodthing — it's a cozy spot on Ramona to ease into the day before things pick up.
cards.why: A solid cup before you get moving is always worth it.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits cover everything from early mainframes to the internet age and it's genuinely fascinating even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: This is one of the most underrated museums in the Bay Area.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Walk the California Ave Farmers Market and browse the vendors — fresh produce, baked goods, flowers, the works. Great way to spend a Sunday morning.
cards.why: Perfect weather to wander an outdoor market with a coffee still in hand.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus — walk the Rodin sculpture garden outside, then take your time through the galleries inside.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful campus on a Sunday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Oklava Cafe
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Oklava Cafe on University Ave for lunch — Mediterranean-inspired food, great vibe, and solid people-watching from the street.
cards.why: You need lunch and this place punches above its price point.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 250 University Ave STE 110, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a scoop at Salt and Straw on University Ave before you figure out dinner plans — they do inventive seasonal flavors and the line moves fast.
cards.why: A 71-degree afternoon calls for ice cream, full stop.

cards.name: Hiroshi
cards.address: 328 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Hiroshi in Los Altos for an upscale Japanese dinner — this is a splurge-worthy meal with serious omakase-style options and a calm, intimate setting.
cards.why: It's Sunday, you've had a full day, and a great dinner is the right way to close it out.

cards.name: Mikiya Wagyu Shabu House | Santa Clara
cards.address: 3590 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Wagyu shabu-shabu in Santa Clara. You cook the thin-sliced beef yourself in a hot broth at the table, and the quality of the meat makes it worth every minute.
cards.why: A proper dinner that feels like an occasion without being overly formal about it.

cards.name: Fremont Hills Country Club - Los Altos Hills
cards.address: 12889 Viscaino Pl, Los Altos Hills, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a round at Fremont Hills — the course sits up in the Los Altos Hills with views that make the whole thing feel worth it even before you tee off.
cards.why: Perfect morning temperature for golf before it warms up.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a coffee and something to eat at Kenz Coffee Bar on University Ave — good spot to decompress after the hike before the afternoon kicks off.
cards.why: You've earned a proper coffee at this point.

cards.name: Rodin Sculpture Garden
cards.address: Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Rodin Sculpture Garden on the Stanford campus — it's an outdoor collection of bronze sculptures and genuinely one of the more impressive free things in the area.
cards.why: Outdoor, free, and takes maybe an hour — easy afternoon addition.

cards.name: Vin Vino Wine
cards.address: 437 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Vin Vino on California Ave is a tiny wine bar with a tight, well-chosen list. Get a glass and settle in — it's the kind of place you stay longer than you planned.
cards.why: Sunday afternoon wine is a completely valid life choice.

cards.name: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio
cards.address: 632 Fremont Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Halo Pottery in Los Altos for an open studio session — Hila's space is relaxed and you don't need to be good at pottery for it to be a good time.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun thing to do on a Sunday and way more interesting than another walk.

cards.name: CUANYUE MALATANG (Sunnyvale)
cards.address: 150 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Cuanyue Malatang in Sunnyvale — build your own bowl of spicy broth with whatever proteins and veggies you want, it's customizable and deeply satisfying.
cards.why: One of the best malatang spots in the South Bay and a great way to end the day.

cards.name: Dakao Sandwiches II
cards.address: 72 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a banh mi from Dakao Sandwiches II — fresh, cheap, and the kind of breakfast that actually fills you up.
cards.why: Hard to beat a $15-and-under morning when the sandwich is this good.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Alum Rock Park and walk the creek trails through the canyon. The morning cool keeps it comfortable and the foothills are green this time of year.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a morning hike before it warms up.

cards.name: Apni Royal Kitchen
cards.address: 579 S Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Apni Royal Kitchen for lunch and try whatever's hot and fresh off the steam tray — this place punches way above its price point.
cards.why: Seriously good food for under $15, no question.

cards.name: The Hot Spot Studios Milpitas / Bay Area
cards.address: 30 S Abbott Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Check out The Hot Spot Studios in Milpitas, a creative art studio space where you can walk through exhibits and see what local artists are up to this weekend.
cards.why: A low-key Sunday afternoon vibe that's actually interesting.

cards.name: Claw Meee
cards.address: 532 Barber Ln, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Kill some time at Claw Meee and see if you can actually win something — the machines are well-stocked and it's more fun than it has any right to be.
cards.why: Exactly the kind of dumb fun a Sunday afternoon needs.

cards.name: IBU Taproom & Bottle Shop
cards.address: 1422 Dempsey Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to IBU Taproom for dinner and a pint. Sit at the bar, pick something from the rotating taps, and order food to go with it.
cards.why: A craft beer spot with actual food is the right way to end a Sunday.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at EDscapade Games with an escape room. Book a room ahead if you can, and let the post-dinner adrenaline do the rest.
cards.why: Way more fun than sitting on a couch for the rest of the night.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at the Campbell Farmers Market — grab a coffee from one of the vendors, pick up some fresh fruit, and wander the stalls before the crowds show up.
cards.why: 56 degrees and sunny is perfect farmers market weather.

cards.name: La Traviata
cards.blurb: Head to La Traviata — one of Verdi's most beloved operas, full of drama, gorgeous singing, and a story that moves fast for opera.
cards.why: A Sunday afternoon opera is a genuinely good use of a mild spring day.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and build your own spread from a dozen vendors. Get a taco from one spot, a drink from another, and snag a patio seat if the sun is out.
cards.why: It's the easiest way to eat well downtown without committing to one cuisine.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to Japantown for dinner. Get a bowl of udon at Gombei, pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo, and browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St before calling it a night.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and the food is the real deal.

cards.name: Goodtime Bar
cards.address: 30 Fountain Alley #160, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Goodtime Bar on Fountain Alley. Grab a cocktail, find a spot, and decompress after the performance.
cards.why: It's a great low-key spot to transition from afternoon to evening.
